HTML:
The Team

The Health Information Team plays a central role in managing the lifecycle of health data across Tasmania’s public health system. From data collection and storage to analysis and reporting, the team ensures that accurate, timely, and high-quality information supports both the health of Tasmanians and the performance of health services.

Health data is a critical enabler of system-wide planning, funding, and reform. The work of the Health Information Team underpins key departmental functions, including service agreement monitoring, national reporting obligations, and strategic decision-making.

The Role

The Database Support Administrator works within the Health Information Team to provide support services regarding information contained on databases managed by the Unit. The role supports the Department of Health in fulfilling its reporting obligations to external bodies, senior management and operational areas, facilitating informed decisions based on sound and robust data collected.

You will need

  • Knowledge of, and experience in, the use of computerised information systems/databases, including the development and production of statistical reports, together with proven skills and experience in using Word and Excel and the ability to learn other computer software packages.
  • Demonstrated coordination and organisational skills whilst undertaking and handling multiple tasks on a day-to-day basis, together with the ability to exercise initiative, judgement and discretion.
  • Well-developed interpersonal and communication skills with the ability to negotiate and liaise with a wide range of internal and external clients and stakeholders.

Note: - Please refer to the Statement of Duties for all the selection criteria of this position to address in your application.

Why Tasmania

The Department of Health Tasmania oversees various hospitals across the state, including the Royal Hobart Hospital, Launceston General Hospital, Northwest Regional Hospital, and Mersey Community Hospital, as well as district hospitals and ambulance services. Additionally, it provides community health, mental health, and primary healthcare services to the Tasmanian community through more than 300 sites.

Tasmania, Australia's only island state, offers unparalleled natural beauty and a luxurious lifestyle. Pursuing your health career in Tasmania’s public health system means building strong connections with colleagues and patients. It also means embracing a lifestyle where you can enjoy extraordinary experiences outside of work while making a significant impact on the community during work.

Details of appointment

Permanent full time, day worker position, working 76 hours per fortnight, commencing as soon as possible.

  • Notwithstanding hours to be negotiated with the successful applicant.

Applicants should note that, for a period of twelve months from the date of publication, this selection process may be used to fill subsequent or similar full time, part time, and casual vacancies.

Salary

$76,849 to $82,996 per annum. Our Employer 12% superannuation contribution is on top of this amount.

  • Salary range is in accordance with Public Sector Unions Wages Agreement 2025.

Benefits available to eligible candidates

  • Professional development and accelerated pathways.
  • A range of leave entitlements, including study leave and Professional Development Support.
  • Fitness Passport – You, and your family can access 40+ fitness facilities across Tasmania for just $14.50 per week per person, or $28.05 for a family.

More information about our Employee Benefits can be found here

Eligibility

Applicants should note the following criteria are desirable:

  • Previous experience in a field associated with health analytics

Title: Business Analyst
Place: Newcastle Upon Tyne, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Location: Newcastle (5 days onsite or willingness to relocate)




About the Opportunity

If you have 2+ years of experience as a Business Analyst and are looking to take the next step in your career, this could be a great opportunity for you. As a Business Analyst, you’ll join a collaborative team where you’ll continue to develop your expertise through meaningful project work, ongoing mentoring, and structured support from an experienced manager.




This is a fantastic opportunity for someone analytical and curious who is looking to deepen their experience in business analysis within a supportive, growth-focused environment. We’re committed to helping you further build your skills so you can progress into more senior roles across analysis, product, or delivery




Key Responsibilities

Design and implement scalable, efficient data architectures aligned with business and application requirements
Define data models, schemas, storage solutions, and integration frameworks
Collaborate with engineering, product, and analytics teams to ensure data integrity, availability, and accessibility
Translate business requirements into logical and physical data models
Develop and maintain comprehensive documentation for data structures, pipelines, and processes
Identify, troubleshoot, and resolve data-related performance and quality issues
Support and guide team members on best practices in data architecture and engineering
Participate in architectural discussions and contribute to strategic decision-making




Required Skills & Experience

2–3 years of experience in data architecture, data engineering, or a related field
Strong proficiency in Python for data processing and automation
Advanced knowledge of SQL for data querying and transformation
Solid understanding of data modeling concepts (e.g., relational, dimensional models)
Experience with data storage technologies (e.g., data warehouses, lakes, cloud platforms)
Familiarity with data integration tools and ETL/ELT processes
Understanding of AI & Data Processes and data-driven system design




Preferred Skills

Exposure to AI & Data Strategy and modern data platforms
Knowledge of data governance, security, and access frameworks
Experience working in cloud environments (e.g., Azure, AWS, or GCP)
Familiarity with big data tools (e.g., Spark, Databricks)




Key Competencies

Problem-solving mindset with strong analytical skills
Ability to work collaboratively across multiple teams
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
Proactive approach to identifying and improving data processes




What You’ll Bring

A passion for building robust and scalable data systems
Ability to balance technical depth with business needs
A collaborative attitude and willingness to contribute to team success




Why Join Us?

Dedicated support: Ongoing mentoring and guidance from experienced professionals
Upskilling opportunities: Training and development to further strengthen your skillset
Career growth: Work on impactful projects and progress into more senior roles
Collaborative culture: A team environment that values knowledge sharing and continuous improvement
Competitive salary and benefits package




Apply Today!

If you’re ready to take the next step in your career and grow with the support of nology and a dedicated team, submit your application now. We look forward to hearing from you!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About The Role




We’re seeking a Data Analyst to support the delivery of trusted reporting and analytical solutions that improve data reliability and operational visibility across the business. This role will play a key part in establishing better processes, strengthening data compliance and delivering meaningful insights that support data driven decision making.




In this hands on position, you’ll work closely with operational stakeholders, Data Analysts and Coordinators to develop Power BI dashboards, automate data analysis and improve the way critical business data is captured and utilised. You’ll help drive the transition from reactive decision making to a more proactive, data driven approach while supporting KPI tracking, supply and demand monitoring and overall operational efficiency.




You Should Have




Strong experience in data analytics, business intelligence or data analysis, with proven capability developing Power BI dashboards and reporting solutions
Strong experience integrating and working with Snowflake
Experience working with SAP and connecting data across multiple systems
Strong understanding of data modelling and optimisation principles
Ability to identify gaps between systems and data sources while improving data visibility and reliability
Experience delivering automated reporting and analytical solutions
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills, with the ability to work effectively across operational and technical teams
Ability to work independently in a fast paced environment and hit the ground running
Approximately four years of commercial experience within data and analytics




Desirable




Exposure to AI integration technologies
Experience using ChatGPT, Codex or APIs within analytical environments
Experience delivering interactive reporting and dashboard automation
Experience working within environmental or operationally focused industries
Water industry or dewatering experience
Exposure to ArcGIS




Key Responsibilities




Work closely with operational stakeholders to support key business initiatives
Develop and maintain high quality Power BI dashboards
Improve data reliability, visibility and governance across multiple data sources
Automate reporting and data analysis processes
Support KPI tracking, supply and demand monitoring and utilisation reporting
Identify opportunities to improve processes and operational efficiency through data
Work closely with operational and technical teams to support proactive decision making
Contribute to broader reporting, analytics and data improvement initiatives across the business




The Benefits




Opportunity to work on a high impact project focused on improving operational reporting and data capability
Exposure to modern technologies including Power BI, Snowflake and AI driven analytical tools
A collaborative, project focused environment with strong operational exposure
Potential opportunity to contribute to future data and analytics initiatives
8:6 FIFO roster




If this sounds like you, we'd love to hear from you. Apply now or contact Kent Sin on 0468 084 658 or kent@versegroup.com.au.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Your new company




You will be joining a well-established and growing organisation that places technology and data at the centre of its operational strategy. The business has a strong reputation for innovation and continuous improvement, offering a collaborative environment where systems and process optimisation are actively encouraged.




Your new role




We are currently recruiting for a Data Analyst to support the IT Manager and wider business prepare for a large data migration project and to support the wider business to better utilise customer data to enable and support strategic business objectives. As the Data Analyst, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring the stability, performance and ongoing development of key business systems.




You will work closely with stakeholders across the business to deliver meaningful data insights. This will involve developing reports and dashboards using tools such as SQL, Excel, Power BI and Tableau, while also supporting data quality and governance standards.

The business is currently in the very early stages of migrating to Salesforce. This role will play a critical part in that project. Longer term, you will take ownership of driving process automation to improve operational efficiency and support business users to make data more accessible and meaningful. You will design and implement workflows, streamline manual processes and take ownership of SharePoint administration, including site structure and permissions. You will also play an active role in troubleshooting and maintaining workflow solutions.




What you'll need to succeed




To be successful in this role, you will need advanced Excel skills (Power Query, pivot tables and complex formulas), strong SQL skills and great communication - bridging the gap between IT/ Data and the wider business. Experience with Power BI and Tableau is advantageous but not essential. You will have a proven ability to analyse data and produce clear, insightful reporting.




What you'll get in return




Salary up to £50,000, 1 day a week working from (possibly 2 days from home), health cash back plan, Life Insurance (2x salary), free food and catered lunches on-site, free on-site parking with electrical charge points. Full benefits package to be confirmed shortly.

In return, you will have the opportunity to work across a broad technology landscape in a role that offers real variety and impact. You will be part of a supportive and forward-thinking team, with opportunities to develop your technical skills and contribute to meaningful business improvements.




What you need to do now... If you are interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV.

Title: Market Insights Analyst
Description:
Join our Future Students & Marketing team.
Full-time, ongoing position at our Hawthorn campus.
$107,439 salary + 17% super.


About The Role

Reporting to the Strategic Insights Lead, the Market Insights Analyst plays a key role in delivering high-quality reporting, analysis and insights to inform evidence-based decision-making across Future Students and Marketing.

With a primary focus on the Australian education sector, the role develops, maintains, and enhances reporting and analytics products, including dashboards and recurring performance reports. It ensures data is accurate, well-governed and presented in a clear, consistent, and actionable format to support a range of stakeholders.

Working collaboratively with teams across Future Students and Marketing, as well as other University partners, the Market Insights Analyst translates business questions relating to market trends and the student recruitment pipeline into analytical requirements. The role is responsible for sourcing, preparing, and analysing data, and providing meaningful insights and recommendations to support strategic and operational objectives.

The position also contributes to the continuous improvement of reporting processes, documentation, and data governance practices, helping to strengthen self-service analytics capability while ensuring compliance with privacy, information management, and data governance requirements.

This is a full-time, ongoing position based at our Hawthorn campus.

About You

To be suitable for this role you will need to have experience in the below key accountabilities:


Demonstrated experience delivering high-quality data analysis, reporting and insights within a complex, customer-focused environment, supporting informed decision-making and business outcomes.
Proven ability to analyse large and complex datasets and translate findings into meaningful reports, insights and recommendations. Experience applying statistical and analytical techniques such as regression analysis, trend forecasting and predictive modelling is highly desirable. Experience with Databricks is advantageous.
Demonstrated experience developing, maintaining, and enhancing interactive dashboards and reporting solutions using Power BI. Proficiency in SQL and Python is highly regarded.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to effectively communicate complex information and insights to a range of internal and external stakeholders.
Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively within a team environment while also exercising initiative and working independently to achieve objectives.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel, Word and related data management and reporting tools.
Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage competing priorities, maintain a high level of accuracy and attention to detail, and deliver quality outcomes within required timeframes.
Knowledge of the Australian higher education sector, including familiarity with student, labour market and regulatory datasets and frameworks such as VTAC, HESC, QILT, TEQSA and ABS, is highly desirable.


About Swinburne University Of Technology

Swinburne’s strategy draws upon our understanding of future challenges. We choose to build Swinburne as the prototype of a new and different university – one that is truly of Technology, of Innovation and of Entrepreneurship. We are committed to a differentiated university proposition in education and research.

Our Ad Astra strategy is the cornerstone of Swinburne’s bold ambition to lead globally in technology-driven education and research. This strategy positions us to create transformative solutions, empower learners, and partner with industry to thrive in an ever-changing world.

What We Offer You!


Work your way with flexible working arrangements, including hybrid options and generous parental leave to support a balanced lifestyle.
Exclusive staff discounts, including discounted Medibank health insurance, 50% off Swinburne courses and other partner offers.
Swinburne offers 17% super and salary packaging options, including car leasing and extra super contributions.


Want to learn more? Head over to our benefits page !

To Apply

Please submit your CV and cover letter addressing the Key Selection Criteria and your suitability for this position.

To review the Position Description and to apply, please scroll down to the bottom of the page.

If you are viewing this advert from an external site, please click ‘apply’ and you will be redirected to Swinburne’s Jobs website to access the Position Description at the bottom of the page.

Please Note: Appointment to this position is subject to passing a Working with Children Check.

If you are experiencing technical difficulties with your application, please contact the Talent Acquisition team at talentacquisition@swin.edu.au

Applications Close: Wednesday 1 st July.

Swinburne offers flexible working options contained in our leave and parenting/carer policies to support work-life balance.

Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

Swinburne has become a world-class university, driving social and economic impacts through science, technology, and innovation. As a dual-sector university, our vision is for people and technology working together to build a better world.

Central to our vision is our commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion. We pride ourselves on being an equal opportunity employer focused on attracting, retaining, and developing great talent. We work to remove barriers related to gender identity, culture, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability, and age.

We strongly encourage applicants from diverse Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. Our Moondani Toombadool Centre leads our Indigenous education and culture at Swinburne, guided by community wisdom and leadership.

We support applicants with disabilities, and reasonable adjustments can be requested at any stage of the recruitment process.

For reasonable adjustment requests - including accessible formats of the position description, application form, or other documents, please contact talentacquisition@swin.edu.au or call +61 3 9214 3550 . Please note: this phone number is for disability and reasonable adjustment enquiries only. General enquiries about the role can also be emailed to talentacquisition@swin.edu.au .

Victoria’s Commitment to Action: Improving international student employment outcomes.

As a signatory to Victoria’s Commitment to Action , Swinburne seeks to remove barriers to international graduate employment. We welcome and encourage applications from international graduates.

Title: Analyst - Product Analytics
Description:
Who is Quantium?

Quantium is a world leader in data science and artificial intelligence. Established in Australia in 2002, Quantium is a global team of more than 1,200 people across 14 locations with a unique blend of capabilities across product and consulting services to help businesses unlock value from data and analytics. Quantium partners with the world's largest corporations to forge a better, more intelligent world.

Who is Quantium?

Quantium is a world leader in data science and artificial intelligence. Established in Australia in 2002, Quantium is a global team of more than 1,200 people across 14 locations with a unique blend of capabilities across product and consulting services to help businesses unlock value from data and analytics. Quantium partners with the world’s largest corporations to forge a better, more intelligent world.

We’re ALL in on AI — transforming ourselves into an AI-native organisation while helping our clients do the same. With 23 years of domain expertise, proprietary data partnerships, and industry-leading AI adoption (90% weekly active usage).

The Product Analytics team has embraced AI-native ways of working across our analytical workflows, and we’re now moving from adoption to transformation — systematically advancing our analytical assets through an agentic maturity framework we designed ourselves.

Our Team Operates Across Three Domains


Retail Products — Building and operating Quantium’s global flagship analytics products including Q.Checkout and Q.Scan
AI Client — Embedding with clients to deliver transformational AI programmes, bringing Quantium’s analytics and AI capability directly into partner organisations
AI Enablement — Rethinking how we operate as an analytics function, driving the tools, frameworks, and practices that make AI-native delivery the default


As an Analyst, you’ll be a collaborative technical contributor working within cross-functional teams alongside Product Leads, Delivery Managers, and engineers across Australia, India, the UK, and North America. You’ll build your foundations in data science, Python, and SQL while learning to combine them with AI tools that are central to how the team operates. This isn’t a team where AI is a side interest — it’s how we work.

How You’ll Create Impact


Build analytical solutions using AI-native workflows, learning to move from simple AI assistance through to more sophisticated agentic approaches
Contribute to progressing analytical assets and workflows along the team’s agentic maturity framework, with guidance from senior team members
Develop the skill of critically evaluating AI-generated outputs — learning when to trust, refine, or override
Use AI tools daily to enhance your analytical workflows and support team productivity
Work with data extraction, cleaning, and quality assessment across datasets using cloud platforms (GCP, BigQuery) and modern data tools
Apply data science methods including feature engineering, model development, and testing with attention to quality
Create specifications for analytics work that engineering teams can implement
Develop approaches that can be scaled and reused across different business challenges
Contribute to product development, day-to-day operations, and continuous improvement of team processes and methods


The Superpowers You’ll Be Bringing To The Team

Technical foundation: Solid Python and SQL skills with an understanding of data science fundamentals — data preparation, modelling approaches, and validation — and a willingness to keep developing your craft.

AI curiosity: Experience using AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, or similar) in your work or studies — or genuine enthusiasm and a clear willingness to learn. You’re open to working in a team where AI-native methods are the default.

Analytical thinking: A systematic approach to problem-solving with the ability to understand business needs and translate them into technical approaches — and to make the complex simple.

Collaborative spirit: Good communication skills for working with diverse teams across different locations and time zones, with the self-awareness to work independently while knowing when to ask for support.

Required Experience And Capabilities


1–5 years of experience in data science, analytics, or similar technical roles
Solid experience with Python and SQL for data analysis and manipulation
Experience working with Google Cloud Platform (GCP) or similar cloud technologies
Understanding of data science fundamentals: data preparation, modelling approaches, and validation methods
Familiarity with version control and documentation practices
Experience using AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, or similar) in your work or studies — or genuine enthusiasm and a clear willingness to learn
Strong analytical thinking and a systematic approach to problem-solving
Good collaboration skills for working with diverse teams across different locations and cultures
Degree in a quantitative field such as engineering, mathematics, statistics, computer science, physics, or a related discipline — or equivalent demonstrated capability


Highly beneficial


Experience building or experimenting with AI-assisted workflows or prompt engineering


What Could Your Quantium Experience Look Like?

Working at Quantium will allow you to challenge your imagination. You will get to solve complex problems using rigour, precision and by asking great questions — but it also means you can think big, outside the box and push your problem-solving skills to the max.

By Joining The Quantium Team, You’ll Get To

Forge your path: So many of our team have moved around different teams or offices. You’ll be in the driver’s seat, and we empower you to make your career your own.

Find your kind: Embrace diversity and connect with your tribe (think foodies, dog lovers, readers, or runners).

Make an impact: Leave your mark. Your contributions resonate, regardless of your role or rank.

Benefits

On top of the Quantium Experience, you will enjoy a range of great benefits that go beyond the ordinary. Some of these include:

Flexible work arrangements: Achieve work-life balance at your own pace with hybrid and flexible work arrangements.

Global mobility: As we expand globally, there are lots of exciting opportunities to work abroad with Quantium.

Remote working: Embrace the opportunity to work outside of your assigned home location for up to 2 months every year.

Apply now and let’s forge a better, more intelligent world together!

Quantium’s recruitment process involves the collection and use of personal information, see our Candidate Data Collection Notice for more information. This provides information on how we collect, use & store your personal information, including potential disclosure to our majority shareholder, Woolworths Group Limited.

Title: Data Analyst - Tenant Services
Place: Newcastle Upon Tyne, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Our vision is to give everyone the belief they can make their move. We aim to make moving simpler, by giving everyone the best place to turn to and return to for access to the tools, expertise, trust, and belief to make it happen.

We’re home to the UK’s largest choice of properties and are the go-to destination for millions of people planning their next move, reading the latest industry news, or just browsing what’s on the market.

Data Analyst - Tenant Services

Reporting to: Head of Referencing

Location: Fully remote (or with the option to go in to one of our office locations twice a week - Milton Keynes, London, Newcastle)

As the UK’s favourite property platform, Rightmove aims to give everyone the belief that they can make their move. To help us achieve this, we’re looking for an Data Analyst to join our team.

Are you a data-driven problem solver with a passion for optimising business operations? Join Rightmove’s Rental Services team as our Rental Services Data Analyst, where you’ll play a crucial role in driving efficiency, forecasting resources, and enhancing performance through data insights. Working cross-functionally, you’ll help shape operational strategies that align with our wider business goals, ensuring we continue to lead the market in rental services. If you’re eager to make a real impact in a fast-paced environment, we’d love to hear from you! 

What You’ll Be Doing


Supporting operational planning and resource forecasting across all of Rental Services.
Driving process improvements and efficiency initiatives to enhance service delivery across all areas.
Analysing performance data to identify opportunities and mitigate risks.
Monitoring KPIs and generating insights through effective data management.
Collaborating with cross-functional teams to optimise business operations.
Manage projects from start to finish, working across the operation with key stakeholders.
Aligning operational improvements with Rightmove’s strategy and objectives.
Ensure business, consumer and customer needs are prioritized in decision making.


Who You Are


A commercially minded analyst who identifies opportunities to improve revenue and performance, with accountability for end‑to‑end delivery.
Highly proficient in Excel and data analysis. 
Demonstrates advanced proficiency in SQL.
Experience leading and driving forward projects and final outcomes
Competent in working directly with senior leadership
A strategic thinker with strong problem-solving skills. 
A confident project manager, seeing through change and transformation. 
Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities. 
Confident in facilitating discussions and driving consensus within teams. 
Detail-oriented with a proactive approach to identifying efficiencies. 
Charismatic leadership, confident in managing projects and seeing through change and transformation. 


About Rightmove

Our vision is to give everyone the belief that they can make their move. We aim to make moving simpler by giving everyone the best place to turn to and return to for access to the tools, expertise, trust and belief to make it happen.

We're home to the UK's largest choice of properties, and are the go-to destination for millions of people planning their next move, reading the latest industry news, or just browsing what's on the market.

Despite This Growth, We’ve Remained a Friendly, Supportive Place To Work, With Employee #1 Still Working Here! We’ve Done This By Placing The Rightmove Hows At The Heart Of Everything We Do. These Are The Essential Values That Reflect Our Culture, And Include


We create value…by delivering results and building trust with partners and consumers.
We think bigger…by acting with curiosity and setting bold aspirations.
We care deeply…by being real, having fun, and valuing diversity.
We move together…by being one team - internally collaborative, externally competitive.
We make a difference…by focusing on delivering measurable impact.


We believe in careers that open doors and help our team develop by providing an open and inclusive work environment, offering ongoing training opportunities, and supporting charity fundraising events. And with 88% of Rightmovers saying we’re a great place to work, we’re clearly doing something right!

If all of this has caught your eye, you may well be a Rightmover in the making......

People are the foundation of Rightmove - We’ll help you build a career on it.

What We Offer


Cash plan for dental, optical and physio treatments.
Private Medical Insurance, Pension and Life Insurance, Employee Assistance Plan.
27 days holiday plus two (paid) volunteering days a year to give back, and holiday buy schemes.
Contributory stakeholder pension.
Life assurance at 4x your basic salary to a spouse, family member or other nominated person in your life.
Competitive compensation package.
Paid leave for maternity, paternity, adoption & fertility.
Travel Loans, Bike to Work scheme, Rental Deposit Loan.
Charitable contributions through Payroll Giving and donation matching.
Access deals and discounts on things like travel, electronics, fashion, gym memberships, cinema discounts and more.
We offer hybrid working with a minimum of 2 days in the office. For our roles, such as Field or Home-based positions, different working arrangements apply - full details will be shared during the recruitment process.


As an Equal Opportunity Employer, Rightmove will never discriminate based on age, disability, sex, race, religion or belief, gender reassignment, marriage / civil partnership, pregnancy/maternity or sexual orientation.

At Rightmove, we believe that a diverse and inclusive workforce leads to better innovation, productivity, and overall success., We are committed to creating a welcoming and inclusive environment for all employees, regardless of their background or identity, to develop and promote a diverse culture that reflects the communities we serve.

By applying, you confirm that you are aged at least 18 or over and that you’ve read and understood our Privacy Policy, which explains how we handle and protect your personal information during the recruitment process.

Title: Pricing Analyst
Description:
Data-Driven Strategy. Real-World Growth. Shape the Future of Pricing at ACM Parts.

ACM Parts, a proud part of the AMA Group, is Australia’s largest provider of alternative and specialty parts to the automotive repair industry, keeping vehicles on the road. We are reimagining the way vehicles can be repaired by providing a choice of options that suit their needs. We offer a comprehensive range of Reclaimed, Genuine, and Aftermarket parts.

The Opportunity

Reporting to Commercial Finance Manager, the Pricing Analyst will be the primary custodian of ACM’s margin optimisation strategy. This is a new role created to transition active day-to-day pricing functions from executive management into a dedicated analytical person.

You will not just crunch the numbers; you will turn data into actionable commercial strategies that directly impact profitability, balance supplier lead times against market demand, and manage SKU-level pricing across a high-volume product portfolio.

Key Responsibilities


Margin & Revenue Optimisation: Design, execute, and monitor pricing frameworks that protect gross margins while maintaining high market competitiveness.
SKU-Level Portfolio Analysis: Take ownership of extensive SKU databases to evaluate product performance, life-cycle pricing, and tiered volume discount structures.
Supply Chain & Cost Alignment: Collaborate closely with Procurement and Supply Chain teams to dynamically adjust pricing models in response to real-time variables like ocean freight costs, shifting supplier lead times, and inventory holding costs.
Competitor & Market Intelligence: Establish and maintain a continuous benchmarking matrix to analyse competitor pricing, local market elasticity, and industry trends.
Commercial Reporting: Build and manage automated pricing models and dashboards (using Power BI/Excel) to present margin performance, leakage, and profitability forecasts to executive leadership.



What We Are Looking For


Experience: 2–4 years of experience as a Pricing Analyst, Commercial Analyst, or Data Analyst—ideally within an automotive, industrial parts, manufacturing, or complex high-SKU distribution environment.
Technical Proficiency: Advanced expertise in Microsoft Excel (complex financial modeling, lookups, arrays) and strong experience with data visualisation tools (Power BI). Knowledge of SQL or ERP systems is highly advantageous.
Commercial Mindset: An understanding that great pricing is a balance of margin preservation and product availability. You look beyond basic "cost-plus" models to identify "Total Value Optimisation."
Communication Skills: The ability to translate highly dense, complex datasets into clear, concise commercial recommendations for non-technical stakeholders and executive managers.
Education: A Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Statistics, Supply Chain, or a related quantitative field.



Benefits


Reward & recognition
Career development and training
Referral bonus for recommending a friend to work with us
Discounts on parts and tools
Uniforms
24/7 professional counselling for Team members and their immediate families



We champion our Team, because it’s our people that set us apart and drive our business' success. We focus on individual strengths and value differences to bring the best out of people, enhancing their skills and capabilities to build fulfilling careers.

AMA Group is an equal opportunity employer. We are dedicated to finding the best fit for every role. All qualified applications will be considered regardless of gender identity or expression, age, religion, national origin, disability, or sexual orientation.

No matter who you are, if you have the drive and talent to progress, when you’re part of our Team, you’re going places - APPLY NOW.

Title: Analyst - Mounties Group Mount Pritchard, NSW
Place: Mount Pritchard, New South Wales, Australia
Description:
About Us

Mounties Group Values are Family, Honesty, Care, Innovation, Fun, Outcome Driven and Loyalty. We are a Mutuality which means our organisation is owned by its members and we exist to improve the lives of our members.

Mounties Group have over 280,000 members that use our services across a broad range of industry sectors including: Health & Fitness, Retail, Medical, Hospitality, Homecare and Allied Health.

We employ over 1,000 people and are rapidly growing, particularly in the Health and Wellbeing segment of our business. Our Head office is located in Mount Pritchard and supports businesses nationally.

About The Role

We are seeking a capable and driven Analyst to join our team on a full-time basis at Mounties, in Mount Pritchard.

Reporting to the Business Development Manager, the successful candidate will play a critical role in transforming complex data into actionable insights that enhance product performance and support strategic decision making.

More specifically, your duties will include but are not limited to:


Analyse gaming product performance, player behaviour, and key metrics to identify trends, opportunities, and risks
Partner with stakeholders to gather and turn business and data requirements into meaningful analytical outputs
Develop and maintain robust dashboards and automated reporting using Power BI
Perform complex data extraction, validation, and querying using MySQL
Apply advanced analytics and AI techniques for forecasting, segmentation, and trend analysis
Translate data insights into clear, actionable recommendations for both technical and non-technical audiences
Continuously improve data processes, reporting frameworks, and analytical methodologies


About You

To be successful in securing this role, you will ideally possess the following skills and experiences:


Extensive understanding of gaming products, performance metrics, player behaviour, and regulatory considerations
Strong analytical capability with experience working on large, complex datasets
Proven ability to gather, analyse, and translate business and data requirements into actionable insights
Proficiency in MySQL for data extraction, validation, and complex querying
Advanced Power BI skills, including data modelling, DAX measures, dashboard design, and automated reporting
Experience applying AI or advanced analytics for forecasting, segmentation, trend analysis, or process optimisation
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to present insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Strong attention to detail, problem-solving ability, and a continuous improvement mindset


WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU?


A supportive and inclusive team & culture
Free on-site parking
Discounted memberships to our fitness & swim centre
Ongoing training and development opportunities
Show more

  • Develop and maintain robust dashboards and automated reporting using Power BI
  • Perform complex data extraction, validation, and querying using MySQL
  • Apply advanced analytics and AI techniques for forecasting, segmentation, and trend analysis
  • Translate data insights into clear, actionable recommendations for both technical and non-technical audiences
  • Continuously improve data processes, reporting frameworks, and analytical methodologies

About You

To be successful in securing this role, you will ideally possess the following skills and experiences:

  • Extensive understanding of gaming products, performance metrics, player behaviour, and regulatory considerations
  • Strong analytical capability with experience working on large, complex datasets
  • Proven ability to gather, analyse, and translate business and data requirements into actionable insights
  • Proficiency in MySQL for data extraction, validation, and complex querying
  • Advanced Power BI skills, including data modelling, DAX measures, dashboard design, and automated reporting
  • Experience applying AI or advanced analytics for forecasting, segmentation, trend analysis, or process optimisation
  • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to present insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
  • Strong attention to detail, problem-solving ability, and a continuous improvement mindset

WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU?

  • A supportive and inclusive team & culture
  • Free on-site parking
  • Discounted memberships to our fitness & swim centre
  • Ongoing training and development opportunities

Title: Reporting Analyst
Description:
We are seeking a talented individual to join our Risk and Control Advisory team at Mercer Super. This role will be based in Sydney or Melbourne. This is a hybrid role that has a requirement of working at least three days a week in the office. If you’re an ambitious professional with a passion for precision and a keen eye for detail, this Reporting Analyst role offers a thrilling opportunity to dive deep into risk data analysis and reporting. The Reporting Analyst will support data analysis and reporting activities related to risk management and compliance, working closely with the Data and Reporting Lead. You will play a key role in establishing and standardising operational risk data to enhance data-driven decision-making and improve risk data governance across business units.

We will count on you to:


Support the establishment, capture, monitoring, and reporting of operational risk data to aid analytics and business decision-making
Collaborate closely with the Data and Reporting Lead to launch innovative forums and projects that elevate risk data governance and reporting standards across the organisation
Assist in creating and automating reports and dashboards by extracting, validating and consolidating data from multiple sources, performing trend and variance analyses, and presenting concise, actionable insights to stakeholders
Ensure accurate coordination and standardisation of data inputs across business units for comprehensive internal risk reporting
Leverage data integration from multiple sources to inform risk management strategies and enhance organisational resilience
Assist in performing proactive trend analysis to identify key risk hotspots and rolling out training to stakeholders and contributors on data processes, tools, and reporting to enhance their understanding and effective use of data in risk management
Maintain clear documentation for data sources, transformations, and reporting logic
Manage multiple priorities with a strong delivery focus while working independently and under direction


What you need to have:


Advanced Excel skills, including Power Query to manipulate and analyse complex datasets
Strong SQL experience for data extraction and transformation
Proficiency in Power BI, including data modelling, DAX, dashboards, and visualisations
Advanced PowerPoint skills for leadership and board-level reporting
Experience with Power Automate to streamline data refreshes, reporting workflows, and approvals


What makes you stand out:


Experience with GRC systems such as ServiceNow and SharePoint
Familiarity with Databricks or large-scale data platforms, plus a genuine interest in building AI bots and automating processes
High attention to detail and a strong delivery mindset with a collaborative and proactive approach to problem-solving
Experience in risk management, compliance, data or business analysis within the superannuation or broader financial services industries


Willingness to continuously improve, learn, and adapt to new technologies and tools in the data management space

Why join our team:


We help you be your best through professional development opportunities, interesting work and supportive leaders
We foster a vibrant and inclusive culture where you can work with talented colleagues to create new solutions and have impact for colleagues, clients and communities
Our scale enables us to provide a range of career opportunities, as well as benefits and rewards to enhance your well-being


Applications can be considered from candidates who have the appropriate approval to work in Australia. Successful applicants will be required to complete a criminal & bankruptcy check prior to commencement of employment.

Mercer is a business of Marsh (NYSE: MRSH), a global leader in risk, reinsurance and capital, people and investments, and management consulting, advising clients in 130 countries. With annual revenue of over $27 billion and more than 95,000 colleagues, Marsh helps build the confidence to thrive through the power of perspective. For more information about Mercer, visit mercer.com, or follow us on LinkedIn and X.

Marsh is committed to creating a diverse, inclusive and flexible work environment. We aim to attract and retain the best people and embrace diversity of age, background, disability, ethnic origin, family duties, gender orientation or expression, marital status, nationality, parental status, personal or social status, political affiliation, race, religion and beliefs, sex/gender, sexual orientation or expression, skin color, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. Applications will only be considered from candidates who have the appropriate approval to work in Australia. Successful applicants will be required to complete a Criminal & Bankruptcy check prior to commencing of employment.

Marsh is committed to hybrid work, which includes the flexibility of working remotely and the collaboration, connections and professional development benefits of working together in the office. All Marsh colleagues are expected to be in their local office or working onsite with clients at least three days per week. Office-based teams will identify at least one “anchor day” per week on which their full team will be together in person.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
We are seeking a Senior Data Analyst to provide critical analytical capability within our Product Remediation function. This role supports complex, high‑profile remediation activity through rigorous data analysis and insight, enabling informed decision‑making, fair customer outcomes, and strong regulatory compliance.

The team’s work is central to how the organisation identifies and resolves issues, manages risk, and demonstrates that remediation outcomes are accurate, complete and appropriately governed. Senior leaders rely on this capability to support regulator‑facing decisions and long‑term risk management.

The Opportunity

As a Senior Data Analyst, you will lead data analysis across significant remediation initiatives, working closely with project, product and business stakeholders to understand root causes, identify impacted customers and quantify remediation outcomes. You will work with large, complex datasets and ensure all outputs are robust, defensible and auditable.

You will also contribute to maintaining strong analytical standards across the team, including supporting and guiding less experienced analysts where required.

Key Responsibilities


Lead data analysis for major, complex and/or reportable remediation initiatives
Partner with Project Managers, Business Analysts and Product Owners to define data requirements and drivers
Design and deliver data solutions to identify impacted customers and remediation outcomes
Analyse complex datasets from multiple systems using SQL, Python and Excel
Provide clear, evidence‑based insights and recommendations to senior leaders and executives
Ensure analytical outputs meet governance, audit and regulatory standards


About You


Extensive experience in data analysis within a project or delivery environment
Tertiary qualification in IT, Engineering, Mathematics, Finance or a related discipline
Advanced proficiency in SQL and Python, including complex query design and optimisation
Strong experience with SQL Server / T‑SQL, Excel (including Power Query) and large, complex datasets
Demonstrated ability to translate technical analysis into clear, practical business insights
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage senior stakeholders


Experience in financial services or other regulated environments is highly regarded.

Find the better way

What gets us excited about working here? Helping people feel good about their money as one of Australia’s leading financial wellbeing organisations. From those starting out to those retiring. As your team, we’ll show up for you and together we will create financial wellbeing for every Australian. Because we know people who are confident about their financial future live better lives.

If this sounds like your kind of career, you sound like our kind of person. With us, you’ll do work that builds your technical know-how and challenges our entire industry to move forward. Along the way, you’ll be free to explore new ideas and technology, solve problems in a team, and independently to get great things done. Around here, we like to call it “putting our fingerprints on the future”.

People-inspired tech

Deep expertise combined with creativity can take on the trickiest of problems. Join our team of brilliant minds and mentors as we explore our way to innovation and apply technology in more human ways than ever.

To Apply

Read more here about why you should join our team.

Applicants will be required to provide evidence of their eligibility to work in Australia, and at a minimum be required to undertake police and basic credit checks as a condition of employment.

Please note that applications from agencies will not be considered at this time.

We acknowledge and celebrate the richness that individual differences bring to our team. If you need assistance or an adjustment during the application process, please reach out and let us know.

Title: Data & Automation Analyst
Description:
Data & Automation Analyst - ( 260000CT )

Description

Data & Automation Analyst

Stantec is a global leader in sustainable engineering, architecture, and environmental consulting. The diverse perspectives of our partners and interested parties drive us to think beyond what’s previously been done on critical issues like climate change, digital transformation, and future-proofing our cities and infrastructure.

We are designers, engineers, scientists, project managers, and strategic advisors. We innovate at the intersection of community, creativity, and client relationships to advance communities everywhere, so that together we can redefine what’s possible.

About The Role

Stantec’s Water team in New Zealand has an impressive portfolio of successful projects that benefit communities nationwide and beyond, with significant contributions to international work. Our water focus covers the management of drinking water, wastewater, stormwater, and groundwater as a collective resource. Dams and hydropower are a key focus, driving resilience and renewable energy solutions. Tackling our water challenges is crucial, and our diverse local network includes over 360 water specialists dedicated to innovation.

We are seeking a Data and Automation Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will support water services projects by applying data analysis and automation techniques to improve project outcomes and contribute to company objectives. Working alongside multidisciplinary teams, you will develop technical solutions, support project delivery, and ensure outputs align with client and Stantec expectations. This is an opportunity to apply and grow your technical skills while contributing to meaningful infrastructure projects.

Key Responsibilities


Provide technical support and expertise in automation, analytics, and data engineering to meet project requirements.
Contribute to the capability and knowledge sharing of the Data and Automation team within the NZ Digital Practice.
Build collaborative relationships and support a culture of knowledge sharing across the Digital Practice and relevant streams.
Support client engagement activities, contribute to identifying opportunities, and assist with business development initiatives aligned with Stantec business strategies.
Deliver robust data analytics and automated solutions on time and within project requirements and budgets.
Promote and apply automation solutions within Stantec NZ and APAC for internal and external clients.
Contribute technical input to project delivery across internal and external client projects.
Support the development, implementation, and ongoing improvement of digital products.


What We Are Looking For


Relevant tertiary qualification desirable (e.g. Engineering, Computer Science)
Technical expertise in data analytics, automation, and data engineering.
Experience with data and automation technologies and methodologies e.g. SQL, Python, R, Power Platform, Power BI, Tableau, FME
Experience with geospatial data desirable


What We Offer

At Stantec, we take your growth and fulfilment seriously. We’re proud to offer many supportive benefits, including:


Professional development and support
Flexible working arrangements, including purchasing up to an extra four weeks of leave and provision for school term contracts, part-time, and job sharing
Learning and talent development programmes, including global, online, and on-the-job opportunities
Professional memberships
Service recognition awards
Mentorship
Wellbeing and connection opportunities
Health insurance, income continuance insurance, and life insurance
Employee assistance program
Developing Professionals Group, a global network for those with under 10 years of experience
Office Social Club and events


We’ve been serving our clients and communities in Aotearoa New Zealand for over 125 years. With almost 900 professionals, our team is based in the main cities and throughout the regions. We collaborate across disciplines and sectors to bring buildings, energy, water, environmental, and transport projects to life.

Globally, Stantec unites approximately 34,000 employees working in over 450 locations across six continents. Our work from initial project concept and planning through design, construction, and commissioning begins at the intersection of community, creativity, and client relationships.

Please register your interest and apply now - applications close Friday 5th June 2026

Other Locations : New Zealand-Wellington-Wellington

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

A career at Hitachi Rail will help create a legacy. With operations in every corner of the world, our work goes to the cutting-edge of digital transformation and technology. From the multi-cultural strength of our global organisation to the sustainable and innovative ways we work to bring people together, there’s something for everyone to get stuck into. And that’s where you come in.

Job Description:

Hitachi Rail is looking for an enthusiastic self-motivated Data Analyst who thrives in a fast-paced environment. The successful candidate is comfortable performing a wide range of tasks from administrative to strategic. The position is based in Brisbane, Australia.

About the Position:

The Data Analyst in the Systems & Reporting team supports data-driven decision-making across the organization. The role focuses on developing and managing data dashboards, maintaining data quality, and guiding the adoption of new data tools and standards. The Data Analyst works closely with the Systems & Reporting Manager to improve data and information management practices and drive consistency in data handling.

The successful candidate will be a strong communicator who can simplify complex data and insights for senior stakeholders, enabling clear decision-making.

Key responsibilities:



Develop, maintain, and manage data dashboards to provide real-time insights for stakeholders.
Use data storytelling techniques to help stakeholders understand trends, patterns, and key messages, not just visualize data points.
Apply data visualization and graphic design principles to create clear, engaging dashboards and reports.
Support the collection, analysis, and synthesis of data to generate actionable insights.
Assist in developing and maintaining the Sequence Data and Information Framework.
Develop Power BI templates and standards to ensure consistency and quality across Sequence Alliance.
Guide and assist Sequence Alliance in the evaluation and adoption of emerging technologies and advanced data platforms, including Fabric IQ, data warehouse, data lake, and data lake-house.
Contribute to improving data and information management practices, including developing standards and best practices for data, information, and record handling and storage.
Assist in the development and maintenance of data applications and custom workflows.
Identify opportunities for improvement in data systems.
Actively support the use and integration of AI tools for data analysis and data systems, including assisting with the rollout of AI functions.
Assist in the development of data and information policies, procedures, and standards.
Support the data collection and analytics aspects of the KRA-KPI program and future operational KPI programs.
Provide training and guidance on data collection and best practices to project teams and individuals.
Collaborate on strategic initiatives that require data analysis support, including projects involving external stakeholders.
Continuously seek opportunities to enhance data handling processes, tools, and practices.



Skills & Experience:



Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., data analytics, information management, business, or related field).
Certification in data analysis or business intelligence (e.g., CBIP or similar) is a plus.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with expertise in data analysis, metrics development, and data visualization.
Proficiency in Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI), MS Dataverse, Fabric IQ and related concepts.
Skills and expertise in data visualization and graphic design principles as they relate to dashboards and reports.
Ability to tell stories from data and take viewers on a journey, not just visualize data points.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to support effective data communication with stakeholders.
Familiarity with industry standards, best practices, and regulations related to data management, governance, and security. Government data requirements and standards knowledge an advantage.



At Hitachi Rail, we know that a diverse business is a sustainable business. Working collaboratively with a range of internal networks, we’re continuously improving our policies and products. From accessible, sustainable design to achieving a better gender balance. We base these efforts on our core values: Harmony, Sincerity, and Pioneering spirit. And by aligning with these, we’re able to create a workplace where people not only feel comfortable but are given opportunities to thrive.

What we offer:



Be part of a Global Company that offers a great work life balance.
Many opportunities to progress your career with training and development available.
Salary Continuance Insurance



Thank you for your interest in Hitachi Rail. If your application is of interest, we will be in contact. Please do not hesitate to discover more about us and our latest jobs at https://www.hitachirail.com/careers.

At Hitachi Rail, there is a place for everyone. We welcome and value differences in background, age, gender, sexuality, family status, disability, race, nationality, ethnicity, religion, and world view. It is our commitment to create an inclusive environment - we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer.

We would be delighted if you would be one of our followers at https://www.linkedin.com/company/hitachirail

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI), MS Dataverse, Fabric IQ and related concepts.
  • Skills and expertise in data visualization and graphic design principles as they relate to dashboards and reports.
  • Ability to tell stories from data and take viewers on a journey, not just visualize data points.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to support effective data communication with stakeholders.
  • Familiarity with industry standards, best practices, and regulations related to data management, governance, and security. Government data requirements and standards knowledge an advantage.

And by aligning with these, we’re able to create a workplace where people not only feel comfortable but are given opportunities to thrive.

What we offer:


  • Be part of a Global Company that offers a great work life balance.
  • Many opportunities to progress your career with training and development available.
  • Salary Continuance Insurance


Thank you for your interest in Hitachi Rail. If your application is of interest, we will be in contact. Please do not hesitate to discover more about us and our latest jobs at https://www.hitachirail.com/careers.

We welcome and value differences in background, age, gender, sexuality, family status, disability, race, nationality, ethnicity, religion, and world view. It is our commitment to create an inclusive environment - we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer.

We would be delighted if you would be one of our followers at https://www.linkedin.com/company/hitachirail

Title: Data Visualisation and Automation Analyst
Description:
Permanent
Up to £50,000 + Pension + PMI + ShareSave + 6.6 weeks holiday + Hybrid Working (1 day per week in the office)
Southampton, Store Support Office




We believe anyone can improve their home to make life better. From our Southampton Store Support office (SSO) we equip our stores, our people, and our whole business with everything it takes to help our millions of customers create a home they’ll love. Join us as a Data Visualisation and Automation Analyst and you’ll be a big part of this.

Working within the Data Visualisation and Automation Team, the Data Visualisation & Automation Analyst will work to produce high quality self-serve dashboards as well as our foundational data enablement products. They will interact with a varied group of stakeholders to drive up the understanding of customers across the organisation, helping to shape our future strategies, improve customer experience, deepen engagement, drive revenue, and improve business efficiencies across both B&Q and TradePoint.




What's the job?

Develop our suite of self-serve dashboards, helping the business to get closer to our customers, informing business strategy and decision making.
Develop our customer data ETL pipelines and data foundation for B&Q, working closely alongside and in collaboration with Group IT when applicable.
Curate and put in place the controls for our customer data lake, democratising our data in a controlled manner, ensuring our analysts and data scientists have access to reliable and consistent data for analysis.
Provide input to initiatives to help define the right data flow architecture that deliver tangible results quickly.
Be an active member of the wider data community across the group, engaging with the group data team and other banners across the group.
Support the growth of new channels, Services, Digital, MarketPlace and Retail Media with key data sets and self-serve reporting.
Build relationships with key stakeholders from across the business and translate their ambitions and goals into analytical challenges.
Bring external thinking to B&Q by maintaining an awareness of analytics usage in retail and other sectors and by keeping abreast of competitor technology and developments.




What we need:

You have experience in creating and delivering actionable self-serve solutions, using modern BI tools such: PowerBI, Tableau, Looker etc.
You’re proficient in SQL and have some experience using other languages such as Python or R.
You’ve had experience using cloud based analytical platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake, Google BigQuery and the like.
You’ve built and managed data workflows before using tools like Airflow and Databricks.
You are fully aware of the importance of Data Governance and good Data Quality and what best practice looks like.
You’ve used Jira/Confluence before for managing workloads and documentation.
You’re curious: about how things work and getting to the bottom of business problems and data nuances.
You understand and maximise the value of analysis and self-serve data products, and act with a commercial lens, prioritising the actions and usefulness of outputs.
You’re a skilled storyteller who can bring that value to others – you know numbers don’t always speak for themselves and have excellent visuals to support.




What's in it for me?

As well as a competitive salary, our benefits package includes:

Award-winning pension scheme
Bonus
ShareSave options
6.6 weeks holiday
Payroll giving
Employee Assistance Programme
Shopping discounts
Colleague wellbeing benefits
…and much more.




Equality and Inclusion

We welcome applications from all candidates; however, please note that we are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this role, and applicants must have the right to work in the UK.

We want to ensure that all employees, future employees, and applicants to all Kingfisher companies are treated equally regardless of age, gender, marital or civil partnership status, colour, ethnic or national origin, culture, religious belief, philosophical belief, political opinion, disability, gender identity, gender expression, or sexual orientation.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At Frasers Group we’re rethinking retail. Through digital innovation and unique store experiences, we’re serving our consumers with the world’s best sports, premium and luxury brands globally. As a leader in the industry, we’re elevating the retail experience for our consumers through our collection of established brands, including Sports Direct, FLANNELS, USC, Frasers, and GAME.



Why join us?



Our vision – we are building the worlds most admired and compelling brand ecosystem



Our purpose – we are elevating the lives of the many with access to the world’s best brands and experiences



At Frasers Group, we fear less and do more. Our people are forward thinkers who are driven to operate outside of their comfort zone to change the future of retail, embracing challenges along the way. The potential to elevate your career is massive, the experience unrivalled. To be able to make the most of it you need to live and breathe our principles:



Think without limits – Think, think fast, think fearlessly
Own it and back yourself - Own the basics, own your role and own the results
Be relevant - Relevant to our people, our partners and the planet

Are you ready to join the Fearless?





Job Description



This is a Data Analyst role where you will be working as part of the analytics team within the Estates function, focusing on developing, analysing & creating tools/models/reports used by the business. You must have an analytical mindset, strong communication skills & be able to adapt to fast paced, ever changing business environments.



Role Description:



Serve as the key analytical point of contact for one of the fastest-growing areas of the business, leading forecasting and delivering analysis to support continued growth.
Liaise with c-suite and senior leadership, fulfilling data and report requests and developing strategies.
Create and implement AI and automation projects, primarily in Airtable.
Leverage VBA to automate manual processes.
Assist in the maintenance and development of Power BI reports.
Produce ad-hoc reports and perform detailed analysis of property data using Excel.
Write and optimise database queries.
Identify trends and provide strategic recommendations.
Collaborate with both technical and non-technical stakeholders across the business.


Qualifications



Essential Skills:



Advanced Excel skills, including VBA and Power Query.
Experience in developing Power BI reports, including writing and optimising DAX measures.
Proficient in SQL, and experienced working with relational databases.
Understanding of financial concepts such as EBITDA, or an interest in developing financial knowledge is advantageous.
Familiarity with Airtable is advantageous.
Experience with programming languages such as Python or R is advantageous.
Background in commercial property, utilities or retail analytics is advantageous.
Able to hold your own in meetings and confident talking to senior stakeholders
Able to manage and prioritise workload.
Strong problem-solving ability & a commercial mindset
Able to communicate with a wide range of stakeholders with various levels of technical understanding.
Proactive, independent, self-starter who is ready to jump in and get things done


Additional Information



Along with your benefits package we also offer a wide range of perks for our colleagues:



Frasers Champion- Our employees are at the heart of our business and we ensure individuals are recognised every single month for their hard work. Frasers Champion is a peer nominated scheme where 8 winners will receive double their pay for a month where they have lived the Frasers Group values.



Retail Reconnect – In order to build the planets most admired and compelling brand ecosystem, all employees must understand our business, product and customers. Each financial year, head office employees will gain insights by spending one to two days in one of our stores or the warehouse. The goal is to learn how the work you do impacts our teams on the frontline, and to bring ideas back to the office which will improve how we work.



Fearless 1200 – Fearless 1200 is our way of recognising our growth. It’s bold, ambitious, and designed to reward colleagues across the business for living our values and delivering impact. Fearless 1200 links how we perform to how we're rewarded – check out the video link to find out more - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O0qmvJofMew



Employee Welfare



Frasers Fit – Our Everlast Gyms Team are on a mission to make our workforce the best, and fittest on the planet! We run free gym classes for employees as well as discounted memberships to our clubs. Frasers Fit is our wellbeing programme which aims to support and improve colleagues Physical, Financial & Mental wellbeing. The app is accessible for every employee and includes training, nutrition and lifestyle advice- all completely free.



Retail Trust – We know that its not just about physical health, mental wellness is equally important which is why all of our employees get free access and support from the Retail Trust charity. This includes a 24 hour wellbeing helpline, wellness hub, counselling and financial/legal support.



What’s next?



Our Recruitment Team will be reviewing applications and all candidates will receive a response, whether you are successful or unsuccessful. Shortlisted applicants may be asked to confirm a few key details before being booked in for a first stage interview with the Recruiter - this will be behaviourally focussed and centred around how you align with our Culture and Values. If successful we anticipate one or two further interview stages with the Hiring Manager/wider team which will be more technically focussed and could include a presentation/task so we can see your skills in action.

Title: Remote Analytics Specialist
Description:
At IKEA, our vision is to create a better everyday life for the many people. We believe that everything we do comes with the responsibility to create a positive impact on people, society and the planet. We are passionate about home furnishing, creating a positive life at home and sharing our tasty responsibly sourced food with the many people across the world.

We are guided by our cultures & values, creating a great place to work.
We stand for equality, diversity and inclusion.
We care about people & planet and promote health & wellbeing.
We nurture your development and provide career opportunities to unlock your potential.







Working at IKEA has its benefits and rewards:
• 5 weeks’ Paid Annual Leave, Paid Parental Leave, Family & Community Leave and much more
• 24/7 access to our Employee Assistance Program for health and wellbeing support
• 15% Co-worker discount
• Low-cost nutritious meals including complimentary drinks in our Co-worker restaurant
• Co-worker uniform provided
• Free Co-worker parking
• Bonus programme (where eligible)
• Benefits Membership (savings and discounts on everyday expenses, entertainment, retail and well-being offers across hundreds of retailers and service providers)
• Co-worker Loyalty programme (extra contribution to your superannuation fund where eligible)

Additional information:
• Position type: Permanent, full-time (hybrid)
• Internal job title: Remote Analytics Specialist
• Work location: Customer Service Centre (RCMP), Tempe NSW
• Working hours: 9:00am – 5:00pm Monday to Friday, (occasional after-hours to attend global meetings)




ABOUT THIS WORK AREA




As a Remote Analytics Specialist, you’ll turn complex data into clear insights that drive business decisions and improve customer outcomes. Your responsibilities will include, but are not limited to:
• Analyse operational and customer data to identify trends, performance drivers, and root causes.
• Develop and maintain dashboards and reports (primarily in Power BI) to track key metrics and business performance.
• Translate data into actionable insights that inform strategy and continuous improvement initiatives.
• Partner with stakeholders across Sales, Customer Resolutions, and Experience Design to influence decisions and prioritisation.
• Collaborate with global analytics teams to align on tools, frameworks, and best practices.
• Produce regular and ad hoc analysis to support local business needs and strategic initiatives.
• Communicate insights through clear storytelling, tailored to both technical and non-technical audiences.










As Remote Analytics Specialist you are or have:
• Experience in a Data Analyst, Business Analyst, or Insights Analyst role.
• Strong SQL skills (BigQuery or similar) with the ability to analyse large datasets.
• Hands-on experience with Power BI or similar visualisation tools.
• Advanced Excel skills and confidence working with complex data.
• Strong communication and data storytelling skills, including PowerPoint.
• The ability to translate data into clear, actionable recommendations.
• Experience working with stakeholders and influencing outcomes across different teams.
• A proactive, self-driven mindset with the ability to manage competing priorities.
• Prior experience of working in eCommerce, or customer operations/contact centres is highly regarded but not essential.




We need people like you




At IKEA, work is so much more than a job. Come join us!
