Title: Planning & Scheduling Data Analyst
Description:
Powered by Water, Driven by Purpose

South West Water keeps the South West flowing with safe, reliable drinking water and wastewater services across some of the UK’s most stunning landscapes.

We’re proud to be part of Pennon Group, a leader in the UK water sector, working towards a greener future. Our goals? As well as lowering our carbon footprint, we’re working with partners to plant 300,000 trees, restore peatlands and supporting farmers and landowners to improve water quality and wildlife.

Whether you’re starting out or seeking a new challenge, our scale and ambition create opportunities for you to shape your own career.

Ready to make a splash? Join our team today.

Join our Wastewater Services team as a Planning & Scheduling Data Analyst, where you’ll transform complex data into actionable insights that drive smarter decisions and improve service reliability.

You’ll bring together data from across operations, field activity, and customer interactions to identify risks, optimise resources, and enhance end‑to‑end performance. This is a key role in strengthening planning, improving customer experience, and boosting the efficiency and resilience of our wastewater services

What You'll Be Doing


Deliver integrated performance reports and dashboards that provide clear insight into planning, scheduling efficiency, operational flow, and customer impact.
Translate complex data into accessible, actionable intelligence for operational, planning, and leadership teams.
Analyse cross‑functional, operational, and field performance data to identify trends, constraints, systemic issues, and improvement opportunities.
Highlight emerging risks and performance bottlenecks across the wastewater service and customer journey
Support evidence‑based continuous improvement initiatives across Planning, Operations, and Customer teams.
Develop forecasting models and predictive analytics to support strategic planning, demand management, and resource optimisation.
Provide insight that supports proactive risk anticipation and scenario planning across operational and customer pathways.
Work collaboratively with Planning & Scheduling, Operations, Customer Services, and Project teams to ensure insights inform decision‑making at all levels.
Act as a trusted data partner, ensuring insight is aligned to operational realities and business priorities.
Improve data quality, structure, and consistency across systems, workflows, and reporting tools.
Enable a reliable, end‑to‑end data view of customer journeys, operational processes, and field performance.


What We Are Looking For


Proven experience in a data, performance, or planning analytics role within an operational or service delivery environment (e.g. utilities, infrastructure, transport, engineering).
Strong experience working with complex, multi-source datasets to generate clear and meaningful performance insights.
Ability to develop reports, dashboards, and management information that support planning, scheduling, and operational decision-making.
Experience analysing end-to-end service or customer journeys to identify inefficiencies, risks, and opportunities for improvement.
Knowledge of forecasting, trend analysis, or predictive techniques to support capacity planning and resource optimisation.
Proven ability to collaborate with operational and customer-facing teams to turn data into actionable recommendations.
Experience improving data quality, consistency, and governance across systems and reporting processes.


What's In It For You


Generous holiday allowance with the ability to buy and sell annual leave for added flexibility.
Competitive company pension scheme.
Training and development within the role.
Access to thousands of discounts and vouchers through Pennon Perks.
24/7 support with our Employee Assistance Programme.
Discretionary bonus.
Cycle to work scheme.
And more!


Closing Date: 1st July 2026

We may close this vacancy early if we receive a high volume of applications. We encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

Please note that the successful candidate will be subject to a mandatory DBS check as part of the onboarding process.

Be yourself, we like it that way. Together, we will build a culture of belonging, where inclusion is instinctive. Diversity is our strength and a reflection of our communities. We care, we value everyone, we celebrate uniqueness.

Our core values, which are essential to our success, are:

Be Rock Solid - Build trust and be trusted. Be the one we all look to and can depend on.

Be You - We want you to bring your best everyday. Be yourself and make your mark in your individual way.

Be the Future - Embrace change. Drive Progress. Own the challenge.

We look after the water and wastewater services for 1.8 million customers across Cornwall, Devon, the Isles of Scilly and parts of Dorset and Somerset.

From treatment and maintenance, to innovation and improvements, we're busy every day finding and fixing leaks, unblocking sewers and going bigger and further in our environmental work to be a company you can be proud of.

South West Water keeps the South West flowing with safe, reliable drinking water and wastewater services across some of the UK’s most stunning landscapes.

We’re proud to be part of Pennon Group, a leader in the UK water sector, working towards a greener future. Our goals? As well as lowering our carbon footprint, we’re working with partners to plant 300,000 trees, restore peatlands and supporting farmers and landowners to improve water quality and wildlife.

Whether you’re starting out or seeking a new challenge, our scale and ambition create opportunities for you to shape your own career.

Ready to make a splash? Join our team today.

Join our Wastewater Services team as a Planning & Scheduling Data Analyst, where you’ll transform complex data into actionable insights that drive smarter decisions and improve service reliability.

You’ll bring together data from across operations, field activity, and customer interactions to identify risks, optimise resources, and enhance end‑to‑end performance. This is a key role in strengthening planning, improving customer experience, and boosting the efficiency and resilience of our wastewater services

What You'll Be Doing

  • Deliver integrated performance reports and dashboards that provide clear insight into planning, scheduling efficiency, operational flow, and customer impact.
  • Translate complex data into accessible, actionable intelligence for operational, planning, and leadership teams.
  • Analyse cross‑functional, operational, and field performance data to identify trends, constraints, systemic issues, and improvement opportunities.
  • Highlight emerging risks and performance bottlenecks across the wastewater service and customer journey
  • Support evidence‑based continuous improvement initiatives across Planning, Operations, and Customer teams.
  • Develop forecasting models and predictive analytics to support strategic planning, demand management, and resource optimisation.
  • Provide insight that supports proactive risk anticipation and scenario planning across operational and customer pathways.
  • Work collaboratively with Planning & Scheduling, Operations, Customer Services, and Project teams to ensure insights inform decision‑making at all levels.
  • Act as a trusted data partner, ensuring insight is aligned to operational realities and business priorities.
  • Improve data quality, structure, and consistency across systems, workflows, and reporting tools.
  • Enable a reliable, end‑to‑end data view of customer journeys, operational processes, and field performance.

What We Are Looking For

  • Proven experience in a data, performance, or planning analytics role within an operational or service delivery environment (e.g. utilities, infrastructure, transport, engineering).
  • Strong experience working with complex, multi-source datasets to generate clear and meaningful performance insights.
  • Ability to develop reports, dashboards, and management information that support planning, scheduling, and operational decision-making.
  • Experience analysing end-to-end service or customer journeys to identify inefficiencies, risks, and opportunities for improvement.
  • Knowledge of forecasting, trend analysis, or predictive techniques to support capacity planning and resource optimisation.
  • Proven ability to collaborate with operational and customer-facing teams to turn data into actionable recommendations.
  • Experience improving data quality, consistency, and governance across systems and reporting processes.

What's In It For You

  • Generous holiday allowance with the ability to buy and sell annual leave for added flexibility.
  • Competitive company pension scheme.
  • Training and development within the role.
  • Access to thousands of discounts and vouchers through Pennon Perks.
  • 24/7 support with our Employee Assistance Programme.
  • Discretionary bonus.
  • Cycle to work scheme.
  • And more!

Closing Date: 1st July 2026

We may close this vacancy early if we receive a high volume of applications. We encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

Please note that the successful candidate will be subject to a mandatory DBS check as part of the onboarding process.

Be yourself, we like it that way. Together, we will build a culture of belonging, where inclusion is instinctive. Diversity is our strength and a reflection of our communities. We care, we value everyone, we celebrate uniqueness.

Our core values, which are essential to our success, are:

Be Rock Solid - Build trust and be trusted. Be the one we all look to and can depend on.

Be You - We want you to bring your best everyday. Be yourself and make your mark in your individual way.

Be the Future - Embrace change. Drive Progress. Own the challenge.

We look after the water and wastewater services for 1.8 million customers across Cornwall, Devon, the Isles of Scilly and parts of Dorset and Somerset.

From treatment and maintenance, to innovation and improvements, we're busy every day finding and fixing leaks, unblocking sewers and going bigger and further in our environmental work to be a company you can be proud of.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Pus Also Studios is a creative technology business from Howatson+Company that helps clients embrace technology and refocus their marketing investment on the high value strategic tasks that drive their businesses forward.

We exist to help clients get to higher quality, more measurable outcomes, faster with continual optimisation at the core.




What's my job?

Located in our Sydney or Melbourne office, you're a Data Analyst in the Plus Also Studios team. Your job is to turn data into decisions, extracting insights from complex datasets to inform client projects and internal initiatives. You'll work across data visualisation, pipeline automation, and cross-team collaboration, with a stack that includes Tableau, Python, BigQuery, and GCP. The work spans both the technical and the strategic, and the best outcomes happen when those two things connect.




A day in the life

Partner with the Plus Also Studios team and H+Co engineering, data, and media to contribute to data strategies that support client projects.
Set up and maintain measurement frameworks for Plus Also generated assets.
Manage ETL/ELT processes to keep data flowing cleanly between platforms - primarily Tableau and BigQuery within GCP, with Looker Studio, Power BI, Databricks, AWS, and Azure in the mix from time to time.
Build dashboards and reports that make complex data easy to understand for technical and non-technical stakeholders alike.
Use Python and SQL for data analysis and automation, drawing on libraries like Pandas and NumPy.
Audit client data stacks to identify opportunities for optimisation.
Collaborate on creative tech projects, ensuring data analytics are embedded throughout.




How will we know if you're the one?

Your data insights consistently inform decisions. Clients and internal teams rely on what you produce.
Your ETL/ELT processes run cleanly, with accurate data flowing between platforms without drama.
You can translate complex data into a clear, compelling story for a room full of people who didn't study statistics.
Stakeholders come back to you - not just for the numbers, but because you make the numbers mean something.
You spot opportunities where data can drive innovation before anyone else asks.




Other things that would make you excel?

Around 3-4 years of experience in a data analyst role.
Strong proficiency in Tableau (your primary BI tool) and solid SQL and Python skills.
Hands-on experience with ETL/ELT processes and cloud data platforms, particularly BigQuery and GCP.
Comfortable working across a range of BI tools (Looker Studio, Power BI) and cloud environments including Databricks, AWS, and Azure.
Experience working in an agency or client-facing environment.
A proactive mindset. You don't wait to be asked, you find where data can add value and go there.




Who are Plus Also Studios?

Plus Also Studios is a creative technology business by Howatson+Company. Our defining value is 'care fiercely'. Care is to trust, to be a human, to accept the responsibility that we put in each other, and our clients put in us. Fiercely brings the action.

How do we do it? Our ambition is to leave everything better than we found it. To move people, our industry, the world forward. All of this flows into how we behave:




Applies intersectional creativity Creativity is not a department, it's a philosophy. We combine unlikely disciplines to unlock full potential and innovation.




Smells uncertainty Stays ahead of opportunities and challenges




Finds new ways Excited by the unknown, who want to help create the next frontier of advertising




Exceeds expectations Outcome driven, that will set a new standard of delivery, who sweat the details and never settle for improving the way something is done




Adds to our culture Goes beyond their job description to make this the best place to work.







Plus Also Studios is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, colour, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. We invite anyone to apply.




If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let the recruitment consultant know.

Title: Data Enablement Analyst
Description:
You'll be the person who turns data into something people trust, reach for, and rely on - and you'll feel the difference that makes across every team you touch.

$113M funded. Global footprint. AI-powered. Human-driven. 🚀

INSHUR is the embedded insurance powerhouse for the on-demand world. We partner with giants like Uber and Amazon to provide fair, data-backed coverage for the modern workforce. We're 200 people strong across the UK, US, and Netherlands, using AI as a multiplier to stay ahead of the curve. Want to work where tech actually meets the road? You're in the right place.

Find out more about our history, values, hiring process and more here.

The role

As our Data Enablement Analyst you'll support the Data Enablement Manager in transforming how INSHUR accesses, understands, and uses data, acting as the connective tissue between business users and the data engineering team. Your mission: make data trusted, usable, and easier to consume. You'll be the first dedicated hire in this function, which means a real opportunity to shape how it grows.

We're based in Brighton, with a hybrid working model. You'll come into the office 1-2 days a week, with the rest of your time remote.

What you'll do

Connect Business Needs to Data Solutions: Working with teams across the business to understand their data needs and translate them into reporting requirements, dashboard improvements, and backlog items for data engineering.

Champion Dashboard Adoption and Self-Service: Onboarding teams to new dashboards and reports, gathering feedback on usability, and helping move the business towards genuine self-serve reporting.

Improve the Reporting Experience: Making existing reports clearer, more intuitive, and easier to act on, through better definitions, smarter layouts, and metrics that make sense at a glance.

Strengthen Data Quality and Understanding: Maintaining data definitions, dictionaries, and reporting guides that help people understand what the data means, where it comes from, and what its limitations are.

Spot Opportunities to Automate: Identifying manual reporting processes that can be streamlined or automated, freeing up time and reducing the risk of human error.

Support Change and Build Trust: Supporting the rollout of new data capabilities, gathering user feedback, and working with the Data Enablement Manager to turn insights into better adoption and deeper trust.

What we're looking for

We care more about how you think than what's on your CV. If you meet most of the essentials and are excited about the problem, apply.

Essential

We'd love to hear from you if you have these essentials to thrive in the role:


Comfort working with data and reports: You'll spend your days in dashboards, spreadsheets, and reporting tools, so a genuine ease with data is the foundation everything else is built on.
Understanding of business processes: Familiarity with finance, operations, or reporting cycles means you'll grasp the context behind the numbers and connect with stakeholders more naturally from day one.
Ability to explain data clearly to non-technical people: This role exists at the intersection of data and decision-making, your talent for making complex information accessible is what makes the whole thing work.
Organisation and attention to detail: From maintaining data dictionaries to keeping documentation current, your thoroughness ensures the resources you create are dependable and trustworthy.
Collaborative communication with stakeholders: You'll be gathering feedback, onboarding users, and communicating changes across multiple teams, building warm, responsive relationships is essential to enabling adoption and trust.
Experience in reporting or analytics: Whether you've built dashboards, written SQL queries, or supported reporting workflows, hands-on experience means you can contribute meaningfully from the start.
A curious mindset about how data is produced and used: The best person for this role doesn't just consume data — they want to understand where it comes from, how it flows, and how to make the whole journey better.


Nice to have


Experience in the insurance sector or a high growth tech business is beneficial, but not essential


How the Data team uses AI

Whether you're new to AI or already building with it, we give everyone the tools, time, and support to grow. AI is a multiplier here, it handles the repetitive so you can focus on the work that actually needs you. Here are a few ways the team is using it;


Speeding up everyday analysis tasks
Building dashboards and reports for Excel or Google Sheets data or BigQuery data.
Creating clear documentation for product details, data definitions, and key decisions.
Drafting Jira tickets, release notes, and executive summaries.


We also use AI tools to support our hiring process — not to make decisions, but to help us run a fairer, faster process. You can read more about how we approach that here, including tips for how you can use AI to prepare.

What to expect from the process


Screen: A 30-minute call with the Talent team. We'll cover the role, your background, and your questions.
First interview: 1 hour video call with the Data Enablement Manager and People Ops Lead to assess your experience and team fit
Onsite Case Study: Chance for you to meet more members of the team and work through a real life challenge


We design this to be a two-way conversation. Come with questions — we want you to leave knowing whether this is the right move for you.

What we offer

We've built a benefits package that invests in our people's long-term personal and professional growth and wellbeing. Here's a sample of what this includes;


Competitive salary, benchmarked to market by location. We discuss compensation openly from the first conversation
30 days of holiday annually, plus bank holidays
Private healthcare for you and your family, including pre-existing conditions
Life insurance at 3x your salary
13 weeks parental leave at full pay for new parents
Workplace nursery scheme with Gogeta, allowing you to pay fees pre-tax
Ride to work scheme
Workplace pension scheme to help you save for retirement
Flexible working hours to suit your lifestyle
£500 annual personal training allowance plus regular learning opportunities on a variety of topics
£40 monthly wellbeing to keep you healthy
24/7 Employee Assistance Program, covering you and immediate family members—offering crisis support, counselling, legal advice, menopause CBT, and more
Unlimited AI usage with Claude and Gemini


Working here

You'll Do Well Here If You

🚀 Move through ambiguity without waiting for a perfect brief

👊 Take ownership of outcomes, not just tasks

⚡ Adapt quickly when priorities shift, and help others do the same

🔧 Look for ways to improve how things work, not just how to complete them

🧠 Use tools, data, and colleagues to make better decisions

This Probably Isn't The Right Fit If You

📋 Prefer clearly defined processes and a stable, slow-moving environment

🚧 Are most comfortable staying within the boundaries of your job description

🔄 Find frequent context-switching draining rather than energising

Inclusion and flexibility

INSHUR is committed to building a team where everyone can bring their full self to work. We follow a structured hiring process and train our interview teams to support fairness and reduce bias.

We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, or age.

This role is advertised as full-time. We're open to discussing part-time, job-sharing, or other flexible arrangements for the right person.

If you need any adjustments during the process, let us know and we'll do our best to accommodate.

Title: Customer Data Insights Analyst
Description:
Permanent
Up to £ 50,000+ Pension + BUPA + ShareSave + 6.6 weeks holiday
Southampton/ Paddington Office ( few days per month )




We believe anyone can improve their home to make life better. From our Southampton Store Support office (SSO) we equip our stores, our people, and our whole business with everything it takes to help our millions of customers create a home they’ll love. Join us as a Customer Data Analyst and you’ll be a big part of this.

Working within the Analytics and Data Science team, you’ll turn data into powerful insights that make a real impact. You’ll collaborate with a wide range of stakeholders to deepen our understanding of customers, helping to shape future strategy, enhance the customer experience, drive engagement and revenue, and improve business efficiency across B&Q and TradePoint.




What's the job?

Help deliver the information provision for “The health of the customer”, including looking at contributions to growth, cohort analysis, churn rates.
Working within a matrix structure with other analysts, delivering a wide range of analytical products and projects, focusing on customer behaviour.
Deliver PIRs related to the B&Q Loyalty schemes (Club & TradePoint) and CRM activities: identifying opportunities and making informed recommendations.
Create forecasting and measure campaign performance across Marketing channels; true incrementality, ROI and contribution to owned exclusive brand mix.
Support on deep-dives and incremental analysis into performance to generate actionable insights.
Be an active member of the wider data community across the group, engaging with the group data team and other banners across the group.
Bring external thinking to B&Q by maintaining an awareness of analytics usage in retail and other sectors and by keeping abreast of competitor technology and developments.
Support the growth of new channels, Services, Digital, MarketPlace and Retail Media with key analysis and information.
Alongside the Analytics Manager, help build relationships with key stakeholders from across the business and translate their ambitions and goals into analytical challenges.




What we need:

Strong SQL skills, with experience in Python and/or R
Experience working in Agile, matrix environments, collaborating across teams and adapting to change
Hands-on experience with cloud data platforms (e.g. Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery) to analyse large, complex datasets
Proven experience in marketing campaign design and analysis, using customer data to optimise journeys, retention, and loyalty
Solid grounding in statistical analysis (e.g. hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, p-values)
High attention to detail, with a focus on data accuracy, validation, and quality
Strong understanding of data governance and data quality best practices
Interest in customer behavioural analysis and omnichannel personalisation
Experience using Jira and Confluence for workload management and documentation




What's in it for me?

As well as a competitive salary, our benefits package includes:

Award-winning pension scheme
Bonus
ShareSave options
6.6 weeks holiday
Payroll giving
Employee Assistance Programme
Shopping discounts
Colleague wellbeing benefits
…and much more.

We welcome applications from all candidates; however, please note that we are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this role, and applicants must have the right to work in the UK.




Equality and Inclusion

We want to ensure that all employees, future employees, and applicants to all Kingfisher companies are treated equally regardless of age, gender, marital or civil partnership status, colour, ethnic or national origin, culture, religious belief, philosophical belief, political opinion, disability, gender identity, gender expression, or sexual orientation.

Find out more about us, including the benefits we offer and our community, sustainability, and inclusion projects at bandqcareers.com.

If you require any additional support or adjustments to help you make an application, please contact us at:

ssorecruitment@b-and-q.co.uk
recruitment@b-and-q.co.uk

  • Experience working in Agile, matrix environments, collaborating across teams and adapting to change
  • Hands-on experience with cloud data platforms (e.g. Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery) to analyse large, complex datasets
  • Proven experience in marketing campaign design and analysis, using customer data to optimise journeys, retention, and loyalty
  • Solid grounding in statistical analysis (e.g. hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, p-values)
  • High attention to detail, with a focus on data accuracy, validation, and quality
  • Strong understanding of data governance and data quality best practices
  • Interest in customer behavioural analysis and omnichannel personalisation
  • Experience using Jira and Confluence for workload management and documentation

  • Shopping discounts

  • …and much more.

Title: Power BI Developer
Description:
Since 2024, 7-Eleven Australia has joined 7-Eleven international to be part of the biggest retail network across the world, represented in 20 countries with over 84,000 stores. We have big growth plans in Australia and a lot of opportunity for someone who wants to be part of ever growing retailer with a global footprint.

Firstly, what we offer you!


Vibrant Open Office in Richmond. Work in a dynamic, collaborative space that sparks creativity
Work Your Way. Enjoy the perfect balance of remote flexibility and in-office collaboration—get the best of both worlds
Make a Difference. Take a paid day off each year to volunteer for a cause you’re passionate about
Fuel Your Day. Enjoy free 7-Eleven coffee and snacks in the office—because great ideas start with great coffee
Family comes first. Get up to 15 weeks of paid parental leave for the primary carer and up to 4 weeks for concurrent leave, so you can focus on what matters most
Stay Social & Connected. Join our Social Club and Open Committee for regular events, celebrations, and fun activities
Grow Without Limits. Access unlimited LinkedIn Learning courses and invest in your personal and professional development



The role

Our Power BI Developer is responsible for ensuring that relevant insights are conveyed to the business using a suitable medium, and in a method which is clear, concise and easy to understand.

You will work with all stakeholders, gathering requirements and delivering insight and reporting solutions through innovative use of analysis and information presentation. Provide expert knowledge and guidance relating to data analysis and presentation.

Develop reporting, analytics and dashboard solutions that leverage the Enterprise Data Platform (EDP) to provide actionable insights into 7-Eleven’s operations and other key business performance metrics.

We need you to:


Create functional and technical design documents in collaboration with 7-Eleven and Support Vendor Engineers.
Lead or participate in functional and business requirements gathering sessions.
Design, develop and optimise the Power BI models measures and dimensions suited for immediate and future requirements.
Utilise Power BI to analyse large datasets and present compelling insights in appropriate visual formats – such as infographics, maps and other multidimensional charts and dashboards.
Supports the development and ongoing execution of the enterprise data quality management strategy.
Supporting 7-Eleven’s Enterprise Architecture by adhering to architecture standards, patterns, policies and principles.
Understand 7-Eleven’s Information Security, Identity and Access Management and Privacy policies and is responsible for ensuring that controls are being effectively managed.
Configuration, data requirements and test plans to satisfy functional unit tests, regression tests and system integration tests.



What’s In Your Toolkit


Technology or IT background
Extensive experience in Power BI or another market leading visualisation tool covering analytics and report development, data model optimisation and the tool administration
Familiarity with the agile ways and delivery method
A can-do attitude and good communication skills



At 7-Eleven our people are at the heart of everything we do. We are committed to creating a workplace that fosters inclusion and celebrates diversity. We strive to make every single 7-Eleven team member feel heard, valued, and respected no matter who they are or what diverse characteristics reflect their unique identity. We are proud to be a Diversity Council Australia Inclusive Employer 2024-2025 and Bronze Accredited with the Australian Workplace Equity Index.

At 7-Eleven, we are committed to ensuring that all prospective employees have the opportunity to perform at their best throughout our recruitment process. If you require any adjustments to support an inclusive and accessible experience, please contact us for a confidential discussion at peoplesupport@7eleven.com.au

** Please note, this email is strictly for adjustment requests related to the recruitment process. Other inquiries sent to this mailbox will not be actioned.**

To find out more about our current opportunities follow us on LinkedIn or view our careers page.

Title: Data Specialist
Description:
Responsibilities

Understand and identify stakeholders' reporting and data visualization requirements.
Translate business needs into prototypes and fully functional dashboards.
Collect, prepare, and assemble data for reporting and visualization purposes.
Develop, maintain, optimize, publish, and retire dashboards and reports throughout their lifecycle.
Create clear data visualizations and data-driven stories to support decision-making.
Ensure compliance with data governance, reporting standards, and security requirements.
Collaborate with Data Engineers to develop and enhance sustainable data solutions and data assets.
Promote data consistency, reuse, and alignment with organizational data strategy.
Establish and implement reporting and visualization best practices.
Act as an ambassador for reporting governance and visualization standards.
Document reporting solutions and train end users on reporting tools and dashboards.
Contribute to OPC optimization projects using innovative data technologies.
Deliver periodic operational, tactical, strategic, and regulatory reports.




Technical Skills

Data visualization and dashboard design.
Data preparation and transformation for reporting purposes.
Power BI.
Tableau.
Advanced Microsoft Excel.
Knowledge of Data Warehouses and Data Marts.
Understanding of data extraction and preparation processes.
Knowledge of data governance, security, and data strategy.
Ability to create data-driven insights and support decision-making.




Preferred Technical Skills

SQL.
SAS.
Python.




Business Skills

Strong communication and presentation skills.
Ability to explain complex data clearly to stakeholders.
Stakeholder management and relationship-building skills.
Negotiation and collaboration skills.




Soft Skills

Analytical thinking.
Attention to detail and accuracy.
Adaptability.
Client-focused mindset.
Results-oriented approach.




Preferred Domain Knowledge

Banking or Financial Services experience.
Knowledge of GDPR and data governance practices.

Title: Data Visualisation Specialist
Description:
We're looking for a detail-oriented and analytical professional to join our client's team as a Data Visualisation Specialist. In this role, you will be pivotal in developing and maintaining business reporting while working closely with various stakeholders to meet their visualization needs.

Responsibilities And Role

The successful candidate will support internal partners by executing various data reporting and visualization projects, which include:


Assessing stakeholder visualization and reporting needs while capturing requirements to craft prototypes and complete dashboards.
Collecting and assembling data to aid in the prototyping of visualizations.
Overseeing the entire life-cycle of dashboards and reports including development, updating, optimization, publication, and decommissioning in line with governance guidelines.
Collaborating with Data Engineers to ensure sustainable data solutions meet new business demands.
Advocating for and implementing reporting and visualization best practices based on predefined standards.
Documenting processes and training users for effective tool utilization.
Engaging in optimization projects while delivering periodic reporting reflective of new developments.



Profile and Ideal Candidate

The ideal candidate will possess a bachelor’s or master’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field and have a minimum of 5 years of relevant experience, including strong technical expertise in:


Preparing data for visualization and constructing narratives to guide decision-making efforts.
Data visualization, dashboard design, and knowledge of data concepts such as Data Warehouses and Data Marts.
Understanding governance and security measures related to data handling.
Proficiency in reporting and visualization tools, particularly advanced MS Office (Excel), Power BI, and Tableau.
Familiarity with SQL and ideally experience with SAS and Python.



Business Acumen And Soft Skills

In addition to technical qualifications, you must have:


Strong communication skills for presenting complex data clearly.
The ability to negotiate and build relationships with internal stakeholders.
Experience within the banking or financial services industry and an understanding of data governance and GDPR compliance is a plus.
Adaptability, a client-focused attitude, and a result-oriented approach.
Excellent analytical thinking and attention to detail.



Fluency in English and Dutch is required, along with the capability to work in a hybrid setting (50% onsite and 50% remote).

Title: Data Analyst Consultant
Description:
Mission Overview:

This is a consultancy mission for a Data Analyst, where you will represent Keystone Solutions on-site at a client in the financial sector. As a Keystone Solutions consultant, you will be embedded within the client's Customer Analysis team, working closely with internal stakeholders to translate business needs into actionable data-driven insights. All responsibilities and requirements listed below will be performed under Keystone Solutions’ consultancy model, offering you the opportunity to work on dynamic projects across various client environments.

Key Responsibilities:


Act as a business and data analyst within the Customer Analysis team, collaborating with team members and stakeholders to translate business needs into hypotheses for verification using client data.
Demonstrate strong knowledge of the business context and apply a critical, analytical mindset to research needs, focusing on key outcomes.
Identify, transform, and analyze relevant data (variables, formats, sources) to support business needs and client-data related questions.
Gather, transform, and analyze raw data using sourcing techniques.
Deliver business impact to improve customer approach.
Present concise stories in PowerPoint with key conclusions and recommendations based on data analysis.



Language Requirements:


Fluency in Dutch, French, and English is mandatory.



Education:


Master’s degree in economics or another academic background with substantial knowledge and experience in data and analytics in a business context.



Required Experience and Knowledge:


At least 4 years of relevant experience in Data Analytics.



Technical Experience:


Data analysis is part of your DNA.
Proven experience with SAS EG, SQL, and Python for data capture and transformation.
Power BI reporting skills.
Ability to set up ad hoc queries and reports using various data analytics tools.
Eagerness to identify and understand relevant data, analyze it effectively, and provide actionable business recommendations.
Experience with Gitlab is an asset.



Business Experience:


Good knowledge of bank and insurance products and the sector is mandatory.



Soft Skills:


Team player
Quick self-starter with a proactive attitude
Strong communication and influencing skills
Excellent analytical and synthesis skills
Autonomy, commitment, and perseverance
Ability to work in a dynamic, multicultural environment
Customer-oriented, critical, and flexible



Why Join Keystone Solutions?


Consultancy Nature: Embrace your identity as a consultant, working on-site and delivering value directly to clients.
Dynamic Projects: Experience a wide variety of challenges and environments, broadening your expertise across multiple client projects.
Turbo-Charged Learning: Accelerate your professional development with exposure to diverse business contexts and advanced analytics tools.
Skyrocketing Ambition: Grow your career within a framework that values ambition and supports your upward trajectory.
K-Stone Values: As a K-Stone, you bring our core values—commitment, flexibility, and excellence—to every engagement.



Ready to Apply?

If you are ready to tackle technical and strategic challenges in a dynamic consultancy environment, apply today at Keystone Solutions Career Portal.

Title: Data Full Stack
Description:
Health can’t wait.

Not for symptoms to get worse. Not for a six‑month appointment. Not for a system to catch up. But that’s exactly how healthcare works today. You wait, until you can’t.

Alan exists to end the wait.

Health is a universal right, and we believe this right can only become real when it’s coupled with prevention. We need to stop treating health as something we repair and start treating it as something we build, every day. It’s not solely a question of willpower. It’s the healthcare system itself that needs to work for everyone, in a sustainable way.

So we are building the new standard in prevention insurance. Alan is the first company that integrates insurance, prevention, and care into a single, acclaimed user experience.

We are on an incredible journey to build a global leading company, with a unique culture. We already partner with 40K+ companies of all sizes, serving more than 1M+ members, and have reached €800M+ in ARR.

Prevention as the new norm. That's what we're building with our team of 800+ people. If it speaks to you: we're hiring across France, Spain, Belgium, and Canada. And beyond.

⭐️ The Data Team


Being part of Alan's data team means you'll have a positive impact on people's health and wellbeing.
We create insights to help our colleagues focus on what is most impactful.
We build features and tools powered by data that directly help our members.
Examples include:
going deep into health consumption patterns,
understanding our product usage,
creating visibility to help our sales team perform,
automating how we reimburse our members,
building products that help our members stay healthy...

We also believe in having a full stack approach:


Each one of us contributes to all 3 aspects of the data spectrum (analytics, engineering, science). This encourages strong ownership and drives impact.
We own our infrastructure. We pride ourselves on a nimble data stack that gives us the flexibility we need. We're mainly using SQL, Python, R, Airflow, Snowflake, and Metabase.
We build in-house tooling to organize our data and serve it to the rest of the company.


🎯 Expected Skills And Abilities



You would thrive at Alan if most of this sounds like you:


Passionate about owning ambitious problems and developing simple solutions for them
Demonstrated impact during a first successful experience.
Experience or willingness to grow in all 3 aspects of the data spectrum (analytics, engineering, science) -- being specialized in one of these areas is good too!
Proficient in SQL, Python and/or R- Setting a high standard of rigor and precision for yourself, interested in your craft
Great listening and communication skills
Humble, kind, collaborative, and still willing to grow (no matter your seniority!)
Fluent and clear in English, both oral and written (no need to speak French!)


For this role we are looking to hire within the C1-E level range.

⚙️ Important note: we hire people, not roles.

If you're excited about this opportunity but don't check every box, we'd love to hear from you. Everyone, no matter how underrepresented, should feel free to apply, as it can only bring learnings or success.

If you identify yourself as a woman: Did you know that research shows women often apply only when meeting 100% of requirements?

Remember, this is just a guide, not a checklist.

We'll be thrilled to receive your application!

🔖 Check out our About Alan and Career pages, as well as our Medium, blog and Glassdoor page for more info.

You want to know more about Alan?


🙌 Perks & Benefits: Alaners are provided with a stimulating environment and perks ensuring they are happy, efficient and spend only high-quality time with co-workers.
🤘 A strong culture: People joining Alan are often surprised and delighted by our innovative working method. We have a set of cultural values that guide our approach to work


🔄 A quick note about the process

Note: While you're applying for a specific area, you may join a different engineering team based on where we think you'll have the most impact. Check out descriptions of other areas here.

Title: Business Analyst
HTML:
The world is more connected than ever before and the pace of change is bewildering. We deliver the best combination of technology and services to enable our clients to fulfil the diverse needs of their customers. By fostering a deeply and personal relationship with our clients, we want to improve the lives of people and organizations.

Axians is the VINCI Energies brand dedicated to ICT and Digital Transformation. We’re present across 38 countries, with more than 17 000 employees and an annual revenue of more than 3.8 billion euros, in 2025.

The best of ICT with a human touch.


Why Join Axians European Institutions' Team?

Multicultural Environment 🌍: You will work alongside professionals from all over the world, fostering a diverse and enriching workplace.

Diverse Projects 📝: Engage in impactful projects with cutting-edge technological advancements facilitated by European Institutions.

Skill Development 🚀: Work in a knowledge-driven environment where you'll continually develop both technical and professional skills through exposure to complex projects.

Long-term Opportunities 🏆: Our partnerships with European Institutions offer stable, long-term roles where you can make a real difference on a European scale.

Contributing to European Initiatives 🏛️: Make a meaningful impact on regional and global challenges.


We are looking for a Business Analyst to join our team in Brussels.


Responsibilities

  • Shadow and test business processes, analyse process issues, delays, bottlenecks, wastes;
  • Construct workflow charts and diagrams, study process and system capabilities, write specifications;
  • Gather, review and analyse business data, including KPIs, reports and other key metrics using data analytics tools;
  • Prepare reports, dashboards, visualisations by collecting, analysing, and summarizing information, trends, business or stakeholders needs;
  • Assess options for process improvement, automation, AI-based opportunities, take part of internal audits;
  • Take part of improvement and AI-related working groups, test new applications or features;
  • Propose and design systems improvements by shadowing, studying current practices and using operational metrics and reports;
  • Maintain documentation regarding processes and operations;
  • Define measurements for processes and improvement initiatives;
  • Identify key stakeholders for the initiatives, liaise between various departments and groups, conduct workshops to extract business needs and align on proposals;
  • Collaborate with and drive cross-functional teams, and communicate effectively.


Knowledge and skills

  • Bachelor’s degree in appropriate field (business analysis, operational research, mathematical modelling, statistics, data analytics or related fields), or 2 years hands-on experience in similar role;
  • Detail oriented, analytical, and inquisitive; great analytical, critical thinking and problem-solving abilities;
  • Experience with data analysis and modelling;
  • Knowledge of Python (maintain & update existing applications), AI productivity tools;
  • Excellent MS Office skills (Excel, Visio, PowerPoint);
  • Ability to effectively communicate and influence key stakeholders to support proposed strategies, process improvements and operational decisions;
  • Familiarity with brainstorming and collaborative methods and tools;
  • Technology enthusiast in general, AI enthusiast in particular.


Miscellaneous

  • Basic experience with/knowledge of machine learning, artificial intelligence, data management;
  • ITIL certifications;
  • Certifications like Business Analyst, Agilist, Scrum, Lean/Sig Sigma, aCAP, or other relevant;
  • EU citizenship.


Ready to embark on an exciting journey with us?

Apply now to join a vibrant team contributing to technological advancements in European Institutions.

Title: Digital Analytics and CRO Consultant
Description:
Why Keyrus, Why Now!

Keyrus is an international group of 2,800 consultants and experts across 28 countries, built on a single conviction: AI does not transform businesses. Architected intelligence does. For more than 30 years, we have been building the data foundations that make intelligent systems work — designing the Operating System of the intelligent enterprise, where intelligence is embedded into the core of business processes to create sustainable value: we operationalise intelligence.

AI does not replace humans. It repositions us to a place no system can follow: understanding, deciding, designing, and creating. At Keyrus, you will not just develop skills - you will develop judgment. Your expertise sharpens with every system you architect, every client challenge you solve, every deployment that compounds on the last.

Over time, you grow into one of the rarest professionals of the intelligence era: someone who bridges data, AI, and human decision-making at scale, across industries and geographies. This is not a role you fill. It is a discipline you master and a story you help write to become a Keyrus Architect of Intelligence.

Technology amplifies. Keyrus culture differentiates. Industrial discipline connects the two.

What You'll Architect

This role focuses on turning customer behavior into measurable, continuously improving performance systems. You design the feedback loops between data, experimentation, and experience.


Design measurement frameworks across websites and apps to capture reliable user behavior data
Implement and manage tagging strategies (e.g. Google Tag Manager, Adobe Launch, Tealium)
Analyse customer journeys by combining data across analytics, transactional, and behavioral sources
Translate data insights into hypotheses that improve user experience and marketing performance
Design, run, and evaluate experimentation programs (A/B tests, CRO initiatives)
Build dashboards and visualisations that support decision-making across teams
Leverage platforms such as Google Analytics 4, Adobe Analytics, or Contentsquare to drive insights
Query and structure data using SQL across environments such as BigQuery, Azure or Databricks
Collaborate with stakeholders to ensure accurate measurement and alignment on performance goals
Contribute to building scalable customer intelligence systems combining analytics, UX, and experimentation


Who You Are


You understand that analytics is not about reporting — it’s about improving decisions and experiences
You think in loops: data → insight → hypothesis → test → improvement
You combine analytical depth with curiosity about user behavior and digital experience
You are comfortable working across tracking, data analysis, and experimentation
You bring structure to complex customer journeys and fragmented data sources


What You Bring

Qualifications / Experience


3+ years of experience in Digital Analytics, CRO, or Digital Marketing Analytics
Experience analysing digital performance across web and/or app environments


Technical & Functional Skills


Experience with tagging and data collection (Google Tag Manager, Adobe Launch, Tealium)
Strong knowledge of digital analytics tools (Google Analytics 4, Adobe Analytics, Contentsquare)
Experience with SQL and data querying across platforms (BigQuery, Azure, Databricks)
Ability to combine multiple data sources into coherent analysis models
Understanding of UX principles, SEO, and digital marketing performance
Experience with A/B testing, experimentation design, and CRO methodologies
Experience with data visualisation and dashboarding for business users


Nice-to-haves


Exposure to machine learning or advanced analytics in marketing


Rewards - What We Offer At Keyrus

Growth

At Keyrus we have created an environment where people are supported, challenged and connected to the people around them.

Working on impactful Data & Digital projects you will develop a broad and versatile skill set and stay ahead in a fast-evolving, AI-driven landscape.

Our digital learning platform, with over 30,000 courses including in-house trainings developed by our experts, is a perfect way to stay on top of your game

A generous training budget is available to help foster a culture where learning, feedback and continuous improvement is the norm

You will have a dedicated coach, benefit from regular check-ins, learn via different communities and have clear alignment with your team and company goals

We want you to grow with confidence. We therefore place a strong emphasis on ownership, open feedback and continuous development.

When people feel trusted, connected and inspired, they do their best work!

Rewards

Mobility budget? Car with fuel or charging car? We offer both so you can choose!

On top of that we also have daily and monthly net allowances, eco vouchers, hospitalisation (DKV) insurance and group insurance, as well as a generous bonus scheme that you can use on our flexible reward plan.

What We Stand For

Collective Intelligence — Collaboration across expertise, functions, and geographies makes it possible to combine know-how and deliver more comprehensive responses to client challenges.

Reliability — The ability to deliver complex projects with rigour is one of the pillars of our client relationship.

Pragmatism — Prioritising concrete impact and measurable value over abstract technological discourse.

Entrepreneurial Spirit — Curiosity, energy, and freedom are the foundations of our culture; they enable initiative and sustained innovation.

About Keyrus

At Keyrus, we help organizations move from experimental AI to industrialized AI, from isolated agents to orchestrated systems, and from insight to execution. This is the discipline we call being an Architect of Intelligence. Designing the Operating System of the intelligent enterprise, where intelligence is embedded into the core of business processes to create sustainable value: we operationalize intelligence.

Powered by our proprietary Human Orchestrated Model™ (HOM), we architect reliable:


Intelligence Foundations,
Human in Command governance,
and Performance Steering,


To create intelligent environments where technology amplifies human capabilities and performance compounds over time.

With 30+ years of expertise and 2,800 employees across 28 countries, we help organizations go beyond transformation: to build adaptive, resilient, and continuously improving intelligent organizations.

AI does not transform businesses. Architected intelligence does.

For over 30 years, Keyrus have been Architects of intelligent systems. We have combined engineering, business consulting, data architecture, and AI in real operational environments. We have done this whilst never forgetting a more human enterprise of allowing humans to design, supervise, and shape what only they can create.. Find out more on our services on keyrus.com.

Title: Data Science Officer - Private Debt
Description:
Data Science Officer– Private Debt | AI, Data & Transformation Focus




Kartesia is an independent and privately-owned European specialist financing small and middle-market companies. We provide long-term liquidity and credit solutions following a fundamental and disciplined approach, based on our knowledge of the European small and mid-cap corporate market and our extensive professional network.




We are looking for a forward‑thinking Data Scientist to play a key role in the digital transformation of our Private Debt platform.




You will leverage advanced analytics, artificial intelligence, and optimized data flows to enhance investment decisions, strengthen reporting capabilities, and improve operational efficiency across the full credit lifecycle and broader firm processes.




This is not a traditional data scientist position, but a transformation role focused on embedding AI into investment processes, building best‑in‑class reporting, optimizing data architecture and flows, implementing agentic solutions, and driving measurable efficiency and scalability gains.

The role sits at the intersection of data science, credit expertise, and IT transformation, with high visibility across investment, risk, and management teams.




Key Responsibilities

AI & Advanced Analytics

Design and deploy AI/ML models and LLM-based systems to enhance
Credit risk assessment
Investment performance forecasting
Scenario analysis and stress testing
Build and orchestrate agentic AI to support end-to-end investment processes
Leverage NLP and generative AI to extract insights from
Financial statements
Credit memos
Legal documentation (loan agreements, covenants)
Explore innovative use cases of AI pilots to augment investment decision‑making and insight generation




Reporting & Data visualization

Enhance end‑to‑end reporting frameworks for:
Develop interactive dashboards (Power BI)
Ensure reporting is scalable, automated, and audit‑ready
Deploy reasoning agents for AI-driven enrichment




Data Flow Optimization & Architecture

Lead the optimization of data pipelines and data flows across systems
Improve data quality, governance, and consistency across platforms
Reduce manual processes through automation and smart data structuring




IT Transformation & Process Improvement

Act as a key contributor to the data & IT transformation roadmap
Partner with investment, risk, and legal teams to design workflows around AI capabilities
Drive the adoption of agentic AI solutions and LLM platforms




Required Experience

2 to 4 years of experience in data science, analytics, or quantitative roles, ideally within private debt, asset management, banking, or consulting
Proven exposure to data transformation / IT projects as to reporting automation and redesign







Required Technical skills

Strong understanding of LLM (Claude, GPT…), alongside solid foundations in machine learning techniques, data architecture and data modeling
Proven experience designing and deploying agentic AI systems including multi-step reasoning workflows, tool integration, orchestration and human-in-the-loop approaches
Proficiency in Python and SQL for analysis, modelling
Experience with BI and data visualization tools (Power BI), data pipelines and ETL/ETL tools (Data Factory), Cloud environments (Azure, Snowflake)

Title: CRO Specialist
Description:
Wat je gaat doen
Zie jij overal kansen om websites te verbeteren en kun je dat nog eens met data staven ook? Perfect! Als CRO Specialist bij iO maak je met jouw datagedreven optimalisaties écht impact op de conversies van websites en portalen. Jij vormt de brug tussen onze marketeers en developers en geeft hen de juiste input om mee aan de slag te gaan.

Op basis van jouw aanbevelingen uit verschillende onderzoeksmethodes, zet je proactief initiatieven op die de gebruikservaring en conversies een boost geven.

Jouw dagelijkse uitdagingen:

Van snelle audits tot diepgaande analyses: Je duikt in de data voor een brede waaier aan klanten. Je voert CRO-analyses en audits uit, brengt de pijnpunten in de customer journey bloot en vertaalt je inzichten naar concrete, impactvolle aanbevelingen.
Diepgaande optimalisatietrajecten: Je bijt je vast in complexe optimalisatieprogramma's. Hierin focus je toegewijd op het continu verbeteren van de gebruikerservaring en het maximaliseren van conversies


Dit alles doe je natuurlijk niet alleen. Je werkt nauw samen met een team van data- en analytics specialisten, UX-designers, marketeers en strategen. Je voert A/B-tests uit, analyseert de resultaten en verwerkt je bevindingen in professionele presentaties. Jouw inzichten giet je in overtuigende verhalen waarmee je zowel klanten als collega’s inspireert en aanzet tot actie.

Bij iO staan we nooit stil. We investeren volop in jouw technische kennis en persoonlijke ontwikkeling. Want wij weten als geen ander: samen staan we sterker.

Daarom ben jij de perfecte CRO Specialist

Je hebt een sterk analytisch vermogen, bent van nature nieuwsgierig, werkt nauwkeurig en hebt een proactieve houding
Online personalisatietools zoals VWO, Spotler, Adobe of Blueconic hebben voor jou geen geheimen
Je bent een krak in het toepassen van CRO-principes (zoals Cialdini en andere overtuigingstechnieken) op diverse businessmodellen (B2B, B2C, e-commerce)
Met minstens 2 jaar ervaring op de teller weet jij perfect hoe je een succesvol optimalisatieplan opstelt en uitvoert
Je vindt moeiteloos je weg in tools als Clarity en Hotjar
Complexe data uit Google Looker Studio, Google Analytics en PowerBI zet jij om in overtuigende en heldere inzichten
Je hebt ervaring met HTML en CSS. Kennis van JavaScript is een dikke plus!
Je weet hoe je duidelijke en overtuigende presentaties maakt en kunt je bevindingen uitstekend onderbouwen


Dit krijg jij van ons

32 vakantiedagen
Op basis van een 40-urige werkweek heb je 20 vakantiedagen en 12 ADV-dagen.

Mobiliteit & eindejaarspremie
Na 6 maanden in dienst kun je bij iO rekenen op een eindejaarspremie. En op mobiliteitsvlak bieden we verschillende mogelijkheden, passend bij jouw job of voorkeur.

Hospitalisatieverzekering
Heb je eens iets voor? Dan is extra stress rond kosten het laatste wat je kunt gebruiken. Iedereen geniet bij ons van een hospitalisatieverzekering bij Axa.

Hybride werken
Een goede werk-privébalans is essentieel. Samen met je team maak je afspraken over wanneer én waar je werkt.

Gezonde snacks & lunch
Op elke iO campus pak je de hele dag een gezond tussendoortje en lunch je voor een kleine vergoeding gezond én gevarieerd.

Persoonlijk ontwikkelbudget
Je krijgt elk jaar € 3.000,- om jezelf te ontwikkelen, in je vak of als persoon, met trainingen, events of cursussen.

Samen vieren
Als we iets te vieren hebben, dan doen we het goed! Het partyteam op jouw campus staat op z'n tijd dan ook garant voor de leukste (thema)feestjes.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
About TCS:

Tata Consultancy Services is an IT services, consulting and business solutions organization that has been partnering with many of the world’s largest businesses in their transformation journeys for over 50 years. TCS offers a consulting-led, cognitive powered, integrated portfolio of business, technology and engineering services and solutions. This is delivered through its unique Location Independent Agile™ delivery model, recognized as a benchmark of excellence in software development.




TCS has been serving customers in Switzerland since 1985. The company’s presence in Switzerland consists of offices in Zurich, Basel and Nyon. TCS Switzerland offers a broad spectrum of IT Services, Consulting and Financial Solutions. TCS has over 600+ consultants working in Switzerland and over 11000 associates supporting Swiss Clients from Offshore.




About this role:

Serve as a strategic advisor to client stakeholders and technical teams.
Drive business development by identifying and pursuing opportunities to sell data analytics services to Chief Data Officers and other key decision-makers.
Develop and present compelling presentations to senior customer executives and stakeholders
Work with customer to drive workshops and create data analytics charters and business cases.
Demand Creation: Work with customers to generate data analytics project opportunities
Customer Advisory: Lead Data & Analytics Assessment, Strategy and Roadmap engagements and drive customer workshops for analytics use cases
PreSales Leadership: Own stages of the presales cycle, RFI, RFP, and contribute to the development of solutions for presales




Desired Competencies:

Proven experience in a leadership role within data analytics.
Extensive experience with cloud data platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP, Snowflake, Databricks) and Lakehouse architecture.
Demonstrated ability to connect with customer business stakeholders to articulate business problem and approach for data analytics solutions.
Deep understanding of entire data life cycle including ingestion, modelling, data quality, data governance,Analytics solutions
Strong presentation and communication skills, with the ability to engage senior-level audiences.
Able to work with various levels of business organization to coordinate between stakeholders on pre-sales activities
Analyzing, understanding and documenting the business processes in detail where required to guide use case development.
Experience in manufacturing Industry
Strong ability to plan and manage multiple initiatives simultaneously.




Does that sound interesting?




You are welcome to submit your application consisting of your CV, personal letter, relevant degrees or certifications details, professional references, etc. We work with ongoing recruitment so send in your application as soon as possible.




We look forward to receiving your application via LinkedIn or mail to pallavi.malhotra@tcs.com




https://www.tcs.com/who-we-are/legal/tcs-privacy-notice/tcs-privacy-notice-european-union

  6. Customer Advisory: Lead Data & Analytics Assessment, Strategy and Roadmap engagements and drive customer workshops for analytics use cases
  7. PreSales Leadership: Own stages of the presales cycle, RFI, RFP, and contribute to the development of solutions for presales


Desired Competencies:

  • Proven experience in a leadership role within data analytics.
  • Extensive experience with cloud data platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP, Snowflake, Databricks) and Lakehouse architecture.
  • Demonstrated ability to connect with customer business stakeholders to articulate business problem and approach for data analytics solutions.
  • Deep understanding of entire data life cycle including ingestion, modelling, data quality, data governance,Analytics solutions
  • Strong presentation and communication skills, with the ability to engage senior-level audiences.
  • Able to work with various levels of business organization to coordinate between stakeholders on pre-sales activities
  • Analyzing, understanding and documenting the business processes in detail where required to guide use case development.
  • Experience in manufacturing Industry
  • Strong ability to plan and manage multiple initiatives simultaneously.


Does that sound interesting?


You are welcome to submit your application consisting of your CV, personal letter, relevant degrees or certifications details, professional references, etc. We work with ongoing recruitment so send in your application as soon as possible.


We look forward to receiving your application via LinkedIn or mail to pallavi.malhotra@tcs.com

Title: Stagiaire Data Analyst
Description:
Description de l'entreprise

Le présent et le futur d’Audemars Piguet se construisent grâce à la contribution de tous nos talents. Inspirés par la richesse de notre passé, nous sommes enthousiastes à l’idée de ce que nous créerons dans le futur. Ensemble, nous sommes résolument tournés vers l’avenir et en quête continue d’excellence dans tous nos métiers.

Si notre mission vous inspire, venez poursuivre votre parcours au sein de notre famille pour que l’on continue de créer l'extraordinaire. Écrivons le prochain chapitre de votre carrière ensemble !

Description du poste

Dans le cadre d’un projet stratégique de digitalisation de nos ateliers de production, nous proposons un stage de 6 mois, à partir de septembre 2026, centré sur la collecte et l’exploitation des données issues des moyens de mesure utilisés dans le flux de production horloger. Le projet s’inscrit au cœur de problématiques industrielles concrètes et aura un impact direct sur la robustesse et la performance de nos processus.

Dans ce cadre, vous serez en charge des principales tâches suivantes :


Structurer et consolider les données provenant de nos ateliers de production
Développer des outils analytiques permettant de créer de la valeur et de sécuriser notre production
Mettre en application ces outils au sein de notre production afin de proposer des réglages initiaux optimisés


Qualifications


Étudiant·e de Master en Data Science, Microtechnique, Génie mécanique, Informatique, ou domaine apparenté
Connaissances approfondies en programmation (Python ou MATLAB)
Maîtrise de MS office
Intérêt pour les systèmes de mesure et/ou la mécanique horlogère
Capacité à travailler de manière rigoureuse, structurée et méthodique
Esprit d’équipe, sens du terrain et motivation pour interagir de manière transverse

Title: Data Analyst befristet 6-8 Monate (w/m/d)
Description:
Galenica AG

Das kannst du bewirken


Du arbeitest praxisnah an konkreten Projekten und leistest damit einen messbaren Beitrag zu Geschäftsentscheidungen
Du übernimmst eigenverantwortlich Analyseprojekte und kannst dabei deine Stärken einbringen und baust gleichzeitig neue Fähigkeiten auf
Du arbeitest auf Augenhöhe mit Data Analysts, Data Scientists und Data Engineers zusammen und unterstützt vielfältige Analytics-Aufgaben
Wir begleiten dich beim Einstieg persönlich und bieten dir gezieltes Mentoring - wir nehmen uns Zeit für dich


Das bringst du mit


Du hast erste Berufserfahrung sowie ein abgeschlossenes oder bald abgeschlossenes Studium in Informatik, Mathematik, Wirtschaft, Naturwissenschaften oder einem verwandten Bereich mit quantitativem Schwerpunkt - idealerweise auf Masterstufe - oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation.
Du verfügst über sehr gute Kenntnisse in gängigen Datenanalyse-Tools wie SQL, Python oder R
Du denkst analytisch, arbeitest lösungsorientiert und selbständig und bringst die Motivation mit, dich aktiv einzubringen und weiterzuentwickeln
Du interessierst dich für Retail- und Healthcare-Themen sowie für die Schnittstelle zwischen Technologie und Business
Du verfügst über gute Englischkenntnisse; Deutsch und/oder Französisch sind von Vorteil


Diese Stelle besetzen wir ohne externe Personaldienstleiter.

Kontakt

Deine Bewerbung wird bearbeitet durch:

Galenica

Rebekka Borer

Specialist Recruiting

Title: Business & Customer Data Analyst
Description:
Business & Customer Data Analyst

Basel-Stadt




MCH Exhibitions & Events organizes leading B2B and B2C events in Switzerland and manages venue operations at Messe Basel and Messe Zürich.

To strengthen our data-driven decision making, we are looking for a Business & Customer Data Analyst who will transform data into actionable insights across marketing, sales, and event operations.

The role will help unlock the value of our visitor, exhibitor and marketing data to support audience growth, exhibitor success, and strategic decision making across our event portfolio. This position sits within the Technology, Intelligence & Marketing team and works closely with marketing, sales, and event teams.




Your responsibilities

Develop and maintain automated dashboards and reports providing insights on event performance, visitor behaviour, marketing effectiveness, campaign performance, exhibitor value, and business performance
Analyze and consolidate data across CRM, ticketing, marketing platforms, and other systems to generate clear insights and recommendations for business teams and leadership
Support leadership with data-driven reporting for strategic decisions
Develop visitor segmentation, audience intelligence models, and customer insights
Analyze visitor acquisition performance across channels and identify opportunities to improve audience targeting and marketing ROI
Provide insights on visitor behaviour across events, including visitor profiles, engagement, markets, and audiences
Provide analytics supporting exhibitor sales, retention, and value propositions
Support sales teams with data-driven insights to demonstrate exhibitor value
Ensure data quality, consistency, and governance within CRM and related systems
Support and improve CRM data structure, reporting capabilities, and analytics readiness
Define best practices for data management across teams
Support ongoing improvement of data infrastructure, integration, and analytics capabilities
Develop a company-wide event performance dashboard
Build visitor segmentation and audience intelligence models
Support marketing teams with campaign performance analytics
Provide insights to improve exhibitor value propositions
Improve CRM data structure to support analytics




Your profile

3 to 5 years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or CRM analytics
Experience working with customer data, marketing data, and CRM analytics, ideally Salesforce
Experience translating complex data into clear business insights for non-technical stakeholders
Strong SQL skills
Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar
Advanced Excel skills
Comfortable using AI tools for building, analysis, and automation
German native and business-proficient English
Nice to have: marketing analytics, data modeling, and experience working with multiple data sources
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities
Curiosity and interest in understanding business challenges
Ability to work across teams, functions, and stakeholder groups

Title: Data Analyst Trainee
Description:
Make your mark for patients

Nous recherchons un/e Stagiaire en Ingénierie de données qui est dynamique et autonome pour rejoindre notre service digital encré au sein d’amélioration continue, basé dans notre site de production de Bulle en Suisse.

Stage en Informatique - Ingénierie des données

À propos du rôle

En tant que coordinateur de données stagiaire chez UCB, vous contribuerez activement à la valorisation des données métiers utilisées sur un site de production biopharmaceutique. Vous évoluerez au sein d’un environnement où qualité et transformation digitale sont au centre des priorités.

Votre mission consistera à faire le lien les équipes opérationnelles, les équipes digitales et les systèmes d’information, afin de garantir que les données soient exploitable en analysant les besoins et en modélisant les processus. Vous concevez des dashboards stratégiques qui créent de la valeur et servent de base aux prises de décision.

Vous évoluerez dans un environnement où la qualité des données est un prérequis essentiel pour la prise de décision, la performance opérationnelle et la conformité.

Ce que vous ferez

Qualifier et documenter les données métiers


Identifier les sources de données (process, systèmes, outils)
Documenter les jeux de données (définition, usage, fiabilité)
Contribuer à la création d’un langage commun autour de la donnée


Faire le lien entre métier et digital


Recueillir les besoins des utilisateurs (ateliers, interviews, terrain)
Traduire les besoins métiers en exigences data compréhensibles
Sensibiliser les équipes à la bonne utilisation des données


Contribuer aux initiatives de transformation digitale


Supporter le Digital Transformation Lead sur les projets liés à la donnée
Mise en place de solutions de suivi et de visualisation (Power BI ou Qlik sense)
Contribuer à l’automatisation et à la standardisation des flux data (Power Platform)


Intéressé(e) ? Pour ce rôle, nous recherchons


Bachelor et Master (obtenu/en cours) en Informatique et systems de communication
Bonne compréhension de la modélisation de processus.
Intérêt marqué pour la gouvernance des données, la qualité et la structuration de l’information,
Capacité d'analyse, esprit critique, curiosité.
Aisance relationnelle et capacité à collaborer avec des équipes multidisciplinaires.
Connaissance des outils digitaux : Power BI, QlikSense, Power Apps, Excel avancé.
La connaissance du milieu biotechnologique & pharmaceutique est un atout, mais pas obligatoire.
Bonne compréhension de l’anglais


Nous offrons un stage rémunéré d’une durée jusqu’à 6 mois ; date d’entrée en fonction : courant 2026, négociable selon les disponibilités.

Are you ready to ‘go beyond’ to create value and make your mark for patients? If this sounds like you, then we would love to hear from you!

About Us

UCB is a global biopharmaceutical company, focusing on neurology and immunology. We are over 9.000 people in all four corners of the globe, inspired by patients and driven by science.

Why work with us?

At UCB, we don’t just complete tasks, we create value. We aren’t afraid to push forward, collaborate, and innovate to make our mark for patients. We have a caring, supportive culture where everyone feels included, respected, and has equal opportunities to do their best work. We ‘go beyond’ to create value for our patients, and always with a human focus, whether that’s on our patients, our employees, or our planet. Working for us, you will discover a place where you can grow, and have the freedom to carve your own career path to achieve your full potential.

At UCB, we’ve embraced a hybrid-first approach to work, bringing teams together in local hubs to foster collaborative curiosity. Unless expressly stated in the description or precluded by the nature of the position, roles are hybrid with 40% of your time spent in the office.

UCB is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions will be made without regard to any characteristic protected by applicable laws.

Should you require any adjustments to our process to assist you in demonstrating your strengths and capabilities contact us on EMEA-Reasonable_Accommodation@ucb.com. Please note should your enquiry not relate to adjustments; we will not be able to support you through this channel.
