Title: Senior Threat Intelligence Analyst, SEAR
Description:
Summary
As part of our efforts to protect our users, Apple is looking for a world-class senior threat intelligence analyst to join a team of security researchers and threat intelligence analysts. This team works together and cross-functionally to drive efforts to solve security engineering challenges, with an emphasis on supporting decisions that provide the greatest long-term security. The success we are striving for is the result of very skilled people working in an environment that cultivates creativity, partnership, and solving old problems in new ways. Protecting over a billion endpoints. Operationalizing security research and threat intelligence into security engineering decisions affecting every layer of the stack. Touching the lives of everyone who interacts with our products. Working for a company whose commitment to user privacy is at the heart of everything it does. Being surrounded by the most creative, passionate, hardworking engineers in the industry. Constantly being challenged to go beyond the norm to find new, innovative ways of solving problems and to make software safer, easier, and more fun to use. You can find all of this in one opportunity, and only at Apple.

Description
You will have a broad set of skills that make you successful as threats continue to evolve, capable of analyzing cyber threats to our users’ devices. Our environment fosters innovation, rapid solution development, and a liberating amount of autonomy. You will be expected to work with security engineers and researchers in order to quickly bring to market new security methods and technologies addressing the complex needs of mobile and distributed computing.

Responsibilities

Provide input into development of security technologies.
Analyze and understand advanced cyber actors, capabilities, and techniques.
Analyze and understand exploit development and proliferation.
Collaborate with security researchers to drive engineering solutions.
Work with security engineers in designing innovative mitigations to cyber threats while preserving privacy, ease-of-use, and user experience.
Work closely with external partners in support of cyber threat intelligence activities.
Remote work, with occasional travel.


Minimum Qualifications

In-depth understanding of the threat intelligence cycle and cyber operations.
Significant years of experience conducting threat research, preferably as a technical lead.
Experience in analyzing highly-complex threat actor campaigns, including infrastructure, malware, and victim endpoints.
Ability to distill complex problems into manageable tasks and then drive those tasks to completion.


Preferred Qualifications

Intellectually curious team player with a genuine passion for discovering, understanding, and mitigating cyber threats.
Some knowledge of iOS internals.
Impeccable attention to detail.
Growth mindset, thrives in fast-paced, changing environment.
Excellent communications skills (verbal and written).
Proven history of successfully working on solo and group projects (experience with virtual/remote collaboration is a plus).


At Apple, we're not all the same. And that's our greatest strength. We draw on the differences in who we are, what we've experienced, and how we think. Because to create products that serve everyone, we believe in including everyone. Therefore, we are committed to treating all applicants fairly and equally. We will work with applicants to make any reasonable accommodations.

At Apple, we believe accessibility is a fundamental human right. You’ll find that idea reflected in everything here — in our culture, our benefits and our digital tools. By welcoming as many perspectives as possible, we help you build a career where you feel like you belong.

Learn about accessibility in Apple’s workplace

Role Number: 200663822-4170

Title: IT & Market Data Procurement Analyst
Description:
Established in 2004, Mercuria is one of the leading integrated energy and commodity trading companies in the world. We bring energy markets together to support the needs of today by trading, structuring finance, and investing into strategic assets, while generating more than $110 billion in turnover.




Our operations span over 50 countries on 5 continents, including all the major energy hubs. We trade physical oil, energy products, environmental products and other commodities from Geneva, London, Singapore, Shanghai, Beijing, Dubai, Houston, Calgary and Greenwich (CT).




We are committed to advancing the transition to a more sustainable, affordable and reliable energy system for tomorrow. Over 50% of our assets are in low carbon & energy transition sectors - providing a strong platform to trade these new markets and support decarbonization.




In 2023, we established Silvania, a $500 million fund, investing into restoration and protection of nature and biodiversity globally and in support of the Paris Agreement goals & UN 30x30 biodiversity initiative. This new asset class supports nature protection and provides sustainable financial returns.










The role




This role sits within the CIO Office in Geneva and is responsible for end-to-end procurement, commercial management, and financial governance across Mercuria’s global technology estate, with a particular focus on IT services, software, and market data.

The position combines hands-on operational execution with commercial ownership and transformation, supporting IT leadership and senior business stakeholders to optimise technology spend, manage vendor relationships, and professionalise procurement processes across a fast-paced commodity trading environment.

The role actively promotes the adoption of data, automation, and AI-enabled capabilities within procurement activities

The role has global scope, high visibility, and regular exposure to senior management.










Key responsibilities




IT & Market Data Procurement

Manage the full procurement lifecycle for IT hardware, software, consultancy services, and market data
Support and challenge new technology spend, ensuring alignment with commercial best practice and governance
Act as a key interface between IT, business stakeholders, and external vendors




Market Data & Software Asset Management

Own software and market data subscriptions (licenses, renewals, usage, compliance)
Optimise costs through rationalisation and a strong understanding of vendor licensing models
Support evolving business needs (new desks, usage, trading activities)




Commercial & Contract Management

Manage IT contract renewals and vendor negotiations in collaboration with IT, Finance, and Legal
Maintain accurate contract records and strong vendor relationships
Monitor market trends and supplier roadmaps to anticipate risks and opportunities




Financial Management & Reporting

Track and analyse IT spend across software, hardware, consultancy, and market data
Support IT financial reporting, forecasting, and budget visibility
Perform spend analysis to identify trends, risks, and optimisation opportunities




Governance & Stakeholder Engagement

Prepare and support PMO, procurement, and governance forums
Produce high-quality reporting and materials for senior management and C-level audiences
Liaise effectively with global internal stakeholders and external suppliers
Promote strong procurement and compliance culture across the organisation.




Transformation & Continuous Improvement

Drive the shift from reactive to proactive, value-driven procurement
Lead process automation, digitalisation, and self-service initiatives
Leverage data, analytics, and AI tools to enhance reporting, controls, decision-making, and operational efficiency
Challenge tactical purchasing behaviours in favour of strategic procurement practices










Technical expertise




+3 years of experience in a similar role
Bachelor’s degree
Proven experience in IT procurement, commercial management, or technology finance
Exposure to market data and software licensing within financial services, trading, or a similar environment is highly desirable
Experience operating in a fast-paced, high-change environment with senior stakeholders exposure
Strong commercial and analytical skills, with the ability to interpret spend data and identify optimisation opportunities
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills
Interest in process improvement, automation, and AI-enabled capabilities
Fluent English










Non-technical Skills




Self-motivated and adaptable, with a strong desire to learn and develop
Comfortable working under pressure and managing competing priorities
Flexible and pragmatic in approach, suited to a trading-driven environment
Confident presenting to senior management and executive audiences
Highly organised, detail-oriented, and proactive
Able to balance hands-on execution with continuous improvement initiatives

Title: Contrôleur de gestion junior & Data analyst (h/f/x)
Description:
Vos missions

En qualité de Contrôleur de gestion junior & Data analyst (h/f/x), vous assurez le suivi des dépenses par centre de frais,
Vous analysez et commentez les écarts du compte de résultat (financier et analytique),
Vous soutenez l’établissement du budget par centre de frais,
Vous participez aux bouclements mensuels ainsi qu’à l’élaboration de plans futurs,
Vous développez des KPI et des rapports / outils BI de performance en collaboration avec les responsables fonctionnels de l’entreprise,
Vous réalisez des analyses spécifiques et pilotez les mesures d’optimisation,


Votre profil

Vous êtes titulaire d’un Bachelor ou d’un Master en économie d’entreprise, finance ou dans un domaine similaire,
Vous bénéficiez d’au minimum 2 à 3 ans d’expérience dans le controlling ou l’analyse de données, idéalement dans un environnement industriel,
Vous maîtrisez le français et l’anglais et possédez de bonnes connaissances en allemand,
Vous maîtrisez la suite MS Office et disposez de bonnes connaissances d’un ERP,
Vous êtes une personne autonome, flexible et dotée de bonnes aptitudes à travailler en équipe,
Vous vous distinguez par vos compétences analytiques, vos aptitudes en communication ainsi que votre capacité à vous imposer.


Notre offre
Pouvons-nous vous motiver à endosser un rôle clé dans une entreprise familiale, dynamique et indépendante ? Nous vous encourageons à partager et à développer vos idées et vous offrons un emploi sûr, des conditions de travail modernes et conciliables avec la vie familiale telles que des horaires de travail flexibles, la possibilité de travailler en Home office, un restaurant d'entreprise, des perspectives de formation continue et de nombreux autres avantages. Le lieu de travail se situe à Bulle, dans la belle région de la Gruyère. Planifiez avec nous votre développement personnel et professionnel.
Afin de garantir une procédure de candidature équitable, nous n’acceptons pas les candidatures par courrier postal ou électronique.
Ce poste est soumis à une procédure de vérification des antécédents.
Avons-nous suscité votre intérêt ? Si c’est le cas, nous serions ravis de recevoir votre candidature en ligne. Si vous avez des questions, veuillez contacter Nadege MALAN.

Title: VP, Global Lead HPE
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a VP, Global Lead HPE based in Switzerland.

This is a high-impact global leadership role responsible for shaping and scaling a multi-billion-dollar technology partnership across regions and business units. You will define and execute a long-term transformation strategy that elevates the portfolio toward higher-value solutions across cloud, networking, data, AI, security, data center, and services. Operating at executive level within a complex global matrix organization, you will align regional leaders, vendor stakeholders, and internal teams around a unified growth vision. The role demands strong commercial acumen, strategic foresight, and the ability to influence senior stakeholders across geographies. You will act as the key driver of global execution consistency while adapting strategies to regional market realities. This is a rare opportunity to lead a flagship global vendor relationship with significant revenue responsibility and long-term strategic influence.

Accountabilities


Own and drive the global HPE business portfolio, ensuring sustained growth and strategic expansion across regions
Define and execute a multi-year transformation strategy aligned with higher-value solutions in cloud, networking, data & AI, security, data center, and services
Lead and coordinate a global matrix organization, ensuring alignment across regions, countries, and business units
Build and maintain executive-level relationships with HPE stakeholders to secure financial, technical, and programmatic support
Oversee global rhythm of business, including monthly and quarterly performance reviews and governance cycles
Collaborate with internal transformation and next-generation teams to develop differentiated go-to-market strategies and solution offerings
Drive end-to-end execution of initiatives from strategy design through regional and local implementation


Requirements


10-15+ years of experience in enterprise technology, infrastructure, or vendor management environments
15+ years of leadership experience managing and developing teams in complex, global organizations
Proven track record in strategic planning, go-to-market execution, and large-scale vendor partnership management
Strong experience operating in matrixed environments, with the ability to influence without direct authority
Deep understanding of infrastructure, hardware, software, and channel ecosystems
Strong commercial and technical acumen with the ability to translate technology capabilities into business strategy
Excellent communication and executive presentation skills, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders globally
Demonstrated ability to negotiate, build consensus, and drive mutually beneficial outcomes
Strong organizational and execution capabilities, with the ability to manage multiple high-priority initiatives under pressure
High ethical standards, sound judgment, and strong interpersonal skills across multicultural environments
Bachelor’s degree in engineering, business, or related field preferred
Fluency in English required; additional languages considered an advantage


Benefits


Senior executive leadership role with global scope and high strategic impact
Competitive compensation package aligned with executive responsibility
Comprehensive benefits tailored to local country standards, including healthcare and insurance support
Strong focus on leadership development and executive career growth opportunities
Access to global learning programs, training, and professional certifications
Inclusive, international working environment with strong cross-functional collaboration
Participation in global initiatives, communities, and leadership networks
Flexible working culture supporting balance between autonomy and global coordination


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. Title: Réceptionniste stagiaire
Description:
Fort d'une solide expertise, la Clinique de Genolier se distingue par son équipe expérimentée et qualifiée et son équipement médical à la pointe de la technologie.



La clinique de Genolier propose une palette de services étendue couvrant la cardiologie, la neurologie, l'oncologie, la chirurgie orthopédique et la chirurgie plastique. Par ailleurs, la clinique met à disposition de ses patients une vaste gamme de prestations axées sur la prévention et la rééducation, pour favoriser leur santé et leur bien-être.



La renommée de la Clinique de Genolier repose sur ses infrastructures médicales d'excellence et ses technologies avant-gardistes. Parmi elles, un centre de diagnostic ultramoderne, une unité de soins intensifs performante et une salle d'opération équipée des instruments et appareils dernière génération pour assurer des soins de qualité optimale.





Description du poste


Accueil des patients et des visiteurs : admission hospitalière et urgence.
Gestion des appels téléphoniques, de la messagerie, des agendas Outlook, du courrier et des colis.
Gestion des plaintes patients.
Gestion caisse.
Réaliser et traiter les demandes de transport.
Diverses tâches administratives.
Avoir un rôle d'ambassadeur.


Qualifications


Posséder une expérience dans le domaine de l'accueil, avoir une excellente présentation, ainsi que le sens du contact et du travail en équipe.
Etre de langue maternelle française avec une bonne maîtrise de l'anglais, l'allemand serait un atout.
Polyvalence, flexibilité, autonomie, organisation et précision.
Expérience dans le domaine l'hôtellerie de luxe serait également un atout.


Informations supplémentaires


Une activité dans un domaine passionnant, au sein d'une entreprise dynamique, stimulante et en pleine expansion.
Un cadre de travail agréable.





Nous accordons une grande attention à la diversité des parcours de vie de nos collaboratrices et collaborateurs. Nos postes sont ouverts tant aux femmes qu'aux hommes.

Title: Data Engineer (Palantir)
Description:
Who we are




SII Group is a trusted technology partner, SII provides high value added solutions for the IT projects of many large corporations. Since its founding in 1979, the Company has been providing solutions adapted to its clients’ needs, by relying on :




> Its acknowledged expertise in various industries and sectors

> Proven 'turnkey' solutions

> An efficient quality system

> Adaptable, evolving services




Today, with a staff of more than 18'000, SII Group is supporting companies across 20 countries.




At SII Switzerland we pay attention to the personal and professional wellbeing of our employees. We place our collaborators at the heart of our actions and activities. If you are motivated by the perspective of joining a big, trusted and recognized group, then let's meet !




To develop our consulting offer and support one of our clients, we are looking for a talented Data Engineer (Palantir).




Missions

Build Pipelines in Foundry: You develop and scale data pipelines (ETL/ELT) directly within Palantir Foundry (e.g., via Code Repositories, Pipeline Builder) and integrate data from a wide variety of source systems.
Data Modeling & Ontology: You help map our data into the Foundry Ontology, making complex business logic tangible and accessible for analysts and end-users.
Data Quality & Lineage: You implement health checks, monitor data quality across the entire data lineage process, and ensure that pipelines run stably.
Code & Transformation: You write clean, efficient code (primarily in PySpark and SQL) to clean and transform large volumes of data.
Team Collaboration: You work closely with Data Scientists, Analysts, and business departments to translate their requirements into functioning data models within Foundry.




Profile

Initial professional experience in Data Engineering (as a Junior, e.g., through relevant internships/working student roles; as a Mid-Level, approx. 1–3 years in a permanent role).
Good to excellent knowledge of Python (ideally experience with PySpark or Pandas) and are highly proficient in SQL.
Prior experience with Foundry (e.g., Contour, Code Repositories, Ontology) is a massive plus. If not: You have a strong foundation in data processing and are highly motivated to dive deep into the Palantir ecosystem.
Solid basic understanding of relational databases, data architectures, and the challenges of working with distributed systems (Big Data).
Solution-oriented, think analytically, and enjoy diving into complex datasets and learning new tools.




For information

Start date : September
Location : Zurich - CH
Duration : permanent contract
Language : fluent in English, German is a plus

Title: GTM Director
Description:
Visium combines strategy, frontier AI, and deep regulated-industry expertise to build and deploy AI-native systems that reinvent how businesses operate - unlocking the value of AI at scale.

We're not a strategy firm that ships slide decks and walks away. Not a tech consultancy that builds what won't get adopted. Not a product company chasing problems it doesn't understand. We're all three at once: strategy, deep technical and integration expertise, and product, working as one. That combination is our flywheel. Consulting keeps us close to the hardest real-world problems. Products let us solve them at scale. Every engagement sharpens our products; every deployment makes the next engagement faster, deeper, and more certain.

We're obsessed with outcomes. While 95% of enterprise AI pilots never reach production, Visium is built to be the 5% delivering productised AI that compounds in value, not point solutions that deliver marginal gains. We are AI-native, not AI-adjacent. We don't bolt AI onto broken workflows; we redesign them with AI at the core.

Role

As a Business Developer Director in Life Sciences you will lead the business development activities and act as the first point of contact for customers, partners, new joiners and public authorities. At Visium, you'll play a pivotal role in driving growth and shaping enterprise-level AI & Data strategies within the Life Sciences sector. This is a high-impact position where your entrepreneurial mindset, strategic thinking, and strong relationship-building skills will directly influence enterprise-level digital transformation initiatives.

If you are motivated by results, enjoy leading teams, and want to shape the future of AI & Data strategies for top organizations, this is your opportunity to make a meaningful impact from day one.

Responsibilities

As a GTM Director, you will be responsible for:

Client Acquisition & Revenue Growth:


Lead the identification, engagement, and acquisition of new enterprise clients in specialty chemicals, pharmaceuticals, biotechnology organizations
Develop and execute strategic outbound initiatives, including executive-level networking, thought leadership engagements, and targeted outreach campaigns
Qualify and prioritize opportunities to align Visium's AI & Data capabilities with client business objectives


Solution Discovery & Client Alignment:


Conduct discovery meetings with C-level and senior stakeholders to understand critical business challenges, regulatory considerations, and innovation priorities in life sciences
Shape tailored proposals and presentations that demonstrate how Visium's solutions accelerate R&D, optimize operations, and improve patient outcomes. Collaborate with delivery teams to ensure successful execution of client projects and measurable impact
Maintain deep knowledge of the Life Sciences sector, including trends in AI, data analytics, digital R&D, regulatory requirements, and competitive landscape


Leadership & Team Development:


Mentor and develop junior business development and client engagement professionals, fostering commercial acumen and domain expertise in life sciences
Drive a culture of collaboration across sales, delivery, and marketing teams to maximize client impact and market presence
Define performance goals, track progress, and share insights to optimize team effectiveness


Market Expertise & Strategic Insights:


Maintain deep knowledge of AI & Data platforms, cloud infrastructure, data engineering best practices, and emerging technologies
Act as a trusted advisor to clients, providing thought leadership and strategic insights that shape long-term technology partnerships
Collaborate with marketing and delivery teams to design targeted campaigns, proposals, and solution offerings tailored to platform engineering challenges


Requirements

To succeed in this role, you bring a combination of expertise, experience, and skills including:


6 - 8 years of experience in business development, client engagement, or strategic consulting, ideally within the Life Sciences or Specialty Chemicals sector
Proven ability to drive enterprise client acquisition and revenue growth in complex, regulated industries
Strong leadership skills with experience mentoring teams and fostering commercial and technical expertise
Excellent communication, presentation, and relationship-building skills with executive-level stakeholders
Entrepreneurial mindset, strategic thinker, and highly results-oriented
Fluent in English, German/French will be considered as a plus.


Benefits

What we offer


A competitive compensation package
A yearly education budget to steep your learning curve
A yearly sport budget because a fit body leads to a fit mind
A flexible working culture because your work-life balance matters to us
A position that enables you to have an impact on 1'000s of people, and the whole company's growth
An international, knowledgeable, and passionate team with a strong collaborative mindset


Check our LinkedIn and website to learn more about us & don't hesitate to contact us if you have any questions.

Title: Laboratory Technician
Description:
Are you interested in working in an ambitious startup that combines molecular imaging with advanced data analytics to support treatment decisions for cancer patients?




Then here is your chance to contribute to the future of personalized medicine!




Job description:

We are looking for a highly motivated technician to join the lab and provide technical support in tissue sample preparation, antibody panel development, tissue section staining, and data acquisition based on imaging mass cytometry. In addition, we need support in overarching tasks such as lab quality assurance and administration.




Laboratory technician role

This position requires a driven and well-organized technician with demonstrated experience in working in a certified lab environment. The main tasks of the successful candidate will be:

Development, production, and QC of antibody panels for diagnostic purposes.
Development, production, and QC of reference samples for imaging mass cytometry.
Processing and analysis of patient samples for research projects and clinical studies using imaging mass cytometry.
Preliminary analysis of acquired data using established analysis pipelines.
Documentation of all lab activities electronically in the laboratory information system.
Contribution to administrative work like quality assurance, equipment maintenance, and inventory management.




Required qualifications

A degree as a biomedical analyst, biology laboratory technician, or equivalent
Proficient expertise in histology techniques and tissue imaging assays
Good knowledge of cell culture techniques
Expertise in standardizing laboratory workflows
Good proficiency in English (knowledge of German is a plus)

Preferred qualifications

At least 1 year of experience in a diagnostic lab or industry setting
Expertise in handling patient samples
Knowledge of single-cell and/or antibody-based assays
Basic expertise in laboratory quality management




About Navignostics:

At Navignostics, we develop cutting-edge data-driven personalized cancer diagnostics based on highly multiplexed tissue imaging to identify the drug (combination) that will optimally benefit each individual patient. We are a young and dynamic spin-off from the University of Zurich with a currently small but highly motivated and collaborative team of scientists with world-leading expertise and with the support of experienced business developers and clinical and regulatory advisors.




Work environment

Navignostics is located in Horgen Oberdorf, which is a 20 min train ride from the Zürich main station. You can expect a diversified, interesting, challenging and patient-oriented job in a future-oriented laboratory. We offer flexible working hours, a great team atmosphere and the possibility to contribute to shaping the company with us.




Diversity

Navignostics is committed to diversity in the workplace. We foster an open environment, healthy work atmosphere and flexible working arrangements.




How to apply

Apply by sending a cover letter, CV, and reference letters to Jana Fischer (jana.fischer@navignostics.ch). Applications will be considered until the position is filled. For further information, please visit our website www.navignostics.com.

Title: Intern Data Analyst (New Engines Department)
Description:
Zurich
Junior, Career start
Technical occupations (other)
Fulltime
Code: 9248


We are a world leading Engine MRO service provider, headquartered in Zurich, Switzerland, with over 90 years of operational experience. Our unwavering dedication to innovation, excellence and environmental responsibility propels us forward on our journey to becoming the leading, most customer-centric, and sustainable Engine focused MRO worldwide.

Working with an extensive network of partners and business development offices in Europe, the USA, Asia and the Middle East, we offer comprehensive, fully customized solutions for the Maintenance, Repair and Overhaul of aircraft engines, airframes and components sales business including impeccable technical support to over 500 customers worldwide. Our mission: We safely power your flight. As an Intern Data Analyst (New Engines Department) you will involve working on digitalization improvement projects and supporting the Operations Team and Management to work more efficiently. The intern will support in creating Power Apps Tool and Power BI Dashboard enabling live data input, update and visualization dashboard.

Your tasks


Create Power Apps Tool supporting Engine Shop Visit Management
Create Power BI Dashboard supporting Operations Management Report
Review and optimize use of SharePoint with New Engines organization


Your Profile


Currently enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate level degree in IT, Application Development
Computer Skills: Proficient with Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook). Power Apps, Power BI
Fluent in English or German (B1 / B2)
Strong interest in the Aerospace MRO industry and to work in a regulated environment

Title: Systems Biology and Microbiome Data Science Intern
Description:
Location: Lausanne, Switzerland

Work model: On site

Contract type / duration: Internship – 6 months

Target start date: September 1, 2026

Deadline for applications: July 2, 2026

Be a force for good and transform your potential into something extraordinary.

As a Systems Biology and Microbiome Data Science Intern at Nestlé Research, Nestlé Institute of Health Sciences, your mission is to help transform complex scientific information into structured, usable data that supports microbiome research and innovation in human health. You will work closely with scientists and partners across Nestlé to identify relevant information from detailed reports and sample-based studies, bring together data from different sources, and make it ready for future analysis and decision-making. This is your opportunity to make an impact while gaining hands on experience and mentorship to kick-start your career.

How you’ll make an impact as a Systems Biology and Microbiome Data Science Intern


Driving data mining and computational analysis of microbiome assays to uncover insights that support human health research.
Conducting literature reviews to identify scientific advancements and strengthen the foundation of ongoing research projects.
Building on existing analyses to generate new biological understanding of microbiome and host interactions.
Partnering with interdisciplinary experts to translate scientific questions into actionable research outputs.
Presenting findings through reports and scientific communication to help shape future research directions.


What Will Make You Successful


Current student or fresh graduate in computational science, systems biology, microbiology, biochemistry, biotechnology, or a related field.
Having confidence using programming tools such as R, Python, or similar technologies to explore and interpret complex scientific data.
Being curious about microbiome science, motivated by human health, and comfortable collaborating across disciplines and teams to turn ideas into insight.
Communicating fluently in English.
Demonstrating courage, collaboration, speed and continuous learning.


At Nestlé Research, we place people at the heart of everything we do.

Our benefits are designed to support your growth and wellbeing, so you can thrive at work and in life:


Flexible working options, including hybrid work possibilities (for office-based roles)
Learning and development opportunities to grow your career
Healthcare contribution and access to the Employee Assistance Program
Attractive employee discounts across various products and services


What To Expect From Our Hiring Process

We are committed to an inclusive and respectful hiring experience and are happy to provide reasonable accommodations if needed. Our workplaces are fully accessible and supported by a strong safety culture, with modern installations and procedures to ensure a safe environment for everyone.

The hiring process typically includes a video interview with Talent Acquisition, an interview with the hiring team, and a final discussion and offer. We review applications as they are received and close the role once a strong list of relevant candidates is identified, so we encourage you to apply early. All information is treated confidentially.

Ready to start your career journey with us?

Apply now and transform your potential into something extraordinary.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
We are looking for a Senior Data Analytics Engineer who can take ownership of tasks, deliver stable, incremental changes frequently, and ensure thorough testing to maintain high-quality standards.




This role tackles a range of complex software and data challenges, including data management, advanced analytics, and business intelligence. It is pivotal in implementing, maintaining, and supporting data pipelines using shared data platforms that exploit modern data technologies and software development practices




Tasks & Responsibilities:

• Participate and contribute to overall data architecture and design.

• Ensure effective design and development of system architectures through frequent product deliveries, employing effective governance methods for transparency and communication.

• Translate functional and technical requirements into detailed design.

• Develop required code for end-to-end data pipelines with optimal extraction, transformation, and loading of data from a wide variety of data sources. Incorporate business rules and functional capabilities, security, data retention requirements, etc.

• Work with stakeholders including business area sponsors, product owners, data architects, data engineers, project managers, and business analysts to assist them with their data-related technical issues and support their data needs.

• Remain up to date with industry standards and technological advancements that will improve the quality of your output.

• Interact with the business to identify, capture, and analyse business requirements.

• Assist and support proof of concepts as data technology evolves.




Must-Haves:

• Minimum of 7 years of work experience in modern data technologies as a Data Analytics Engineer or similar role.

• Experience in building data ingestion pipelines for data lake and data warehouse architecture. As well as strong experience in data modelling, design patterns, and building highly scalable applications.

• Hands-on development using open-source data technologies such as Hadoop, Hive, Spark, HBase, Kafka, Impala, ELK, etc., preferably with Cloudera.

• Experience working with relational databases such as Oracle, SQL Server, etc.

• Strong experience with Python

Entwicklung und Optimierung von Dashboards, Reports und KPI-Landschaften

Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Finance, Controlling, IT und operativen Stakeholdern
Unterstützung bei Forecasting-, Budgetierungs- und Planungsprozessen
Weiterentwicklung bestehender Datenstrukturen und Reporting-Lösungen
Mitarbeit bei Business-Intelligence- und Automatisierungsprojekten
Sicherstellung einer hohen Datenqualität und Transparenz über finanzielle Kennzahlen
Aktive Mitgestaltung einer datengetriebenen Entscheidungs- und Unternehmenskultur

Abgeschlossenes Studium in Finance, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Data Analytics, Betriebswirtschaft oder einer vergleichbaren Fachrichtung
Erste bis mehrjährige Erfahrung im Bereich Finance Analytics, Business Intelligence, Financial Planning & Analysis oder Data Analytics
Sehr gute Kenntnisse in SQL

Gute Kenntnisse in Python für Datenanalysen und Automatisierungen
Sehr gute Excel-Modelling-Skills

Strukturierte, analytische und lösungsorientierte Denkweise
Kommunikationsstärke und die Fähigkeit, komplexe Daten verständlich aufzubereiten


What's On Offer


Kombination aus Finance, Analytics und Technologie
Hohe Sichtbarkeit innerhalb des Unternehmens

Title: Junior Media Insights Consultant, Audience and Market Insights, PFP, 12 months, Geneva, Switzerland (Remote)
Description:
UNICEF works in over 190 countries and territories to save children’s lives, defend their rights, and help them fulfill their potential, from early childhood through adolescence.

At UNICEF, we are committed, passionate, and proud of what we do for as long as we are needed. Promoting the rights of every child is not just a job – it is a calling.

UNICEF is a place where careers are built. We offer our staff diverse opportunities for professional and personal development that will help them reinforce a sense of purpose while serving children and communities across the world. We welcome everyone who wants to belong and grow in a diverse and passionate culture, coupled with an attractive compensation and benefits package.

Visit our website to learn more about what we do at UNICEF.

How can you make a difference? 

This role is being established to strengthen the capacity of the Audience and Market Insights (AMI) unit by bringing in dedicated media analysis expertise. The Junior Media Insights Consultant will be primarily responsible for supporting monitoring, tracking, and analyzing media coverage and performance data, while also contributing to broader desk research activities that support the team's insight outputs. The role suits an early-career analyst with a background in media monitoring, content analysis, or communications research.


Media Analysis and Landscape Mapping: Support the team with media analysis that informs strategic decisions on partnership development and channel prioritization. This includes conducting periodic media landscape analyses — mapping the broader media environment to surface shifts in topic prominence, emerging narratives, evolving editorial agendas, and how key themes are being covered across outlets and competitor platforms. Outputs will range from regular analysis of snapshots to deeper landscape reports, formatted for internal distribution across the AMI team, PFP division, and wider stakeholders.
Desk Research Support: Assist the AMI team on an ad hoc basis with secondary research tasks — including landscape reviews, competitor scans, and audience background research — contributing to the synthesis and write-up of insight reports as workload requires.
Data and Insights Organization: Maintain up-to-date insight databases, dashboards, and internal repositories (e.g., SharePoint, Teams folders, Boolean searches’ archive) to ensure research materials are easily accessible and systematically organized.
Contribution to Research Projects and Insight Reports: Support end-to-end research activities, including questionnaire design, data synthesis, and the creation of final insight reports for internal distribution.


If you would like to know more about this consultancy, please review the complete Terms of Reference here: TMC0002477 ToR.pdf

To qualify as an advocate for every child you will have:

Minimum Requirements


Education: Master’s degree in cultural studies, communications, psychology, marketing, political studies, development studies, or a related field.
Work Experience:
Minimum of 6 months of professional experience in market research, data analysis, or audience insights (agency or client-side).
Familiarity with quantitative and qualitative research methods.
Experience with digital and analytics tools (e.g., Brandwatch, Talkwalker, Google Analytics).
Language requirement: Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English
Skills: Strong analytical and data visualization skills (Excel, PowerPoint, Power BI, Tableau, or similar).
Strong attention to detail, organization, and ability to manage multiple tasks.
Curious and constructive attitude, with a desire to learn and grow in the craft of research and insights.

Desirables


Method neutrality is an asset [quantitative, qualitative, and secondary research]
Knowledge of additional UN languages is an asset


For every Child, you demonstrate...

UNICEF’s Core Values of Care, Respect, Integrity, Trust and Accountability and Sustainability (CRITAS) underpin everything we do and how we do it. Get acquainted with Our Values Charter: UNICEF Values

UNICEF promotes and advocates for the protection of the rights of every child, everywhere, in everything it does and is mandated to support the realization of the rights of every child, including those most disadvantaged, and our global workforce must reflect the diversity of those children. The UNICEF family is committed to include everyone, irrespective of their race/ethnicity, disability, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, nationality, socio-economic background, minority, or any other status.

UNICEF encourages applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of gender, nationality, religious or ethnic backgrounds, and from people with disabilities, including neurodivergence. We offer reasonable accommodation for persons with disabilities. throughout the recruitment process. If you require any accommodation, please submit your request through the accessibility email button on the UNICEF Careers webpage Accessibility | UNICEF. Should you be shortlisted, please get in touch with the recruiter directly to share further details, enabling us to make the necessary arrangements in advance.

UNICEF does not hire candidates who are married to children (persons under 18). UNICEF has a zero-tolerance policy on conduct that is incompatible with the aims and objectives of the United Nations and UNICEF, including sexual exploitation and abuse, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and discrimination based on gender, nationality, age, race, sexual orientation, religious or ethnic background or disabilities. UNICEF is committed to promote the protection and safeguarding of all children. All selected candidates will, therefore, undergo rigorous reference and background checks, and will be expected to adhere to these standards and principles. Background checks will include the verification of academic credential(s) and employment history. Selected candidates may be required to provide additional information to conduct a background check, and selected candidates with disabilities may be requested to submit supporting documentation in relation to their disability confidentially.

Qualified candidates are invited to submit the following documents via the online recruitment portal, TMS (Talent Management System):


An up-to-date TMS profile and curriculum vitae (CV)
Cover letter
A separate financial proposal using this template only TMC0002477 Financial proposal template.docx


Remarks: If the TOR or financial proposal documents are not visible on certain recruitment platforms, please visit our official page Vacancies | UNICEF Careers.

UNICEF does not charge a processing fee at any stage of its recruitment, selection, and hiring processes (i.e., application stage, interview stage, validation stage, or appointment and training). UNICEF will not ask for applicants’ bank account information.

All UNICEF positions are advertised, and only shortlisted candidates will be contacted and advance to the next stage of the selection process.

Additional information about working for UNICEF can be found here.

HTML:
UNICEF works in over 190 countries and territories to save children’s lives, defend their rights, and help them fulfill their potential, from early childhood through adolescence.

At UNICEF, we are committed, passionate, and proud of what we do for as long as we are needed. Promoting the rights of every child is not just a job – it is a calling.

UNICEF is a place where careers are built. We offer our staff diverse opportunities for professional and personal development that will help them reinforce a sense of purpose while serving children and communities across the world. We welcome everyone who wants to belong and grow in a diverse and passionate culture, coupled with an attractive compensation and benefits package.

Visit our website to learn more about what we do at UNICEF.

How can you make a difference? 

This role is being established to strengthen the capacity of the Audience and Market Insights (AMI) unit by bringing in dedicated media analysis expertise. The Junior Media Insights Consultant will be primarily responsible for supporting monitoring, tracking, and analyzing media coverage and performance data, while also contributing to broader desk research activities that support the team's insight outputs. The role suits an early-career analyst with a background in media monitoring, content analysis, or communications research.

  • Media Analysis and Landscape Mapping: Support the team with media analysis that informs strategic decisions on partnership development and channel prioritization. This includes conducting periodic media landscape analyses — mapping the broader media environment to surface shifts in topic prominence, emerging narratives, evolving editorial agendas, and how key themes are being covered across outlets and competitor platforms. Outputs will range from regular analysis of snapshots to deeper landscape reports, formatted for internal distribution across the AMI team, PFP division, and wider stakeholders.
  • Desk Research Support: Assist the AMI team on an ad hoc basis with secondary research tasks — including landscape reviews, competitor scans, and audience background research — contributing to the synthesis and write-up of insight reports as workload requires.
  • Data and Insights Organization: Maintain up-to-date insight databases, dashboards, and internal repositories (e.g., SharePoint, Teams folders, Boolean searches’ archive) to ensure research materials are easily accessible and systematically organized.
  • Contribution to Research Projects and Insight Reports: Support end-to-end research activities, including questionnaire design, data synthesis, and the creation of final insight reports for internal distribution.

If you would like to know more about this consultancy, please review the complete Terms of Reference here: TMC0002477 ToR.pdf

To qualify as an advocate for every child you will have:

Minimum Requirements

  • Education: Master’s degree in cultural studies, communications, psychology, marketing, political studies, development studies, or a related field.
  • Work Experience:
    • Minimum of 6 months of professional experience in market research, data analysis, or audience insights (agency or client-side).
    • Familiarity with quantitative and qualitative research methods.
    • Experience with digital and analytics tools (e.g., Brandwatch, Talkwalker, Google Analytics).
  • Language requirement: Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English
  • Skills: Strong analytical and data visualization skills (Excel, PowerPoint, Power BI, Tableau, or similar).
  • Strong attention to detail, organization, and ability to manage multiple tasks.
  • Curious and constructive attitude, with a desire to learn and grow in the craft of research and insights.
Desirables

  • Method neutrality is an asset [quantitative, qualitative, and secondary research]
  • Knowledge of additional UN languages is an asset

Title: Business Intelligence I
Description:
Sobre o Bradesco

O Bradesco é um dos maiores grupos financeiros do Brasil, com uma história marcada pelo pioneirismo e inovação. Por meio da nossa carteira amplamente diversificada de produtos financeiros, serviços bancários e de seguros contribuímos com a realização das pessoas e o crescimento sustentável de empresas e sociedade. Venha fazer parte do nosso ecossistema financeiro e impactar a experiência de milhões de pessoas!

No Bradesco valorizamos a diversidade em todas as suas formas. Por isso, as nossas vagas são abertas a todas as pessoas, independentemente de gênero, raça, orientação sexual, deficiência, idade ou qualquer outra característica.

Saiba mais em https://banco.bradesco/html/classic/sobre/index.shtm

Responsabilidades e atribuições

O que é o nosso time de Segurança Corporativa?

Somos dedicados a proteger os ativos, informações e nossos funcionários. Trabalhamos proativamente para identificar e mitigar riscos, respondendo rapidamente a incidentes e promovendo uma cultura de segurança em toda a organização. Nosso compromisso é com a integridade, a confidencialidade e a disponibilidade dos recursos corporativos, assegurando a continuidade dos negócios e a confiança de nossos clientes.

Modelo de trabalho : Híbrido - 2x Presencial

Unidade: São Paulo (SP) ou Uberlandia (SP)

Como será seu dia a dia?

Sendo Business Intelligence I, atuará na gerência Processos e Diagnósticos e suas principais atividades serão:



Realizar análises de dados para identificação e prevenção de fraudes.
Apoiar o monitoramento de transações e comportamentos suspeitos.
Investigar padrões, desvios e possíveis vulnerabilidades.
Apoiar a construção e evolução de regras preventivas.
Elaborar análises e diagnósticos para suporte à tomada de decisão.
Atuar na melhoria contínua dos processos de monitoramento.
Apoiar iniciativas de mitigação de riscos relacionados a produtos financeiros.
Trabalhar em parceria com áreas internas para evolução dos processos de prevenção.



Requisitos e Qualificações

O que você precisa ter ou saber?



Perfil analítico com interesse em dados.
Ensino superior em andamento ou concluído recentemente com ênfase em dados.
Conhecimento em Excel.
Noções de lógica de programação.
Conhecimentos básicos em estatística.
Interesse e/ou experiência em prevenção à fraude e análise investigativa.
Boa capacidade de interpretação e análise de dados.
Facilidade de aprendizado e adaptação a novos contextos.



Será um diferencial se você tiver:



Conhecimento em Python ou outra linguagem de programação.
Experiência prévia (inclusive estágio) com dados ou fraude.
Vivência em bancos, fintechs ou meios de pagamento.
Conhecimento em ferramentas como SAS, Databricks ou Teradata.



O que nós oferecemos

No Bradesco valorizamos a saúde e bem-estar, oferecendo um extenso portfólio de benefícios a todas nossas pessoas funcionárias:



PLR ou Bônus: Conforme a elegibilidade de cargo*
Convênio Médico
Convênio Odontológico
Seguro de Vida
Vale Alimentação
Vale Refeição
13º Cesta Alimentação
Total Pass
Vale Transporte (adesão opcional)
Descontos em produtos e serviços em empresas parceiras
Previdência Privada (adesão opcional, com participação financeira da Organização Bradesco)
Viva Bem Bradesco: programa de saúde, bem-estar e qualidade de vida
Unibrad: Universidade Corporativa Bradesco
Isenção de Tarifas: condições especiais em diversos produtos e serviços
Auxílio Creche ou Babá
Licença Paternidade estendida de 20 dias
Licença Maternidade de 180 dias, acompanhamento assistencial da gestação até o pós-parto

Title: Analista de Dados Pleno | Business Intelligence
Description:
About The Team

Sobre a Shô

Aqui na Shô, acreditamos que cada membro de nossa equipe é fundamental para o nosso sucesso, e que diversidade de perspectivas e vivências são essenciais para movimentar nosso negócio e impulsionar o nosso propósito de mudar o mundo para melhor.

Fazer parte do nosso time é a oportunidade de se desenvolver dentro de uma cultura de muita agilidade. Se você ama assumir grandes responsabilidades e fazer o que nunca foi feito, a Shô é seu lugar. Aqui você terá grandes desafios, trabalhará em colaboração com um time diverso e contribuirá para o futuro do e-commerce no Brasil, criamos impacto positivo na vida de milhões de pessoas.

Modelo de Trabalho

Adotamos um modelo de trabalho híbrido para posições administrativas alocadas no nossos escritórios.

Qual será a sua missão

A missão desta posição é elevar o desempenho de Customer Service (CS) por meio do monitoramento contínuo dos principais indicadores operacionais, identificando as oportunidades de melhoria mais críticas e transformando-as em iniciativas de alto impacto.

Atuará com projetos multifuncionais que apoiam novos objetivos de negócio, aprimora os processos de CS de ponta a ponta, antecipa riscos e implementa soluções escaláveis. Também é responsável pela configuração e otimização do chatbot de CS, ampliando a automação e melhorando a experiência do cliente.

Atuando com alto grau de autonomia, esta função garante a execução disciplinada dos projetos, a comunicação clara com a liderança sênior e a entrega consistente de resultados mensuráveis em múltiplas frentes de trabalho simultâneas.

Job Description

Como será o seu dia a dia


Atuar como parceiro estratégico das diversas áreas de negócio, auxiliando no planejamento, definição de estratégias e crescimento da Shopee por meio da tomada de decisão orientada por dados.
Servir como consultor interno, traduzindo conceitos e terminologias complexas em insights claros e acionáveis para os stakeholders internos.
Coletar, tratar, limpar e analisar grandes volumes de dados para apoiar decisões de negócio.
Desenvolver e implementar dashboards para monitoramento dos principais indicadores de desempenho.
Realizar análises ad hoc para apoiar tomadas de decisão e projetos especiais.


Requirements

O que você precisa para assumir o desafio


Graduação ou pós-graduação (bacharelado ou mestrado) em áreas quantitativas ou áreas correlatas.
Experiência com pelo menos uma linguagem de consulta de dados (ex.: SQL).
Familiaridade com ferramentas de Business Intelligence (BI), como Tableau, Power BI ou Looker, é altamente desejável.
Conhecimento em linguagens de análise avançada, como Python ou R, será considerado um diferencial.
Experiência em atuação com múltiplos stakeholders de diferentes áreas da organização.
Inglês intermediário para comunicação com stakeholders internos.
Excelentes habilidades interpessoais e de comunicação.
Perfil proativo, com iniciativa e atitude orientada à resolução de problemas.


Benefícios


Assistência médica e odontológica, estendendo-se aos dependentes e seguro de vida coletivo;
Parceira com Gympass e TotalPass;
Vale Refeição/Alimentação flexível;
Vale Transporte, Vale Mobilidade ou Estacionamento no local;
Benefício flexível;
Zenklub e Conexa;
Assistência Pet;
Benefícios exclusivos para quem tem filhos;
Bônus anual discricionário, atrelado à performance.


Diversidade e Inclusão

Valorizamos talentos diversos e incentivamos a candidatura de pessoas de todas as origens, sem distinção de raça, gênero, orientação sexual, deficiência, idade ou qualquer outra característica. Trabalhamos continuamente para promover um ambiente acolhedor, com iniciativas e grupos de afinidade que fortalecem uma cultura cada vez mais inclusiva.

Queremos ser o lugar onde você possa ser quem é, se desenvolver com autonomia e contribuir para transformar o futuro ao nosso lado. É a partir dessa pluralidade que geramos impacto e construímos o futuro do e-commerce no Brasil.

O QUE TE FAZ ÚNICO, NOS FAZ MELHORES

E NOSSO IMPACTO DEIXA MARCA!

N-A40

Title: Analista de Dados Jr
HTML:
Company Description

A Serasa Experian é a primeira e a maior Datatech do Brasil. Líder em soluções de inteligência para análise de riscos e oportunidades, com foco nas jornadas de crédito, autenticação e prevenção à fraude. Com tecnologia de ponta, inovação e os melhores talentos, transforma a incerteza do risco na melhor decisão, ajudando pessoas a realizarem seus sonhos e empresas de todos os portes e segmentos a prosperarem.

Temos 22.000 pessoas operando em 32 países e a cada dia estamos investindo em novas tecnologias, profissionais talentosos e inovação para ajudar todos os clientes a maximizarem cada oportunidade. Com sede corporativa em Dublin, Irlanda, a Experian está listada na Bolsa de Valores de Londres (EXPN) e compõe o índice FTSE 100.

Job Description

Responsabilidades Principais:

  • Levantamento de requisitos junto aos times de negócio;
  • Planejamento e desenvolvimento de pipelines de carga de dados;
  • Criação de relatórios analíticos e Dashboards;
  • Acompanhamento e resolução de Incidentes.

Qualifications

Bacharelado Ciência da Computação, Engenharia ou área relacionada;

Inglês Técnico.

Imprescindível

  • Conhecimento em linguagem de programação SQL ou similares para consultas em bases de dados.
  • Conhecimento em ferramentas de visualização de dados como Tableau, PowerBI ou similares;
  • Conhecimento em construção de gráficos e Dashboards;
  • Conhecimento em modelagem e relacionamento entre tabelas;
  • Conhecimento básico em programação.

Desejável

  • Experiencia com ferramentas de ETL (IBM DataStage, Azure DataFactory, MS Integration Services, ou afins..);
  • Experiencia na criação de Dashboards, com capacidade de coletar, organizar, analisar e disseminar grandes quantidades de informações com atenção aos detalhes e precisão;
  • Conhecimento em BigData e tratamento de grandes volumes;
  • Conhecimento em metodologias de Business Intelligence.

Additional Information

A Serasa Experian é muito mais do que você imagina. Com o propósito de criar um futuro melhor, ampliando oportunidades para pessoas e empresas, no Brasil somos mais de 4 mil pessoas que atuam em diversos times e especialidades. Aqui, cada conhecimento e diversidade se complementa e você pode trabalhar no que mais ama, estamos comprometidos a construir uma cultura inclusiva e um ambiente no qual pessoas possam equilibrar a carreira com seus compromissos e interesses pessoais, prezando pelo bem-estar.

A gente se dedica muito em ser uma das melhores e mais inovadoras empresas para se trabalhar do país, possibilitando experiências e carreiras incríveis para nossas pessoas. Title: ANALISTA DE DADOS JR
HTML:
Principais Atividades

  • Realizar análises de dados e monitoramento dos indicadores de qualidade das operações logísticas;

  • 🚚 First Mile (Coleta)

Requisitos

  • Ensino Superior cursando ou completo em Administração, Logística, Engenharia, Estatística, Tecnologia da Informação ou áreas correlatas;

  • Excel (intermediário/avançado);
  • Word;
  • Facilidade com análise e interpretação de dados;
  • Conhecimento em indicadores operacionais e de qualidade;
  • Perfil analítico e atenção aos detalhes.
  • Experiência em operações logísticas, transportes ou e-commerce;

Title: Analista de Dados
HTML:
Sobre a Área

A área de Analytics do Banco tem papel central na tomada de decisão das áreas de negócio atuadas, transformando dados em insights acionáveis que impactam diretamente os resultados do negócio. Atuamos apoiando decisões do dia a dia e iniciativas estruturais de crescimento, eficiência e gestão de risco.

Nosso time é responsável por analisar dados, identificar padrões, construir diagnósticos e gerar recomendações baseadas em evidências, utilizando técnicas de estatística, modelagem analítica, visualização de dados e experimentação, entendendo profundamente seus desafios para propor soluções analíticas de alto impacto.

No Seu Dia a Dia

  • Realizar estudos e análises avançadas utilizando técnicas e ferramentas de Big Data;
  • Manter e otimizar ETLs garantindo a integridade e disponibilidade dos dados;
  • Desenvolver dashboards claros e intuitivos, facilitando a tomada de decisões estratégicas.

Esperamos De Você

  • Graduação completa em Engenharia, Computação, Matemática ou áreas correlatas;
  • Disponibilidade para atuar no modelo presencial (5x por semana) no escritório de São Paulo (Faria Lima);;
  • Experiência sólida em Python e SQL;
  • Conhecimento em soluções e serviços AWS;
  • Domínio de ferramentas de visualização de dados, especialmente PowerBI;
  • Excelentes habilidades analíticas, capacidade de resolução de problemas e curiosidade intelectual;
  • Inglês avançado é um diferencial;
  • Conhecimento prévio do mercado financeiro (investimentos, crédito, banking, etc.) ou plataformas digitais é um diferencial;
  • Conhecimento e experiência prática em inferência estatística é um diferencial.

Benefícios

  • Participação nos Lucros e Resultados (PLR);
  • Auxílio Alimentação e Refeição;
  • Plano Médico;
  • Plano Odontológico;
  • Auxílio Creche/Babá;
  • Vale Transporte;
  • WellHub;
  • TotalPass;
  • Programa de Apoio Pessoal (EAP);
  • Planos por adesão como Previdência Privada e Seguro de Vida;
  • Desconto em Farmácia;
  • Programa de Nutrição;
  • Programa de Gestantes;
  • Licença Maternidade e Paternidade Estendida – empresa Cidadã.

Title: ANALISTA DE DADOS PL
Description:
Quer fazer parte da nossa turma?

São mais 30 anos recheados de aprendizados e doces conquistas. A Cultura vivenciada na empresa traduz a essência de quem somos e onde queremos chegar.

Vivemos para Juntos tocar a vida das pessoas compartilhando momentos especiais.

Ingredientes como Foco, Atitude, União, Alegria e muita Paixão por realizar nos impulsiona a fazer sempre o melhor, transformando o Ordinário em Extraordinário.

A nossa turma é formada por pessoas que fazem da Cacau show um lugar especial para se trabalhar, onde todos são tratados com igualdade, independentemente de gênero, raça, idade, origem ou orientação sexual, sendo nosso processo baseado em meritocracia.

Na Cacau todos são Show!!!

Se você é apaixonado pelo que faz, possui senso de dono e muito “gás” para trabalhar, essa vaga é a sua cara!!!

Responsabilidades e atribuições


Desenvolver e manter pipelines de transformação de dados (ETL/ELT) no Databricks (utilizando PySpark ou Spark SQL).
Modelar dados no ambiente de Lakehouse/Delta Lake dentro da nuvem Azure, garantindo escalabilidade e performance.
Aplicar boas práticas de engenharia de software aos dados (versionamento com Git, testes de qualidade e CI/CD).
Garantir a governança, documentação e confiabilidade das tabelas core da empresa.
Business Intelligence (BI e Visualização) Desenvolver, otimizar e manter dashboards e relatórios interativos no Power BI.
Administrar a capacidade do Power BI (Premium/Fabric), além de gerenciar workspaces, governança de acessos e gateways.
Traduzir requisitos de negócio em métricas claras e KPIs monitoráveis, garantindo usabilidade e desempenho dos painéis.
Análises de Dados (Insights e Estratégia)
Realizar análises exploratórias e diagnósticas para responder a perguntas complexas de negócio.
Identificar tendências, anomalias e oportunidades de melhoria por meio de métodos estatísticos.
Traduzir resultados analíticos em recomendações acionáveis para stakeholders não técnicos.



Requisitos e qualificações


Experiência nas áreas de Análise de Dados, BI ou Analytics Engineering.
Ecossistema Microsoft / Azure: Experiência prática com serviços de dados da Azure (ex: Azure Data Factory, ADLS).
Databricks: Vivência sólida no desenvolvimento de rotinas de dados em Databricks (PySpark e SQL).
Power BI: Domínio avançado de Power BI (DAX, Power Query, Modelagem Tabular) e experiência com a administração do serviço/capacidade.
Linguagens e Ferramentas: Domínio avançado de SQL (obrigatório), proficiência em Python, e versionamento de código (Git).
Soft Skills: Capacidade analítica aguçada, excelente comunicação para traduzir requisitos técnicos para a área de negócios e autonomia.



Informações adicionais


Assistência Médica;
Assistência Odontológica;
Seguro de Vida;
Vale transporte/Fretado ou Estacionamento na empresa;
Cesta Básica ou Vale alimentação;
Restaurante na empresa;
Academia e Salão de Beleza na empresa;
Desconto nos produtos Cacau Show (Matriz);
PPR.

Title: Analista de Operações Jr (Foco em Dados/Indicadores) - Temporário
Description:
Descrição

Para o match perfeito acontecer precisa de PESSOAS, com as mais diversas experiências e pontos de vista, para que possamos continuar evoluindo enquanto empresa.

Por isso, aqui na SulAmérica, incentivamos a candidatura de pessoas de todas as raças/etnias, orientações sexuais, identidades de gênero, gerações, com ou sem deficiência, para todas as nossas vagas. Afinal, as nossas diferenças é o que nos complementam!

#LGBTI+ #PessoasNegras #PcD #50+ #Gerações #PessoasComDeficiência

Principais Desafios

Perfil estratégico e altamente organizado para atuar na intersecção entre a operação e inteligência de dados. O objetivo principal desta posição é transformar o volume de dados da nossa "esteira" de serviços médicos em indicadores claros, garantindo que gargalos sejam identificados rapidamente para evitar a desistência de propostas e otimizar a tomada de decisão.


Gestão de Indicadores: Extrair dados de planilhas e sistemas para compilar indicadores diários de performance.
Monitoramento da Esteira: Acompanhar o fluxo de propostas (aceitas vs. recusadas) e identificar pontos de retenção que possam gerar desistências.
Interface Estratégica: Atuar junto à Central de Controle (NUADS) e times de indicadores para garantir que os relatórios ofereçam um norte estratégico à gestão.
Análise Crítica: Realizar intersecções de números, identificar tendências e sugerir melhorias operacionais baseadas em dados.
Visualização de Dados: Construir gráficos e dashboards que facilitem a leitura ágil dos resultados.


Requisitos Técnicos (Hard Skills)


Excel Intermediário/Avançado: Domínio indispensável de PROCV, SOMASE, Tabelas Dinâmicas, Funções de Média/Mediana e construção de Gráficos/Dashboards.
Experiência: Vivência em construção de base de dados e controle de indicadores.
Formação: Superior completo em Administração, Ciências Contábeis, Economia, Engenharias, Estatística ou Atuária (áreas correlatas).


Modalidade: 3x Presencial, 2x Home Office

Local de trabalho: Alameda Santos (Próximo ao Metrô Consolação)

Contratação: Temporário - (com grandes chances de efetivação)

Elaborar e revisar documentos técnicos que dão suporte aos processos de registro e pós registro de produtos;

Inglês técnico;
Vivência prévia em laboratório, especialmente em atividades relacionadas a análises e metodologias analíticas;
Conhecimento do segmento veterinário.

Title: Analista de Dados (Cultura de Dados) Junior (Senior Specialist, Administrative Support)
Description:
ID da Requisição: 71368

Sobre a Whirlpool Corporation

Whirlpool Corporation (NYSE: WHR) Ser a melhor empresa de eletrodomésticos, melhorando continuamente a vida em casa. Como a única grande fabricante de eletrodomésticos para cozinha e lavanderia dos EUA, a empresa está impulsionando inovações significativas para atender às crescentes necessidades dos consumidores, por meio do portfólio de marcas icônicas, incluindo Whirlpool, KitchenAid, JennAir, Maytag, Amana, Brastemp, Consul e InSinkErator. Em 2024, a empresa registrou aproximadamente US$17 bilhões em vendas anuais – quase 90% delas nas Américas – 44.000 colaboradores e 40 centros de pesquisa de tecnologia e manufatura. Informações adicionais sobre a empresa podem ser encontradas em WhirlpoolCorp.com.

A equipe da qual você fará parte

A equipe de Analytics & Data Culture é uma área central no crescimento da Whirlpool, atuando como a ponte entre as áreas de negócio e as soluções de dados. Somos responsáveis por todo o ciclo de vida dos projetos de analytics, atuando junto a outros times de TI para guiar e garantir a qualidade das entregas. Nosso objetivo é não apenas entregar produtos de dados, mas também capacitar toda a companhia a ser orientada a dados.

Resumo desta função

Buscamos um Analista de Dados Júnior para apoiar a interface entre as necessidades de negócio e o desenvolvimento técnico de produtos de dados. Esta função é essencial para ajudar a equipe a entender e traduzir soluções de dados, e atuará de perto com o time para apoiar a disseminação da cultura analítica na companhia.

Suas responsabilidades incluirão

Apoiar o entendimento e a tradução das necessidades de negócio para as soluções de analytics.

Participar do processo de "discovery" para novos projetos e produtos de dados junto aos analistas mais seniores.

Auxiliar a documentar as regras técnicas e de negócio das soluções desenvolvidas.

Ajudar na disseminação da cultura analítica, auxiliando em treinamentos sobre as ferramentas de analytics.

Acompanhar o uso e a aderência das ferramentas de analytics sob a orientação da equipe.

Desenvolver visões de dados e dashboards básicos, seguindo os padrões de arquitetura estabelecidos.

Requisitos mínimos


Experiência prática ou académica com bases de dados e consultas em SQL;
Conhecimento básico/intermediário em ferramentas de visualização, como Tableau (Desktop/Cloud/Prep) ou Looker;
Inglês em nível intermediário.


Habilidades e experiências desejáveis

Noções básicas de linguagens de programação voltadas para dados, como Python.

Noções básicas ou interesse em conceitos de engenharia de dados no Google Cloud Platform (GCP) (ex: BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Cloud Composer/Airflow).

Noções teóricas sobre modelagem de dados (ex: conceitos de Star Schema).

Conhecimento em Excel.

Perfil proativo, com vontade de aprender e apoiar na identificação e resolução de problemas.

O que oferecemos


Um dia de home office na semana;
Horário flexível
"No dresscode".
Wellhub
Vale-Transporte, Fretados ou Estacionamento na empresa;
Vale Alimentação;
Refeição no local;
Benefícios como empréstimo consignado ou social, plano de saúde, odontológico, seguro de vida e previdência privada compatíveis com o mercado;
Programa de Apoio ao Empregado, contando com atendimento 24hrs com profissionais jurídicos, sociais, financeiros, assistentes sociais e Psicólogos;
Auxílio Creche ou Berçário na empresa;
Serviços disponíveis na unidade: salão de beleza e estética, agência bancária interna, lavanderia, lanchonete, restaurante e sala de lactação;
Duas semanas de trabalho remoto em qualquer lugar;
Após 5 anos de empresa, colaboradores elegíveis podem tirar quatro semanas de licença remunerada;
Desconto em produtos pelo Compra Certa;
Licença maternidade/paternidade estendida. Saiba mais em https://www.whirlpoolcareers.com/trabalhe-conosco-no-brasil/


Informações adicionais

Essa posição está localizada em São Paulo, Brasil, a cerca de 15min de distância da estação Sacomâ da Linha Verde. Onde se concentra nosso Centro de Distribuição de Peças de Reposição e a Sede Administrativa dos seguintes departamentos:Compra Certa (e-commerce), Tecnologia, Serviços de Atendimento ao Consumidor, Logística, Controladoria, Tesouraria, Tributário, Jurídico, Recursos Humanos, Comunicação, Suprimentos e Digital.

Sobre a Whirlpool Corporation

Whirlpool Corporation (NYSE: WHR) Ser a melhor empresa de eletrodomésticos, melhorando continuamente a vida em casa. Como a única grande fabricante de eletrodomésticos para cozinha e lavanderia dos EUA, a empresa está impulsionando inovações significativas para atender às crescentes necessidades dos consumidores, por meio do portfólio de marcas icônicas, incluindo Whirlpool, KitchenAid, JennAir, Maytag, Amana, Brastemp, Consul e InSinkErator. Em 2024, a empresa registrou aproximadamente US$17 bilhões em vendas anuais – quase 90% delas nas Américas – 44.000 colaboradores e 40 centros de pesquisa de tecnologia e manufatura. Informações adicionais sobre a empresa podem ser encontradas em WhirlpoolCorp.com.

A equipe da qual você fará parte

A equipe de Analytics & Data Culture é uma área central no crescimento da Whirlpool, atuando como a ponte entre as áreas de negócio e as soluções de dados. Somos responsáveis por todo o ciclo de vida dos projetos de analytics, atuando junto a outros times de TI para guiar e garantir a qualidade das entregas. Nosso objetivo é não apenas entregar produtos de dados, mas também capacitar toda a companhia a ser orientada a dados.

Resumo desta função

Buscamos um Analista de Dados Júnior para apoiar a interface entre as necessidades de negócio e o desenvolvimento técnico de produtos de dados. Esta função é essencial para ajudar a equipe a entender e traduzir soluções de dados, e atuará de perto com o time para apoiar a disseminação da cultura analítica na companhia.

Suas responsabilidades incluirão

Apoiar o entendimento e a tradução das necessidades de negócio para as soluções de analytics.

Participar do processo de "discovery" para novos projetos e produtos de dados junto aos analistas mais seniores.

Auxiliar a documentar as regras técnicas e de negócio das soluções desenvolvidas.

Ajudar na disseminação da cultura analítica, auxiliando em treinamentos sobre as ferramentas de analytics.

Acompanhar o uso e a aderência das ferramentas de analytics sob a orientação da equipe.

Desenvolver visões de dados e dashboards básicos, seguindo os padrões de arquitetura estabelecidos.

Requisitos mínimos

  • Experiência prática ou académica com bases de dados e consultas em SQL;
  • Conhecimento básico/intermediário em ferramentas de visualização, como Tableau (Desktop/Cloud/Prep) ou Looker;
  • Inglês em nível intermediário.

Habilidades e experiências desejáveis

Noções básicas de linguagens de programação voltadas para dados, como Python.

Noções básicas ou interesse em conceitos de engenharia de dados no Google Cloud Platform (GCP) (ex: BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Cloud Composer/Airflow).

Noções teóricas sobre modelagem de dados (ex: conceitos de Star Schema).

Conhecimento em Excel.

Perfil proativo, com vontade de aprender e apoiar na identificação e resolução de problemas.

O que oferecemos

  • Um dia de home office na semana;
  • Horário flexível
  • "No dresscode".
  • Wellhub
  • Vale-Transporte, Fretados ou Estacionamento na empresa;
  • Vale Alimentação;
  • Refeição no local;
  • Benefícios como empréstimo consignado ou social, plano de saúde, odontológico, seguro de vida e previdência privada compatíveis com o mercado;
  • Programa de Apoio ao Empregado, contando com atendimento 24hrs com profissionais jurídicos, sociais, financeiros, assistentes sociais e Psicólogos;
  • Auxílio Creche ou Berçário na empresa;
  • Serviços disponíveis na unidade: salão de beleza e estética, agência bancária interna, lavanderia, lanchonete, restaurante e sala de lactação;
  • Duas semanas de trabalho remoto em qualquer lugar;
  • Após 5 anos de empresa, colaboradores elegíveis podem tirar quatro semanas de licença remunerada;
  • Desconto em produtos pelo Compra Certa;
  • Licença maternidade/paternidade estendida. Saiba mais em https://www.whirlpoolcareers.com/trabalhe-conosco-no-brasil/

Informações adicionais

Essa posição está localizada em São Paulo, Brasil, a cerca de 15min de distância da estação Sacomâ da Linha Verde. Onde se concentra nosso Centro de Distribuição de Peças de Reposição e a Sede Administrativa dos seguintes departamentos:Compra Certa (e-commerce), Tecnologia, Serviços de Atendimento ao Consumidor, Logística, Controladoria, Tesouraria, Tributário, Jurídico, Recursos Humanos, Comunicação, Suprimentos e Digital.
