La Responsabilità etica e sociale: ci dedichiamo costantemente a realizzare progettualità legate alla sostenibilità sociale, perché riteniamo che ognuno di noi può dare il proprio contributo lavorando con integrità, trasparenza ed onestà in tutte le azioni

SU DI NOI

La Fondazione Policlinico Universitario Agostino Gemelli IRCCS si conferma come il luogo in cui competenze scientifiche e tecniche, sensibilità umana, etica e valori cattolici diventano un impegno concreto per cure eccellenti e accessibili all’intera comunità. Il valore che viene quotidianamente posto al centro dell’operare è, fin dalle origini, la cura della persona malata, nel rispetto integrale della sua dignità, dei suoi bisogni, della sua sofferenza e della sua speranza.

LO SAPEVI CHE?

Nel 2018 è stato riconosciuto il carattere scientifico del Policlinico Gemelli per le discipline di “Medicina Personalizzata” e di “Biotecnologie innovative”, diventando un Istituto di Ricovero e Cura a Carattere Scientifico (IRCCS).

Nel maggio 2024, la Fondazione ha conseguito il rinnovo dell’accreditamento triennale come Academic Medical Center da parte della Joint Commission International (JCI), leader mondiale nell’accreditamento in ambito sanitario.

La Fondazione, anche per l’anno 2024, ha ottenuto la conferma (per l’ottavo anno consecutivo) della certificazione Top Employers Italia, prestigioso riconoscimento rilasciato alle Organizzazioni che stabiliscono e mantengono elevati standard nelle pratiche di gestione delle risorse umane, nonché la certificazione “Italy’s Best Employers 2024”.

La Fondazione ha ottenuto il Sigillo Ufficiale “Leader in Diversità e Inclusione 2025”, come struttura che si è distinta in Italia per il proprio operato in termini di diversità e inclusione (D&I) e la Certificazione per la Parità di Genere.

VUOI UNIRTI A NOI?

I PROSSIMI STEPS

Il recruiting della Fondazione Policlinico Universitario Agostino Gemelli IRCCS avviene tramite diversi canali in relazione alla tipologia di profilo ricercato con l’obiettivo di garantire la migliore candidate experience possibile.

Una volta scelta la posizione, basta seguire pochi e semplici passi per inoltrare la propria candidatura. Se in linea con il profilo, si avrà la possibilità di accedere ad un colloquio orale, motivazionale e tecnico.

Le candidature ricevute saranno considerate in base al match con la posizione ricercata, indipendentemente da nazionalità, origine etnica, genere, orientamento sessuale, età, religione etc.

Il processo di selezione è strutturato nel rispetto dei principi di equità, assicurando la conformità con le leggi in materia di pari opportunità e la neutralità rispetto al genere.

Title: Data science applications engineer
Description:
Data science applications engineer

Job Requisition ID: 20639

Date Posted: 12 June 2026

Closing Date: 3 July 2026 23:59 CET/CEST

Publication: Internal & External

Type of Appointment: Fixed-Term

Directorate: Earth Observation Programmes

Workplace:

Frascati, IT

Grade Band: A2 - A4

This is a fixed-term appointment with an initial duration of 4 years. Depending upon performance and organisational needs, the appointment may be extended up to a possible maximum duration of 8 years.

Location

ESRIN, Frascati, Italy

Description

The EO Applications Engineer will report to the Head of the Enterprise Section in the Green Solutions Division, Climate Action, Sustainability and Science Department within the Directorate of Earth Observation Programmes.

The Enterprise Section in the Green Solutions Division is responsible for initiating innovative developments linked to novel processing methods, the exploitation of new data sets, the development of new methods integrating EO and non-EO data streams and the stimulation of new EO applications and addressing structural blockages to wider uptake of Earth observation-based applications and analytics.

If selected for this position, you will be responsible for setting up and running ground-breaking EO application development activities exploiting advanced AI methods, novel modelling approaches (such as fusion of EO and agent-based models, fuzzy graphs, neuromorphic processing, etc.) and innovative combinations of EO and non-EO data (such as transponders, IoT sensor networks, robotic platforms, etc.). These will target both improvements over current EO applications and services (notably in domains such as civil security) as well as stimulating expanded uptake of Earth observation-derived information into new demand sectors, in particular economic activity management and human systems process characterisation. Applications development in resilience-oriented domains will also be pursued, in particular with respect to hybrid threat detection, characterisation and mitigation.

You will be cooperating with complementary initiatives both within EOP (eg Digital Twin Earth, F-lab, Scout missions, “Stepping Stones”) and other ESA directorates, in particular the Directorate of Commercialisation, Industry and Competitiveness as well as the Directorate of Technology, Engineering and Quality.

Duties

The main tasks and responsibilities will include:


working with key stakeholders in diverse stakeholder communities to establish and consolidate requirements for geoinformation products and services and to identify priority gaps in current capabilities;
working with EO and non-EO industry stakeholder groups to agree priority innovative AI-based EO processing methods for industrialisation, in particular for enhanced information extraction, multi-dataset fusion, inference and short-term prediction;
elaborating technical specifications for applications and services to address priority requirements and gaps agreed with relevant stakeholders;
identifying, assessing and implementing ideas for innovative combinations of EO and advanced AI capabilities to enhance decision support system functionalities;
setting up and managing industrial procurements for the execution of development activities for novel Earth observation-based applications and services;
interfacing with the EOP Ground Segment and Mission Management Department to ensure consistency with complementary ground segment capability development activities;
interfacing with ESA and EU industrial development and commercialisation initiatives to identify and capitalise on potential to transfer and scale up successful projects;
contributing to ESA communication efforts promoting EO results, mission achievements and EO benefits for society and Earth Action.



Technical competencies

Experience in the use of space-based applications within one or more of the following domains; environmental management and security, climate adaptation, natural resources management, infrastructure management, urban development, societal resilience

Specialist working experience in AI-based processing methods exploiting EO data

Experience in working with public and private sector stakeholders to stimulate expanded uptake of space-based capabilities

Experience in setting up and executing projects involving the exploitation of Earth observation data in combination with complementary data streams

Knowledge of ESA EO programmes and activities, in particular programmes to support European space industry development

Behavioural competencies

Result Orientation

Operational Efficiency

Fostering Cooperation

Relationship Management

Continuous Improvement

Forward Thinking

For more information, please refer to ESA Core Behavioural Competencies guidebook

Education And Professional Experience

A master's in a relevant discipline such as signal processing, earth science, computer science or data science is required for this post together with 4 years of relevant professional experience.

Additional Requirements

Experience in setting up and running ESA procurements and managing ESA contracts addressing space application developments would be an advantage.

Strong problem-solving skills to deal with day-to-day operational challenges.

Strong results orientation with the ability to set priorities and present practical solutions orally and in writing.

Diversity, Equity and Inclusiveness

ESA is an equal opportunity employer, committed to achieving diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. We therefore welcome applications from all qualified candidates irrespective of gender, sexual orientation, ethnicity, religious beliefs, age, disability or other characteristics.

At the Agency we value diversity, and we welcome people with disabilities. Whenever possible, we seek to accommodate individuals with disabilities by providing the necessary support at the workplace. The Human Resources Department can also provide assistance during the recruitment process. If you would like to discuss this further, please contact us via email at contact.human.resources@esa.int.

Important Information and Disclaimer

In principle, recruitment will be within the advertised grade band (A2-A4). However, if the selected candidate has less than four years of relevant professional experience following the completion of the master’s degree, the position may be filled at A1 level.

During the recruitment process, the Agency may request applicants to undergo selection tests. Additionally, successful candidates will need to undergo basic screening before appointment, which will be conducted by an external background screening service, in compliance with the European Space Agency's security procedures.

Note that ESA is in the process of transitioning to a Matrix setup, which could lead to organisational changes affecting this position.

The information published on ESA’s careers website regarding working conditions is correct at the time of publication. It is not intended to be exhaustive and may not address all questions you would have.

Nationality and Languages

Please note that applications are only considered from nationals of one of the following States: Austria, Belgium, Czechia, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Luxembourg, the Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, the United Kingdom and Canada, Cyprus, Latvia, Lithuania and Slovakia.

According to the ESA Convention, staff shall be recruited on the basis of their qualifications, taking into account an adequate distribution of posts among nationals of the Member States.

The working languages of the Agency are English and French. A good knowledge of one of these is required. Knowledge of another Member State language would be an asset.

diploma di scuola superiore

contratto a tempo determinato 6 mesi
tempo pieno dal lunedì al venerdì

affiancamento operativo strutturato con procedure già definite

Title: Data Support Analyst
Description:
We are looking for a Data Support Analyst to join our team, helping us organize and clarify the operational metrics that guide our projects. In this entry-level position, you will work closely with our consultants as they support an Italian energy provider with headquarters in Rome to improve their leadership coordination. You will help gather, clean, and structure the simple data sets that make management patterns visible to leadership teams. This is a permanent, full-time hybrid position that combines remote work with regular collaboration days in our shared workspace.

About the role
This role exists to turn messy spreadsheets, meeting records, and operational logs into clear, readable information that our advisors can use. In our consulting practice, we do not build complex dashboards or write long reports. Instead, we help organizations see where their projects are stalling and clarify who owns which decision. As a Data Support Analyst, you will be the person who maintains the accuracy of our project tracking and helps build the simple, clean tables that help our clients see their own habits. Your work ensures that our advice is always grounded in verified facts.

What you will do
On a regular basis, your responsibilities will include:
Organizing raw updates from project teams into clean spreadsheets.
Reviewing meeting records to verify that action items and decisions match our tracking databases.
Mapping out operational steps and handoffs to help show where projects experience delays.
Preparing simple tables, charts, or summaries for our consulting team before client reviews.
Maintaining internal records so that our project context is always accurate and updated.



What we are looking for
We want to hire someone who enjoys organizing information and values clarity over complexity. We are looking for:
Strong attention to detail, showing a clear focus on accuracy and completeness in your work.
A calm and methodical approach to work, especially when dealing with incomplete details.
Clear, simple writing habits, explaining problems or data patterns without using complicated jargon.
Curiosity about how organizations work and how communication can break down.
A natural preference for order, keeping track of tasks and completing them reliably.



Helpful background
This is an entry-level position, so we do not expect years of experience or advanced technical certifications. It will help if you have:
Basic familiarity with spreadsheets, such as entering formulas in Google Sheets or Microsoft Excel.
Experience organizing information, whether from university projects, internships, or administrative positions.
The ability to learn simple software tools quickly and follow structured instructions.
An interest in business operations, management, or organizational structure.



What the role offers
This is a permanent, full-time hybrid position. Here is what you can expect as part of our team:
Attractive and competitive pay for the Data Support Analyst role.
A balanced hybrid setup, letting you work from home while spending regular days working in person alongside our consulting team.
Deep learning opportunities in management consulting, where you will learn how leadership decisions get made.
A supportive, low-stress environment that values quiet, focused work over constant meetings.
Reliable equipment to support both your home office and your time in our shared workspace.



About Olive Tree
Olive Tree Consulting Group is a focused advisory practice that helps leadership teams solve recurring management issues. We typically work on the space between strategy and daily execution. A team can have very capable people and still find that decisions keep reopening, priorities stall, or responsibility remains unclear. These are rarely personality problems; they are structural habits that have not kept up with the company's growth.
We favor practical changes over large-scale transformation programs. Our work involves looking at how decisions are made, how meetings are run, and how ownership is recorded. We prefer useful written notes and clear ownership over polished presentations and vague alignment. Good advisory work should make things plainer and leave the team with a rhythm they can continue on their own.
The firm is built on the principle that memory is not a management system. We help teams create the smallest possible written record to support their work so that important follow-up does not depend on who was in the room or who has the best memory. The goal is a quieter management pattern that makes the existing work easier to lead.

Title: Data analyst Intern
Description:
Description de l'entreprise

Expecta a pour mission de stimuler la croissance des entreprises en révélant la valeur cachée de leurs données propriétaires. Grâce à des solutions basées sur l'IA, l'entreprise propose une suite de données end-to-end couvrant l'ETL, la visualisation de données, les modèles prédictifs ainsi que des services de conseil stratégique. Les outils innovants d'Expecta simplifient l'analyse de données et permettent aux entreprises de prendre des décisions éclairées et fondées sur les données. À travers ses solutions, Expecta entend redéfinir la manière dont les organisations exploitent leurs données pour réussir.




Description du poste

Ce stage de Data Analyst à temps plein, en présentiel, est basé à Rome ou à Milan, en Italie. Le stagiaire participera à des projets d'analyse de données, à la réalisation d'analyses statistiques, à la construction de modèles de données, ainsi qu'à la préparation de rapports détaillés et de visualisations. Il collaborera avec des équipes pluridisciplinaires afin de fournir des insights actionnables et de contribuer aux processus de décision fondés sur les données. Ce poste offre également l'opportunité de travailler avec des outils basés sur l'IA et de renforcer les capacités d'analyse de données.




Missions et responsabilités

Participer à la planification, à la conception et à la création de tableaux de bord et d'analyses pour de nouveaux clients
Concevoir et mettre en œuvre des entrepôts de données (data warehouses)
Mener des travaux de recherche et d'analyse pour soutenir les opérations




Profil recherché

Étudiant(e) en dernière année de Master en économie, administration des entreprises, data science, informatique ou domaine similaire
Expérience en SQL
Première expérience avec des outils ETL (SSIS, Azure Data Factory, Alteryx, etc.)
Première expérience avec des outils de visualisation (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, etc.)
Une expérience avec le langage DAX est un plus
Excellentes compétences de communication écrite et orale en anglais et en français




Avantages

Rémunération mensuelle : 800 €
Travail depuis notre bureau de Rome ou depuis notre espace de coworking à Milan, avec la possibilité de télétravailler 1 à 2 jours par semaine
Programme de formation de 3 semaines

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst







In Medialogic S.p.A., non costruiamo solo report: abilitiamo il futuro dei nostri clienti attraverso i dati. Siamo un System Integrator che punta tutto sull'innovazione e, soprattutto, sulle Persone. Crediamo che il talento sia il vero motore del cambiamento tecnologico.




Siamo alla ricerca di un Data Analyst appassionato ed esperto per fare la differenza nei nostri progetti di analisi più ambiziosi.




Ti occuperai di:




Progettare, sviluppare e ottimizzare modelli di dati e dashboard performanti, scalabili e dall'architettura solida.
Tradurre requisiti di business e dati complessi in insight di alta qualità, garantendo un'eccellente accuratezza nell'analisi e nella visualizzazione.
Collaborare attivamente con i team tecnologici e di business per garantire l'integrazione fluida di database, feed e API esterne.
Garantire la qualità del dato e dei modelli attraverso l'applicazione di metodologie statistiche avanzate, attività di refactoring del codice e ottimizzazione delle query.




Cosa cerchiamo (Requisiti fondamentali):




Modellazione e BI: Competenza nella modellazione dati e nell'utilizzo di Power BI, PowerQuery e linguaggio DAX.
Data Management & SQL: Ottima conoscenza di Excel (sviluppo macro e integrazioni con database relazionali) e padronanza di SQL (T-SQL).
Python per il Data Science: Uso di Python finalizzato alla data analysis e librerie specializzate (Polars, Statsmodels, Sk-learn, Torch, SQLAlchemy).
Statistica e ML: Solida conoscenza dei principali metodi statistici e di machine learning, inclusa l'analisi delle serie storiche.
Integrazione Dati: Dimestichezza nell'interfacciamento con API per il recupero dati (es. OpenMeteo, Database aperti dell'Unione Europea) e gestione di feed RSS.




💼 Cosa offriamo

Contratto di assunzione a tempo indeterminato (CCNL Metalmeccanico).
Accesso a corsi specializzati e supporto per l'ottenimento di certificazioni.
Buoni pasto giornalieri e piano di Welfare Aziendale.
Un contesto dinamico, orientato all’innovazione e al supporto reciproco.




📩 Sei pronto per la sfida?

Se pensi che questa opportunità sia in linea con il tuo percorso e la tua ambizione, non aspettare: inviaci il tuo CV!

Il presente annuncio è rivolto ad entrambi i sessi, ai sensi delle leggi 903/77 e 125/91, e a persone di tutte le età e tutte le nazionalità, ai sensi dei decreti legislativi 215/03 e 216/03.

Title: Assistente/a di direzione per struttura GDO
Description:
Cerchiamo una persona decisa e organizzata per il ruolo di Assistente/a di direzione all'interno della nostra struttura operativa della Grande Distribuzione Organizzata. Ti occuperai di supportare la direzione nella gestione quotidiana delle attività amministrative e di coordinamento, fungendo da interfaccia privilegiata tra i vertici aziendali, i fornitori e i punti vendita della rete.

Le tue responsabilità saranno concrete e varie: gestirai l'agenda appuntamenti e la pianificazione di riunioni e trasferte per i dirigenti, curerai la corrispondenza commerciale in entrata e uscita, e sarai il primo punto di contatto per clienti, fornitori e visitatori in sede. Ti occuperai inoltre della preparazione di report e comunicazioni interne, del coordinamento delle spedizioni tra sede centrale e punti vendita, e dell'aggiornamento delle anagrafiche fornitori su gestionale aziendale. Avrai modo di lavorare con strumenti digitali consolidati e di misurarti con una realtà in costante movimento.

Requisiti:


diploma di scuola secondaria superiore, preferibilmente indirizzo commerciale o linguistico
buona conoscenza del pacchetto Office, in particolare Excel e Outlook
ottime capacità relazionali e attitudine alla riservatezza
precisione nella gestione delle priorità e autonomia operativa
disponibilità immediata, con inserimento previsto entro 2 settimane


Offerta e benefit:


contratto a tempo determinato 12 mesi finalizzato a indeterminato
retribuzione 1.600-2.000 € lordi mensili (CCNL Commercio)
Buoni pasto
Orario flessibile
Formazione continua
Welfare aziendale
Mensa aziendale


Contratto a tempo pieno full-time 40 ore, dal lunedì al venerdì con possibile articolazione 8:30-12:30 / 13:30-17:30.

Sede di lavoro: Roma

Title: Addetto/a gestione clienti - anche prima esperienza
Description:
Azienda operante nel settore della salute e del benessere, specializzata nella fornitura di sistemi e prodotti per la cura della persona, ricerca una risorsa da inserire con il ruolo di Addetto/a gestione clienti.

La figura rappresenta un punto di riferimento per la clientela nella fase di accoglienza, assistenza e gestione delle richieste, seguendo il cliente con un approccio diretto e continuativo. Il ruolo prevede attività di relazione quotidiana con il pubblico, supporto nella presentazione dei prodotti e dei servizi disponibili e gestione delle esigenze espresse dalla clientela.

Attività principali


Gestione diretta del rapporto con i clienti attraverso accoglienza, ascolto e supporto informativo
Assistenza alla clientela nella comprensione dei prodotti e dei servizi dedicati alla cura della persona
Raccolta delle esigenze dei clienti e orientamento verso le soluzioni più adatte
Gestione delle richieste di approfondimento, aggiornamento o supporto successivo al primo contatto
Monitoraggio della relazione con il cliente e aggiornamento delle informazioni raccolte nei sistemi aziendali
Collaborazione con il team interno per garantire continuità nella gestione delle richieste e delle attività legate alla clientela
Supporto nella gestione degli appuntamenti e delle comunicazioni con i clienti.


Requisiti richiesti


Diploma di scuola secondaria di secondo grado
Ottime capacità comunicative e predisposizione al rapporto diretto con il pubblico
Attitudine all’ascolto e alla gestione delle esigenze dei clienti
Buona dimestichezza con strumenti informatici e applicativi di uso aziendale
Precisione, affidabilità e capacità di organizzare le attività quotidiane
Capacità di lavorare in team e di interagire con interlocutori diversi.


Offerta e benefit


Contratto a tempo determinato di 6 mesi
Orario full time dal lunedì al venerdì
Affiancamento operativo.

Title: Addetto/a inserimento dati ordini distributivi
Description:
Si apre una nuova opportunità nella distribuzione di prodotti per punti vendita, tra flussi ordini, documenti di trasporto e aggiornamenti continui a gestionale. Cerchiamo una persona precisa. Nel nostro team il lavoro conta davvero: ogni dato inserito bene evita ritardi, errori di consegna e passaggi inutili tra ufficio operativo e magazzino.

La risorsa seguirà l’inserimento di anagrafiche clienti, codici articolo e ordini giornalieri, mantenendo allineati i dati tra reparto amministrativo e area logistica. Insieme costruiremo un flusso di lavoro ordinato e veloce, con attività concrete come registrazione di circa 90-120 righe ordine al giorno su Excel e gestionale TeamSystem, controllo di DDT e note di consegna, aggiornamento stati spedizione e verifica delle discrepanze quantità/prezzo prima del rilascio definitivo delle pratiche.

Requisiti:


diploma di scuola superiore
anche senza esperienza pregressa
buona dimestichezza con pc, Excel e posta elettronica
attenzione ai dettagli e velocità di digitazione
disponibilità entro 2 settimane


Offerta e benefit:


contratto a tempo determinato di 12 mesi
Retribuzione lorda mensile indicativa: 1450-1550 €
Buoni pasto
Formazione continua
Welfare aziendale


L’inserimento è pensato per chi vuole entrare in un contesto operativo, capire come si muove davvero la distribuzione e diventare un riferimento affidabile nella gestione dei dati quotidiani. Offriamo affiancamento iniziale e obiettivi chiari, senza confusione.

Sede di lavoro: Roma
Show more

Title: ADDETTO/A ALLA VENDITA
Description:
Per il nostro store di Roma via del Corso, cerchiamo un/una Addetto/a vendita da inserire con contratto part-time 24 ore.

Ti occuperai di assistenza clienti, supporto alla vendita, gestione della cassa, riassortimento e cura del punto vendita.

La figura ideale ha esperienza nel retail o in negozio, ama il contatto con il pubblico ed è disponibile a lavorare su turni, weekend e festivi.

Ti riconosci in questo ruolo?


Hai esperienza nella vendita, preferibilmente nel retail, ad esempio come commesso/a, sales assistant o addetto/a vendita
Hai una forte attitudine al cliente, con un approccio pratico, orientato all’ascolto e al consiglio
Hai Spirito di iniziativa, organizzazione e orientamento agli obiettivi;
Sei disponibile a lavorare su turni, nei weekend e nei giorni festivi



Cosa farai


Accoglierai i clienti e li supporterai nella scelta dei prodotti, offrendo un’assistenza semplice, diretta e orientata alla vendita
Ti occuperai della cura del punto vendita, del riordino, del riassortimento e del supporto alle attività di cassa
Gestirai l’allestimento del negozio, valorizzando i prodotti e contribuendo a creare un’esperienza d’acquisto positiva e coinvolgente



Cosa offriamo


Contratto di lavoro part-time 24 ore, a tempo determinato
Inquadramento secondo CCNL Commercio
Retribuzione annua lorda indicativa: €14.300 – €15.400
Sconto dipendenti del 30% sui prodotti Carpisa
Sistema incentivante legato alle performance



Chi siamo

CARPISA è un brand italiano che da oltre vent’anni è protagonista nel mondo delle valigie, borse e accessori, con una rete di store in Italia e all’estero.

Nei nostri punti vendita accogliamo ogni giorno i clienti e li accompagniamo nella scelta del prodotto più adatto, in un ambiente inclusivo, dinamico e orientato alla collaborazione.

Candidati ora

Entra in Carpisa e vivi il retail ogni giorno da protagonista, in un contesto fatto di energia, relazione con il cliente e lavoro di squadra.

Title: BUSINESS ANALYST AND CONTROLLING
Description:
Position

As Business Analyst & Controlling, you will contribute to the achievement of business objectives by supporting planning processes, monitoring financial and operational performance, and providing accurate analysis to guide decision-making.

The role ensures reliable reporting, clear visibility on key business drivers and timely identification of variances, risks and opportunities, supporting the organization in maintaining financial discipline, improving performance and achieving budget targets.

Job responsibilities

Main Accountabilities


Prepare the sales budget and revisions
Monitor performance of sales, margins and highlighting main variances for different areas (by channel, by product, by region)
Monthly P&L reporting highlighting main variations vs Budget and Previous Year in order to ensure monitoring of budget targets (both at consolidated and market)
3-Year Plan projection and preparation for the various channels
Preparation of ad hoc reports, presentations as requested by Commercial Regional Managers
Review the monthly Marketing Budget for monitoring A&P spending.
Act as a finance business partner, providing financial analysis, business insights and constructive recommendations to support decision-making and performance management.
Analyze Advertising & Promotion spending by project, supporting budget monitoring, approval processes, actual tracking and return-on-investment assessment.
Contribute to month-end closing activities by ensuring the accuracy, consistency and timely availability of financial data in coordination with the relevant finance processes.
Support the implementation and continuous improvement of standard reporting methodologies, dashboards and business performance analysis tools.
Manage and maintain controlling dimensions ensuring reliable data structure and effective analysis.
Provide adequate financial documentation and analysis to support statutory closures, internal reviews and external audit requirements.
Contribute to the development of a more analytical and performance-oriented approach across reporting and controlling activities, identifying opportunities to improve visibility, efficiency and decision support.


Profile

Qualifications


Degree in Finance, Economics, Business Administration, Accounting or related field.
At least 2–4 years of relevant experience in controlling, business analysis, commercial finance or financial planning and analysis, preferably within a structured international environment.
Solid knowledge of budgeting, forecasting, reporting techniques and P&L analysis.
Good understanding of accounting principles, cost controlling, margin analysis and financial performance indicators.
Strong proficiency in Office applications, particularly Excel and PowerPoint; knowledge of Power BI or other reporting and data visualization tools is a plus.
Fluent English, both written and spoken; knowledge of additional languages is considered a plus.


Soft Skills


Strong analytical mindset, with the ability to interpret financial data and translate numbers into clear business insights.
Business awareness and commercial agility, with the ability to understand business priorities, operational dynamics and value creation levers.
Accuracy, attention to detail and reliability in managing financial information, reporting deadlines and sensitive data.
Proactive and solution-oriented approach, with the ability to identify risks, opportunities and improvement actions.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to explain financial topics clearly and challenge constructively.
Collaborative attitude and entrepreneurial spirit, with the ability to work effectively with cross-functional teams in a dynamic business environment.

Title: NDC Internship 2027
Description:
Primary Location

Italy-Rome

Schedule

Full-time

Application Deadline

23-Jun-2026, 12:59:00 AM

Grade

INTERN

Post context

If you would like to gain experience working in a political and military alliance that promotes freedom and security, in an academic setting, then the NATO Defense College Internship Programme might be for you!

The NATO Defense College (NDC) provides senior-level education, on transatlantic security issues, enabled by research on matters relevant to the Alliance, and supported by engagement with several stakeholders.

Through its internship programme, the NDC offers candidates from diverse backgrounds the opportunity to develop practical skills, work alongside international professionals and subject-matter experts, share ideas, and contribute to the goals of the Alliance.

Main objectives of the NDC Internship Programme


To give interns the opportunity to learn from the NATO community and get a better understanding and a more balanced view of the Organization.
To strenghten NATO's access to theoretical and technical knowledge that the intern can apply through practical work assignments.
To contribute to a more diverse workforce.
To increase knowledge and understanding of NATO across Allied countries.



Eligibility criteria

To apply for a NATO internship, you must fulfil the following criteria on the closing date for applications:


Age: Applicants must be at least 21 years old.
Nationality: Applicants must be a citizen of a NATO member country
Educational status: Applicants must have completed at least two years of university study and be enrolled in a third year, or must have obtained their highest degree no more than a year prior to their application.
Languages: proficiency in one of the two official NATO languages: English or French. A working knowledge of the second is considered an asset. Knowledge of other languages may also be considered an asset for certain positions.



Selection process:


Applications are screened and evaluated during the month following the application deadline.
Longlisted candidates will receive an invitation to complete an online assessment on a platform called HireVue.
Shortlisted candidates will be contacted around July/August and invited to an online interview.
Successful candidates will be subject to a security clearance process and will be invited to begin their internship in February or August of the following year.



Additional information

The security clearance procedure varies by country and takes on average six months to complete.

Before the start of an internship, selected candidates must prove that you have obtained health insurance for the duration of your internship.

An internship does not guarantee future employment. In order to gain employment by NATO at any point following your internship, you must apply through the standard recruitment process.

What we offer


Internship stipend: If you are selected for the NDC-funded internship programme, you will receive a monthly stipend (EUR 1,146.22 in 2026). This amount is adjusted by year in line with inflation and NATO salary adjustments.
Travel: Once the Internship Office has confirmed your start date, you will be entitled to reimbursement of the most rapid and economical means of transport
Leave: You will be entitled to 2.5 days of paid leave per month.
In-house training: During the internship, you will take part in an induction programme and briefings to provide you with an overview of NDC's activities and structure.



NATO is committed to diversity and inclusion and strives to provide equal access to career opportunities regardless of gender, age, nationality, ethnic origin, religious belief, cultural background, sexual orientation or disability. NATO welcomes applicants from all member countries and strongly encourages women to apply.

As an employer, NATO values integrity, transparency and accountability in accordance with international norms and practices established for the defence and related security sector.

NOTE:

NATO will not accept any phase of the recruitment and selection process, in whole or in part, by means of generative artificial-intelligence (AI) tools, including and without limitation to chatbots, such as Chat Generative Pre-Trained Transformer (Chat GPT), or other language generating tools. NATO reserves the right to screen applications to identify the use of such tools. All applications prepared, in whole or in part, by means of such generative or creative AI applications may be rejected without further consideration at NATO’s sole discretion, and NATO reserves the right to take further steps in such cases as appropriate.
